revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

revision.comment: Programmatically generating so that if this gets imported to wikipedia as a draft, the bot will attribute it to Immanuelle and send them g6 warnings

revision.comment: Auto-generated page using script written by Immanuelle, either as a placeholder redirect or to make bots recognize the page as made by Immanuelle
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Ruijū_Sandai_Kyaku]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

'''類聚三代格'''（るいじゅうさんだいきゃく）は、[[平安時代]]（恐らく[[11世紀]]）に書かれた法令集。著者は不明。[[弘仁格]]・[[貞観格]]・[[延喜格]]のいわゆる[[格]]を事例ごとに類聚（ジャンル分け）してまとめたもの。全30巻と伝えられているが、現存するものは全20巻本と全12巻本の2種類であり、両方合わせて重複部分を除いても内容が一部欠ける（完本に至らないが、散逸推定分を加えても30巻には至らないと考えられる）状態であるため、原本がどういう形であったかは不詳である。
平安時代に[[令外官]]が増加すると[[律令]]で定められた職制が有名無実と化し、従来の官庁ごとに配列されている[[格]]では実際の政務には必要な情報の入手が困難となった。そこで「神社事」・「国分寺事」・「庸調事」・「禁制事」・「断罪贖銅事」などのように事例ごとに類聚して90篇（現存82篇）にまとめられた。
3代の格は弘仁格の一部を除いては現存しておらず、格の原文がほぼそのまま引き写されたと考えられる「類聚三代格」は、当時の格について知るために貴重な資料である。

revision.comment: 加筆
revision.text:
{{文学}} '''類聚三代格'''（るいじゅうさんだいきゃく）は、[[平安時代]]（恐らく[[11世紀]]）に書かれた法令集。著者は不明。 == 概説 == [[弘仁格]]・[[貞観格]]・[[延喜格]]のいわゆる[[格式|格]]を事例ごとに類聚（ジャンル分け）してまとめたもの。全30巻と伝えられているが、現存するものは全20巻本と全12巻本の2種類であり、両方合わせて重複部分を除いても内容が一部欠ける（完本に至らないが、散逸推定分を加えても30巻には至らないと考えられる）状態であるため、原本がどういう形であったかは不詳である。
平安時代に[[令外官]]が増加すると[[律令]]で定められた職制が有名無実と化し、従来の官庁ごとに配列されている格では実際の政務には必要な情報の入手が困難となった。そこで「神社事」・「国分寺事」・「庸調事」・「禁制事」・「断罪贖銅事」などのように事例ごとに類聚して90篇（現存82篇）にまとめられた。
3代の格は弘仁格の目録を除いては現存しておらず、格の原文がほぼそのまま引き写されたと考えられる「類聚三代格」は、当時の古代法制の実態を知るために貴重な史料である。 ==12巻本系統による構成== *第一巻----神社に関する祭幣・託宣・神主・祓い *第二巻----造仏・法会・僧職任命 *第三巻----国分寺・僧の位階・僧の禁忌・地方の講師 *第四巻----中央官庁の廃置・定員 *第五巻----諸国官僚の廃置・位階 *第六巻----官僚の給与体系 *第七巻----公卿の意見、地方役人の取り締まり *第八巻----農業奨励・租税・金銭貸借 *第九巻----班田・公営田・墾田・山川藪沢 *第十巻----天皇・宮中の食膳・恩赦・文書・印 *第十一巻--軍隊・徴兵・相撲・駅・材木 *第十二巻--各種の禁令・刑罰
[[Category:平安時代|るいしゆうさんたいきやく]] {{Japanese-history-stub|るいしゆうさんたいきやく}}

{{文学}} '''類聚三代格'''（るいじゅさんだいきゃく）は、[[平安時代]]（恐らく[[11世紀]]）に書かれた法令集。著者は不明。 == 概説 == [[弘仁格]]・[[貞観格]]・[[延喜格]]のいわゆる[[格式|格]]を事例ごとに類聚（ジャンル分け）してまとめたもの。全30巻と伝えられているが、現存するものは全20巻本と全12巻本の2種類であり、両方合わせて重複部分を除いても内容が一部欠ける（完本に至らないが、散逸推定分を加えても30巻には至らないと考えられる）状態であるため、原本がどういう形であったかは不詳である。
平安時代に[[令外官]]が増加すると[[律令]]で定められた職制が有名無実と化し、従来の官庁ごとに配列されている格では実際の政務には必要な情報の入手が困難となった。そこで「神社事」・「国分寺事」・「庸調事」・「禁制事」・「断罪贖銅事」などのように事例ごとに類聚して90篇（現存82篇）にまとめられた。
3代の格は弘仁格の目録を除いては現存しておらず、格の原文がほぼそのまま引き写されたと考えられる「類聚三代格」は、当時の古代法制の実態を知るために貴重な史料である。 ==12巻本系統による構成== *第一巻----神社に関する祭幣・託宣・神主・祓い *第二巻----造仏・法会・僧職任命 *第三巻----国分寺・僧の位階・僧の禁忌・地方の講師 *第四巻----中央官司の廃置・定員 *第五巻----諸国官僚の廃置・位階 *第六巻----官僚の給与体系 *第七巻----公卿の意見、地方役人の取り締まり *第八巻----農業奨励・租税・金銭貸借 *第九巻----班田・公営田・墾田・山川藪沢 *第十巻----天皇・宮中の食膳・恩赦・文書・印 *第十一巻--軍隊・徴兵・相撲・駅・材木 *第十二巻--各種の禁令・刑罰
{{デフォルトソート:るいしゆうさんたいきやく}} [[Category:平安時代の書籍]] [[Category:日本の律令制]] [[Category:法令集]]

'''類聚三代格'''（るいじゅうさんだいきゃく）は、[[平安時代]]（恐らく[[11世紀]]）に編纂された法令集。著者は不明。
== 概説 == [[弘仁格]]・[[貞観格]]・[[延喜格]]のいわゆる[[格式|格]]を事例ごとに類聚（ジャンル分け）してまとめたもの。全30巻と伝えられているが、現存するものは全20巻本と全12巻本の2種類であり、両方合わせて重複部分を除いても内容が一部欠ける（完本に至らないが、散逸推定分を加えても30巻には至らないと考えられる）状態であるため、原本がどういう形であったかは不詳である。
平安時代に[[令外官]]が増加すると[[律令]]で定められた職制が有名無実と化し、従来の官庁ごとに配列されている格では実際の政務に必要な情報の入手が困難となった。そこで「神社事」・「国分寺事」・「庸調事」・「禁制事」・「断罪贖銅事」などのように事例ごとに類聚して90篇（現存82篇）にまとめられた。
三代の格は弘仁格の目録を除いては現存しておらず、格の原文がほぼそのまま引き写されたと考えられる「類聚三代格」は、当時の古代法制の実態を知るために貴重な史料である。
==12巻本系統による構成== *第一巻 - 神社に関する祭幣・託宣・神主・祓い *第二巻 - 造仏・法会・僧職任命 *第三巻 - 国分寺・僧の位階・僧の禁忌・地方の講師 *第四巻 - 中央官司の廃置・定員 *第五巻 - 諸国官僚の廃置・位階 *第六巻 - 官僚の給与体系 *第七巻 - 公卿の意見、地方役人の取り締まり *第八巻 - 農業奨励・租税・金銭貸借 *第九巻 - 班田・公営田・墾田・山川藪沢 *第十巻 - 天皇・宮中の食膳・恩赦・文書・印 *第十一巻 - 軍隊・徴兵・相撲・駅・材木 *第十二巻 - 各種の禁令・刑罰

revision.comment: 無出典のためコトバンクより書き下ろし
revision.text:
'''類聚三代格'''（るいじゅうさんだいきゃく）は、[[平安時代]]に編纂された法令集。編者は不明<ref name="Nipponica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|author=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref>。
== 概説 == 三代格（[[弘仁格]]、[[貞観格]]、[[延喜格]]）を内容別に「神社事」・「国分寺事」・「庸調事」などと類聚（ジャンル分け）してまとめたもので<ref name="Nipponica" /><ref name="Sekaidaihyakka">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|author=世界大百科事典 第2版|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref>、先行の格に収録されているものは再録していない<ref name="Britannica" />。成立時期は『[[日本大百科全書]]』によれば[[長保]]4年（[[1002年]]）と[[寛治]]3年（[[1089年]]）の間とされる<ref name="Nipponica" />。三代格では官司別にまとめており、法典として利用するとき不便である上、[[平安時代]]中期に[[有職故実]]化の傾向があったため、典例整備事業の一環として成立したとされる<ref name="Nipponica" />。
完本が現存せず、不完全な写本が伝わっているのみである<ref name="Mypedia">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|author=百科事典マイペディア|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref>。元となる弘仁格、貞観格、延喜格はそれぞれ10巻、12巻、12巻構成であり<ref name="Britannica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|author=ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref>、『類聚三代格』は15巻<ref name="Britannica" />もしくは17巻<ref name="Obunsha">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|author=旺文社日本史事典 三訂版|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref>が現存するとされる。『類聚三代格』の写本は12巻本と20巻本が現存し、『[[本朝書籍目録]]』では全30巻としているものの<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" />、完本の巻数は20巻<ref name="Obunsha" />、30巻<ref name="Mypedia" />など諸説ありで、『ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典』では全30巻の根拠が薄いとしている<ref name="Britannica" />。また、現存する12巻本と20巻本の写本を合わせても欠落がある<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" />。
三代の格は弘仁格の目録を除いては現存しておらず、格の原文が含まれる『類聚三代格』は、当時の古代法制の実態を知るために貴重な史料である<ref name="Britannica" />。
==12巻本系統による構成== *第一巻 - 神社に関する祭幣・託宣・神主・祓い *第二巻 - 造仏・法会・僧職任命 *第三巻 - 国分寺・僧の位階・僧の禁忌・地方の講師 *第四巻 - 中央官司の廃置・定員 *第五巻 - 諸国官僚の廃置・位階 *第六巻 - 官僚の給与体系 *第七巻 - 公卿の意見、地方役人の取り締まり *第八巻 - 農業奨励・租税・金銭貸借 *第九巻 - 班田・公営田・墾田・山川藪沢 *第十巻 - 天皇・宮中の食膳・恩赦・文書・印 *第十一巻 - 軍隊・徴兵・相撲・駅・材木 *第十二巻 - 各種の禁令・刑罰
== 出典 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[類聚国史]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://codh.rois.ac.jp/pmjt/book/200018252/ 類聚三代格 - 日本古典籍データセット]
{{デフォルトソート:るいしゆうさんたいきやく}} [[Category:平安時代の書籍]] [[Category:日本の格式]] [[Category:法令集]]

'''類聚三代格'''（るいじゅうさんだいきゃく）は、[[平安時代]]に編纂された法令集。編者は不明<ref name="Nipponica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref>。
== 概説 == 三代格（[[弘仁格]]、[[貞観格]]、[[延喜格]]）を内容別に「神社事」・「国分寺事」・「庸調事」などと類聚（ジャンル分け）してまとめたもので<ref name="Nipponica" /><ref name="Sekaidaihyakka">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第2版|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref>、先行の格に収録されているものは再録していない<ref name="Britannica" />。成立時期は『[[日本大百科全書]]』によれば[[長保]]4年（[[1002年]]）と[[寛治]]3年（[[1089年]]）の間とされる<ref name="Nipponica" />。三代格では官司別にまとめており、法典として利用するとき不便である上、[[平安時代]]中期に[[有職故実]]化の傾向があったため、典例整備事業の一環として成立したとされる<ref name="Nipponica" />。
完本が現存せず、不完全な写本が伝わっているのみである<ref name="Mypedia">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=百科事典マイペディア|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref>。元となる弘仁格、貞観格、延喜格はそれぞれ10巻、12巻、12巻構成であり<ref name="Britannica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref>、『類聚三代格』は15巻<ref name="Britannica" />もしくは17巻<ref name="Obunsha">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=旺文社日本史事典 三訂版|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref>が現存するとされる。『類聚三代格』の写本は12巻本と20巻本が現存し、『[[本朝書籍目録]]』では全30巻としているものの<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" />、完本の巻数は20巻<ref name="Obunsha" />、30巻<ref name="Mypedia" />など諸説ありで、『ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典』では全30巻の根拠が薄いとしている<ref name="Britannica" />。また、現存する12巻本と20巻本の写本を合わせても欠落がある<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" />。
三代の格は弘仁格の目録を除いては現存しておらず、格の原文が含まれる『類聚三代格』は、当時の古代法制の実態を知るために貴重な史料である<ref name="Britannica" />。
==12巻本系統の構成== *第一巻 - 神社に関する祭幣・託宣・神主・祓い *第二巻 - 造仏・法会・僧職任命 *第三巻 - 国分寺・僧の位階・僧の禁忌・地方の講師 *第四巻 - 中央官司の廃置・定員 *第五巻 - 諸国官僚の廃置・位階 *第六巻 - 官僚の給与体系 *第七巻 - 公卿の意見、地方役人の取り締まり *第八巻 - 農業奨励・租税・金銭貸借 *第九巻 - 班田・公営田・墾田・山川藪沢 *第十巻 - 天皇・宮中の食膳・恩赦・文書・印 *第十一巻 - 軍隊・徴兵・相撲・駅・材木 *第十二巻 - 各種の禁令・刑罰
== 20巻本系統の構成 == *第一巻 - 神社事、神封物幷租地子事、祭幷幣事、神叙位幷託宣亊、齋王亊、科祓事 *第二巻 佛事上- 造佛々名亊、經論幷法會請僧亊、修法灌頂事、年分度者事 *第三巻 佛事下 - 國分寺亊、定額寺亊、僧綱員位階幷僧位階亊、諸国講讀師亊、僧尼禁忌亊 *第四巻 - *第五巻 - *第六巻 - *第七巻 - *第八巻 - *第九巻 - *第十巻 - *第十一巻 - *第十二巻 - *第十三巻 *第十四巻 *第十五巻 *第十六巻 *第十七巻 *第十八巻 *第十九巻 *第二十巻
== 出典 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=国史大系 第12巻 |year=1900 |publisher=経済雑誌社 |chapter=類聚三代格 |volume= |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991102/1/178 |doi=10.11501/991102}} * {{Cite book|和書 |title=国史大系 第25巻 新訂増補 |year=1936 |publisher=国史大系刊行会 |series= |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3431640/1/4 |doi=10.11501/3431640 |chapter=類聚三代格}}
== 関連項目 == *[[類聚国史]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://codh.rois.ac.jp/pmjt/book/200018252/ 類聚三代格 - 日本古典籍データセット]
{{デフォルトソート:るいしゆうさんたいきやく}} [[Category:平安時代の書籍]] [[Category:日本の格式]] [[Category:法令集]]

'''類聚三代格'''（るいじゅうさんだいきゃく）は、[[平安時代]]に編纂された法令集。編者は不明<ref name="Nipponica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref>。
== 概説 == 三代格（[[弘仁格]]、[[貞観格]]、[[延喜格]]）を内容別に「神社事」・「国分寺事」・「庸調事」などと類聚（ジャンル分け）してまとめたもので<ref name="Nipponica" /><ref name="Sekaidaihyakka">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第2版|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref>、先行の格に収録されているものは再録していない<ref name="Britannica" />。成立時期は『[[日本大百科全書]]』によれば[[長保]]4年（[[1002年]]）と[[寛治]]3年（[[1089年]]）の間とされる<ref name="Nipponica" />。三代格では官司別にまとめており、法典として利用するとき不便である上、[[平安時代]]中期に[[有職故実]]化の傾向があったため、典例整備事業の一環として成立したとされる<ref name="Nipponica" />。
完本が現存せず、不完全な写本が伝わっているのみである<ref name="Mypedia">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=百科事典マイペディア|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref>。元となる弘仁格、貞観格、延喜格はそれぞれ10巻、12巻、12巻構成であり<ref name="Britannica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref>、『類聚三代格』は15巻<ref name="Britannica" />もしくは17巻<ref name="Obunsha">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=旺文社日本史事典 三訂版|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref>が現存するとされる。『類聚三代格』の写本は12巻本と20巻本が現存し、『[[本朝書籍目録]]』では全30巻としているものの<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" />、完本の巻数は20巻<ref name="Obunsha" />、30巻<ref name="Mypedia" />など諸説ありで、『ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典』では全30巻の根拠が薄いとしている<ref name="Britannica" />。また、現存する12巻本と20巻本の写本を合わせても欠落がある<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" />。
三代の格は弘仁格の目録を除いては現存しておらず、格の原文が含まれる『類聚三代格』は、当時の古代法制の実態を知るために貴重な史料である<ref name="Britannica" />。
==12巻本系統の構成== *第一巻 - 神社に関する祭幣・託宣・神主・祓い *第二巻 - 造仏・法会・僧職任命 *第三巻 - 国分寺・僧の位階・僧の禁忌・地方の講師 *第四巻 - 中央官司の廃置・定員 *第五巻 - 諸国官僚の廃置・位階 *第六巻 - 官僚の給与体系 *第七巻 - 公卿の意見、地方役人の取り締まり *第八巻 - 農業奨励・租税・金銭貸借 *第九巻 - 班田・公営田・墾田・山川藪沢 *第十巻 - 天皇・宮中の食膳・恩赦・文書・印 *第十一巻 - 軍隊・徴兵・相撲・駅・材木 *第十二巻 - 各種の禁令・刑罰
== 20巻本系統の構成 == *第一巻 - 神社事、[[神封|神封物]]幷租地子事、祭幷幣事、神叙位幷託宣亊、[[斎王|齋王]]亊、[[科祓]]事 *第二巻 佛事上- 造佛々名亊、經論幷法會請僧亊、修法[[灌頂]]事、年分度者事 *第三巻 佛事下 - [[国分寺|國分寺]]亊、[[定額寺]]亊、[[僧綱|僧綱員]]位階幷僧位階亊、諸国[[講讀師]]亊、僧尼禁忌亊 *第四巻 - 癈置諸司亊、加減諸司官員幷癈置事[[雑任|雜任]] *第五巻 - 分置諸國事、加減諸國官員幷癈置事雜任附出、定官員幷官位亊、定内外五位等級亊、交替幷[[解由]]亊 *第六巻 - [[位祿]][[季禄|季祿]][[時服]][[馬料|馬䉼]]亊、[[要劇料|要劇月䉼]]亊、[[公廨]]亊、[[事力]]幷交替丁亊、[[公粮]]亊、[[賻物]]亊 *第七巻 - 公卿意見亊、[[牧宰]]亊、[[郡司]]亊 *第八巻 - 農桑亊、調庸亊、封戸亊、不動々用亊 *第九巻 - *第十巻 - 釈尊亊、國忌亊、供御亊 *第十一巻 - *第十二巻 - *第十三巻 *第十四巻 *第十五巻 *第十六巻 *第十七巻 *第十八巻 *第十九巻 *第二十巻

== 20巻本系統の構成 == *第一巻 - 神社事、[[神封|神封物]]幷租地子事、祭幷幣事、神叙位幷託宣亊、[[斎王|齋王]]亊、[[科祓]]事 *第二巻 佛事上- 造佛々名亊、經論幷法會請僧亊、修法[[灌頂]]事、年分度者事 *第三巻 佛事下 - [[国分寺|國分寺]]亊、[[定額寺]]亊、[[僧綱|僧綱員]]位階幷僧位階亊、諸国[[講讀師]]亊、僧尼禁忌亊 *第四巻 - 癈置諸司亊、加減諸司官員幷癈置事[[雑任|雜任]] *第五巻 - 分置諸國事、加減諸國官員幷癈置事雜任附出、定官員幷官位亊、定内外五位等級亊、交替幷[[解由]]亊 *第六巻 - [[位祿]][[季禄|季祿]][[時服]][[馬料|馬䉼]]亊、[[要劇料|要劇月䉼]]亊、[[公廨]]亊、[[事力]]幷交替丁亊、[[公粮]]亊、[[賻物]]亊 *第七巻 - 公卿意見亊、[[牧宰]]亊、[[郡司]]亊 *第八巻 - 農桑亊、調庸亊、封戸亊、不動々用亊 *第九巻 - 欠巻 *第十巻 - 釈尊亊、國忌亊、供御亊 *第十一巻 - 欠巻 *第十二巻 - 諸使幷[[公文]]亊、[[隠首]][[括出]][[浪人]]亊、[[正倉]][[官倉]]亊 *第十三巻 - 欠巻 *第十四巻 - [[出挙|出舉]]亊、借貸亊、[[雑米|雜米]]亊、[[義倉]]亊、[[塡納]]亊、[[鋳銭司|'''鑄'''銭]]亊 *第十五巻 - [[挍班田]]亊、[[損田]]幷租[[地子]]亊、[[易田]]幷[[公営田|公營田]]亊、[[墾田]]幷[[佃]]亊、[[寺田]]亊、[[諸司田]]亊、[[軄田]][[位田]][[公廨田]]亊 *第十六巻 - [[閑癈地]]亊、[[道槁]]亊、[[船瀬]]幷浮槁[[布施屋]]亊、山野薮澤江河池沼亊、堤堰溝渠亊 *第十七巻 - *第十八巻 - *第十九巻 - *第二十巻 -

完本が現存せず、不完全な写本が伝わっているのみである<ref name="Mypedia">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=百科事典マイペディア|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref>。元となる弘仁格、貞観格、延喜格はそれぞれ10巻、12巻、12巻構成であり<ref name="Britannica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref>、『類聚三代格』は15巻<ref name="Britannica" />もしくは17巻<ref name="Obunsha">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=旺文社日本史事典 三訂版|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref>が現存するとされる。『類聚三代格』の写本は12巻本と20巻本が現存し、『[[本朝書籍目録]]』では全30巻としているものの<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" />、完本の巻数は20巻<ref name="Obunsha" />、30巻<ref name="Mypedia" />など諸説ありで、『ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典』では全30巻の根拠が薄いとしている<ref name="Britannica" />。また、現存する12巻本と20巻本の写本を合わせても欠落がある<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" />。
三代の格は弘仁格の目録を除いては現存しておらず、格の原文が含まれる『類聚三代格』は、当時の古代法制の実態を知るために貴重な史料である<ref name="Britannica" />。
==12巻本系統の構成== *第一巻 - 神社に関する祭幣・託宣・神主・祓い *第二巻 - 造仏・法会・僧職任命 *第三巻 - 国分寺・僧の位階・僧の禁忌・地方の講師 *第四巻 - 中央官司の廃置・定員 *第五巻 - 諸国官僚の廃置・位階 *第六巻 - 官僚の給与体系 *第七巻 - 公卿の意見、地方役人の取り締まり *第八巻 - 農業奨励・租税・金銭貸借 *第九巻 - 班田・公営田・墾田・山川藪沢 *第十巻 - 天皇・宮中の食膳・恩赦・文書・印 *第十一巻 - 軍隊・徴兵・相撲・駅・材木 *第十二巻 - 各種の禁令・刑罰
== 20巻本系統の構成 == *第一巻 - 神社事、[[神封|神封物]]幷租地子事、祭幷幣事、神叙位幷託宣亊、[[斎王|齋王]]亊、[[科祓]]事 *第二巻 佛事上- 造佛々名亊、經論幷法會請僧亊、修法[[灌頂]]事、年分度者事 *第三巻 佛事下 - [[国分寺|國分寺]]亊、[[定額寺]]亊、[[僧綱|僧綱員]]位階幷僧位階亊、諸国[[講讀師]]亊、僧尼禁忌亊 *第四巻 - 癈置諸司亊、加減諸司官員幷癈置事[[雑任|雜任]] *第五巻 - 分置諸國事、加減諸國官員幷癈置事雜任附出、定官員幷官位亊、定内外五位等級亊、交替幷[[解由]]亊 *第六巻 - [[位祿]][[季禄|季祿]][[時服]][[馬料|馬䉼]]亊、[[要劇料|要劇月䉼]]亊、[[公廨]]亊、[[事力]]幷交替丁亊、[[公粮]]亊、[[賻物]]亊 *第七巻 - 公卿意見亊、[[牧宰]]亊、[[郡司]]亊 *第八巻 - 農桑亊、調庸亊、封戸亊、不動々用亊 *第九巻 - *第十巻 - 釋尊亊、國忌亊、供御亊 *第十一巻 - *第十二巻 - 諸使幷[[公文]]亊、[[隠首]][[括出]][[浪人]]亊、[[正倉]][[官倉]]亊 *第十三巻 - *第十四巻 - [[出挙|出舉]]亊、借貸亊、[[雑米|雜米]]亊、[[義倉]]亊、[[塡納]]亊、[[鋳銭司|'''鑄'''銭]]亊 *第十五巻 - [[挍班田]]亊、[[損田]]幷租[[地子]]亊、[[易田]]幷[[公営田|公營田]]亊、[[墾田]]幷[[佃]]亊、[[寺田]]亊、[[諸司田]]亊、[[軄田]][[位田]][[公廨田]]亊 *第十六巻 - [[閑癈地]]亊、[[道槁]]亊、[[船瀬]]幷浮槁[[布施屋]]亊、山野薮澤江河池沼亊、堤堰溝渠亊 *第十七巻 - [[國諱]][[追号|追號]]幷[[改姓|改姓名]]亊、[[蠲免]]<ref>{{Cite kotobank |word=蠲免 |encyclopedia=中日辞典 第3版 |access-date=2024-03-27}}</ref>亊、[[赦除]]<ref>{{Cite kotobank |word=赦除 |encyclopedia=普及版 字通 |access-date=2024-03-27}}</ref>亊、[[募賞]]亊、文書幷印亊 *第十八巻 - [[軍毅]]兵士[[鎮兵|鎭兵]]亊、[[統領]][[選士]][[衛卒]][[衛士]][[仕丁]]亊、[[健児|健兒]]亊、 *第十九巻 - *第二十巻 -
== 出典 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=国史大系 第12巻 |year=1900 |publisher=経済雑誌社 |chapter=類聚三代格 |volume= |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991102/1/1 |doi=10.11501/991102}} * {{Cite book|和書 |title=国史大系 第25巻 新訂増補 |year=1936 |publisher=国史大系刊行会 |series= |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3431640/1/4 |doi=10.11501/3431640 |chapter=類聚三代格}}
== 関連項目 == *[[類聚国史]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://codh.rois.ac.jp/pmjt/book/200018252/ 類聚三代格 - 日本古典籍データセット]

revision.text:
'''類聚三代格'''（るいじゅうさんだいきゃく）は、[[平安時代]]に編纂された法令集。編者は不明<ref name="Nipponica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref>。
== 概説 == 三代格（[[弘仁格]]、[[貞観格]]、[[延喜格]]）を内容別に「神社事」・「国分寺事」・「庸調事」などと類聚（ジャンル分け）してまとめたもので<ref name="Nipponica" /><ref name="Sekaidaihyakka">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第2版|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref>、先行の格に収録されているものは再録していない<ref name="Britannica" />。成立時期は『[[日本大百科全書]]』によれば[[長保]]4年（[[1002年]]）と[[寛治]]3年（[[1089年]]）の間とされる<ref name="Nipponica" />。三代格では官司別にまとめており、法典として利用するとき不便である上、[[平安時代]]中期に[[有職故実]]化の傾向があったため、典例整備事業の一環として成立したとされる<ref name="Nipponica" />。
完本が現存せず、不完全な写本が伝わっているのみである<ref name="Mypedia">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=百科事典マイペディア|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref>。元となる弘仁格、貞観格、延喜格はそれぞれ10巻、12巻、12巻構成であり<ref name="Britannica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref>、『類聚三代格』は15巻<ref name="Britannica" />もしくは17巻<ref name="Obunsha">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=旺文社日本史事典 三訂版|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref>が現存するとされる。『類聚三代格』の写本は12巻本と20巻本が現存し、『[[本朝書籍目録]]』では全30巻としているものの<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" />、完本の巻数は20巻<ref name="Obunsha" />、30巻<ref name="Mypedia" />など諸説ありで、『ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典』では全30巻の根拠が薄いとしている<ref name="Britannica" />。また、現存する12巻本と20巻本の写本を合わせても欠落がある<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" />。
三代の格は弘仁格の目録を除いては現存しておらず、格の原文が含まれる『類聚三代格』は、当時の古代法制の実態を知るために貴重な史料である<ref name="Britannica" />。
==12巻本系統の構成== *第一巻 - 神社に関する祭幣・託宣・神主・祓い *第二巻 - 造仏・法会・僧職任命 *第三巻 - 国分寺・僧の位階・僧の禁忌・地方の講師 *第四巻 - 中央官司の廃置・定員 *第五巻 - 諸国官僚の廃置・位階 *第六巻 - 官僚の給与体系 *第七巻 - 公卿の意見、地方役人の取り締まり *第八巻 - 農業奨励・租税・金銭貸借 *第九巻 - 班田・公営田・墾田・山川藪沢 *第十巻 - 天皇・宮中の食膳・恩赦・文書・印 *第十一巻 - 軍隊・徴兵・相撲・駅・材木 *第十二巻 - 各種の禁令・刑罰
== 20巻本系統の構成 == *第一巻 - 神社事、[[神封|神封物]]幷租地子事、祭幷幣事、神叙位幷託宣亊、[[斎王|齋王]]亊、[[科祓]]事 *第二巻 佛事上- 造佛々名亊、經論幷法會請僧亊、修法[[灌頂]]事、年分度者事 *第三巻 佛事下 - [[国分寺|國分寺]]亊、[[定額寺]]亊、[[僧綱|僧綱員]]位階幷僧位階亊、諸国[[講讀師]]亊、僧尼禁忌亊 *第四巻 - 癈置諸司亊、加減諸司官員幷癈置事[[雑任|雜任]] *第五巻 - 分置諸國事、加減諸國官員幷癈置事雜任附出、定官員幷官位亊、定内外五位等級亊、交替幷[[解由]]亊 *第六巻 - [[位祿]][[季禄|季祿]][[時服]][[馬料|馬䉼]]亊、[[要劇料|要劇月䉼]]亊、[[公廨]]亊、[[事力]]幷交替丁亊、[[公粮]]亊、[[賻物]]亊 *第七巻 - 公卿意見亊、[[牧宰]]亊、[[郡司]]亊 *第八巻 - 農桑亊、調庸亊、封戸亊、不動々用亊 *第九巻 - *第十巻 - 釋尊亊、國忌亊、供御亊 *第十一巻 - *第十二巻 - 諸使幷[[公文]]亊、[[隠首]][[括出]][[浪人]]亊、[[正倉]][[官倉]]亊 *第十三巻 - *第十四巻 - [[出挙|出舉]]亊、借貸亊、[[雑米|雜米]]亊、[[義倉]]亊、[[塡納]]亊、[[鋳銭司|'''鑄'''銭]]亊 *第十五巻 - [[挍班田]]亊、[[損田]]幷租[[地子]]亊、[[易田]]幷[[公営田|公營田]]亊、[[墾田]]幷[[佃]]亊、[[寺田]]亊、[[諸司田]]亊、[[軄田]][[位田]][[公廨田]]亊 *第十六巻 - [[閑癈地]]亊、[[道槁]]亊、[[船瀬]]幷浮槁[[布施屋]]亊、山野薮澤江河池沼亊、堤堰溝渠亊 *第十七巻 - [[國諱]][[追号|追號]]幷[[改姓|改姓名]]亊、[[蠲免]]<ref>{{Cite kotobank |word=蠲免 |encyclopedia=中日辞典 第3版 |access-date=2024-03-27}}</ref>亊、[[赦除]]<ref>{{Cite kotobank |word=赦除 |encyclopedia=普及版 字通 |access-date=2024-03-27}}</ref>亊、[[募賞]]亊、文書幷印亊 *第十八巻 - [[軍毅]]兵士[[鎮兵|鎭兵]]亊、[[統領]][[選士]][[衛卒]][[衛士]][[仕丁]]亊、[[健児|健兒]]亊、[[器仗]]<ref>{{Cite kotobank |word=器仗 |access-date=2024-03-27}}</ref>亊、[[關]]<ref>{{Cite kotobank |word=關 |access-date=2024-03-27}}</ref>幷[[烽候]]<ref>{{Cite kotobank |word=烽候 |access-date=2024-03-27}}</ref>亊、[[夷俘]]幷[[外蕃人]]亊、[[相撲#歴史|相撲]]亊、國飼幷牧馬牛亊、[[駅伝制#日本|駅傳]]亊、材木亊 *第十九巻 - 禁制亊 *第二十巻 - 断罪[[贖銅]]亊

三代の格は弘仁格の目録を除いては現存しておらず、格の原文が含まれる『類聚三代格』は、当時の古代法制の実態を知るために貴重な史料である<ref name="Britannica" />。
==12巻本系統の構成== *第一巻 - 神社に関する祭幣・託宣・神主・祓い *第二巻 - 造仏・法会・僧職任命 *第三巻 - 国分寺・僧の位階・僧の禁忌・地方の講師 *第四巻 - 中央官司の廃置・定員 *第五巻 - 諸国官僚の廃置・位階 *第六巻 - 官僚の給与体系 *第七巻 - 公卿の意見、地方役人の取り締まり *第八巻 - 農業奨励・租税・金銭貸借 *第九巻 - 班田・公営田・墾田・山川藪沢 *第十巻 - 天皇・宮中の食膳・恩赦・文書・印 *第十一巻 - 軍隊・徴兵・相撲・駅・材木 *第十二巻 - 各種の禁令・刑罰
== 20巻本系統の構成 == *第一巻 - 序亊、神社事、[[神封|神封物]]幷租地子事、祭幷幣事、[[神位|神叙位]]幷[[託宣]]亊、[[斎王|齋王]]亊、[[科祓]]事 *第二巻 佛事上- 造佛々名亊、經論幷法會請僧亊、修法[[灌頂]]事、年分度者事 *第三巻 佛事下 - [[国分寺|國分寺]]亊、[[定額寺]]亊、[[僧綱|僧綱員]]位階幷僧位階亊、諸国[[講讀師]]亊、僧尼禁忌亊 *第四巻 - 癈置諸司亊、加減諸司官員幷癈置事[[雑任|雜任]] *第五巻 - 分置諸國事、加減諸國官員幷癈置事雜任附出、定官員幷官位亊、定内外五位等級亊、交替幷[[解由]]亊 *第六巻 - [[位祿]][[季禄|季祿]][[時服]][[馬料|馬䉼]]亊、[[要劇料|要劇月䉼]]亊、[[公廨]]亊、[[事力]]幷交替丁亊、[[公粮]]亊、[[賻物]]亊 *第七巻 - 公卿意見亊、[[牧宰]]亊、[[郡司]]亊 *第八巻 - 農桑<ref>{{Cite kotobank |word=農桑 |access-date=2024-03-27}}</ref>亊、調庸亊、[[封戸]]亊、不動[[動用倉|動用]]亊 *第九巻 - *第十巻 - 釋尊亊、國忌亊、供御亊 *第十一巻 - *第十二巻 - 諸使幷[[公文]]亊、[[隠首]][[括出]][[浪人]]亊、[[正倉]][[官倉]]亊 *第十三巻 - *第十四巻 - [[出挙|出舉]]亊、借貸亊、[[雑米|雜米]]亊、[[義倉]]亊、[[塡納]]亊、[[鋳銭司|'''鑄'''銭]]亊 *第十五巻 - [[挍班田]]亊、[[損田]]幷租[[地子]]亊、[[易田]]幷[[公営田|公營田]]亊、[[墾田]]幷[[佃]]亊、[[寺田]]亊、[[諸司田]]亊、[[軄田]][[位田]][[公廨田]]亊 *第十六巻 - [[閑癈地]]亊、[[道槁]]亊、[[船瀬]]幷浮槁[[布施屋]]亊、山野薮澤江河池沼亊、堤堰溝渠亊 *第十七巻 - [[國諱]][[追号|追號]]幷[[改姓|改姓名]]亊、[[蠲免]]<ref>{{Cite kotobank |word=蠲免 |encyclopedia=中日辞典 第3版 |access-date=2024-03-27}}</ref>亊、[[赦除]]<ref>{{Cite kotobank |word=赦除 |encyclopedia=普及版 字通 |access-date=2024-03-27}}</ref>亊、[[募賞]]亊、文書幷印亊 *第十八巻 - [[軍毅]]兵士[[鎮兵|鎭兵]]亊、[[統領]][[選士]][[衛卒]][[衛士]][[仕丁]]亊、[[健児|健兒]]亊、[[器仗]]<ref>{{Cite kotobank |word=器仗 |access-date=2024-03-27}}</ref>亊、[[關]]幷[[烽候]]<ref>{{Cite kotobank |word=烽候 |access-date=2024-03-27}}</ref>亊、[[夷俘]]幷[[外蕃人]]亊、[[相撲#歴史|相撲]]亊、國飼幷牧馬牛亊、[[駅伝制#日本|駅傳]]亊、材木亊 *第十九巻 - 禁制亊 *第二十巻 - 断罪[[贖銅]]亊
== 出典 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=国史大系 第12巻 |year=1900 |publisher=経済雑誌社 |chapter=類聚三代格 |volume= |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991102/1/1 |doi=10.11501/991102}} * {{Cite book|和書 |title=国史大系 第25巻 新訂増補 |year=1936 |publisher=国史大系刊行会 |series= |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3431640/1/4 |doi=10.11501/3431640 |chapter=類聚三代格}}
== 関連項目 == *[[類聚国史]]

revision.text:
'''類聚三代格'''（るいじゅうさんだいきゃく）は、[[平安時代]]に編纂された法令集。編者は不明<ref name="Nipponica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref>。
== 概説 == 三代格（[[弘仁格]]、[[貞観格]]、[[延喜格]]）を内容別に「神社事」・「国分寺事」・「庸調事」などと類聚（ジャンル分け）してまとめたもので<ref name="Nipponica" /><ref name="Sekaidaihyakka">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第2版|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref>、先行の格に収録されているものは再録していない<ref name="Britannica" />。成立時期は『[[日本大百科全書]]』によれば[[長保]]4年（[[1002年]]）と[[寛治]]3年（[[1089年]]）の間とされる<ref name="Nipponica" />。三代格では官司別にまとめており、法典として利用するとき不便である上、[[平安時代]]中期に[[有職故実]]化の傾向があったため、典例整備事業の一環として成立したとされる<ref name="Nipponica" />。
完本が現存せず、不完全な写本が伝わっているのみである<ref name="Mypedia">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=百科事典マイペディア|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref>。元となる弘仁格、貞観格、延喜格はそれぞれ10巻、12巻、12巻構成であり<ref name="Britannica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref>、『類聚三代格』は15巻<ref name="Britannica" />もしくは17巻<ref name="Obunsha">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=旺文社日本史事典 三訂版|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref>が現存するとされる。『類聚三代格』の写本は12巻本と20巻本が現存し、『[[本朝書籍目録]]』では全30巻としているものの<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" />、完本の巻数は20巻<ref name="Obunsha" />、30巻<ref name="Mypedia" />など諸説ありで、『ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典』では全30巻の根拠が薄いとしている<ref name="Britannica" />。また、現存する12巻本と20巻本の写本を合わせても欠落がある<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" />。
三代の格は弘仁格の目録を除いては現存しておらず、格の原文が含まれる『類聚三代格』は、当時の古代法制の実態を知るために貴重な史料である<ref name="Britannica" />。
==12巻本系統の構成== *第一巻 - 神社に関する祭幣・託宣・神主・祓い *第二巻 - 造仏・法会・僧職任命 *第三巻 - 国分寺・僧の位階・僧の禁忌・地方の講師 *第四巻 - 中央官司の廃置・定員 *第五巻 - 諸国官僚の廃置・位階 *第六巻 - 官僚の給与体系 *第七巻 - 公卿の意見、地方役人の取り締まり *第八巻 - 農業奨励・租税・金銭貸借 *第九巻 - 班田・公営田・墾田・山川藪沢 *第十巻 - 天皇・宮中の食膳・恩赦・文書・印 *第十一巻 - 軍隊・徴兵・相撲・駅・材木 *第十二巻 - 各種の禁令・刑罰
== 20巻本系統の構成 == *第一巻 - 序亊、神社事、[[神封|神封物]]幷租地子事、祭幷幣事、[[神位|神叙位]]幷[[託宣]]亊、[[斎王|齋王]]亊、[[科祓]]事 *第二巻 佛事上- 造佛々名亊、經論幷法會請僧亊、修法[[灌頂]]事、年分度者事 *第三巻 佛事下 - [[国分寺|國分寺]]亊、[[定額寺]]亊、[[僧綱|僧綱員]]位階幷僧位階亊、諸国[[講讀師]]亊、僧尼禁忌亊 *第四巻 - 癈置[[諸司]]亊、加減諸司官員幷癈置事[[雑任|雜任]] *第五巻 - 分置諸國事、加減諸國官員幷癈置事雜任附出、定官員幷官位亊、定内外五位等級亊、交替幷[[解由]]亊 *第六巻 - [[位祿]][[季禄|季祿]][[時服]][[馬料|馬䉼]]亊、[[要劇料|要劇月䉼]]亊、[[公廨]]亊、[[事力]]幷交替丁亊、[[公粮]]亊、[[賻物]]亊 *第七巻 - 公卿意見亊、[[牧宰]]亊、[[郡司]]亊 *第八巻 - 農桑<ref>{{Cite kotobank |word=農桑 |access-date=2024-03-27}}</ref>亊、調庸亊、[[封戸]]亊、[[不動穀|不動]][[動用倉|動用]]亊 *第九巻 - *第十巻 - 釋尊亊、國忌亊、供御亊 *第十一巻 - *第十二巻 - 諸使幷[[公文]]亊、[[隠首]][[括出]][[浪人]]亊、[[正倉]][[官倉]]亊 *第十三巻 - *第十四巻 - [[出挙|出舉]]亊、借貸亊、[[雑米|雜米]]亊、[[義倉]]亊、[[塡納]]亊、[[鋳銭司|'''鑄'''銭]]亊 *第十五巻 - [[挍班田]]亊、[[損田]]幷租[[地子]]亊、[[易田]]幷[[公営田|公營田]]亊、[[墾田]]幷[[佃]]亊、[[寺田]]亊、[[諸司田]]亊、[[軄田]][[位田]][[公廨田]]亊 *第十六巻 - [[閑癈地]]亊、[[道槁]]亊、[[船瀬]]幷浮槁[[布施屋]]亊、山野薮澤江河池沼亊、堤堰溝渠亊 *第十七巻 - [[國諱]][[追号|追號]]幷[[改姓|改姓名]]亊、[[蠲免]]<ref>{{Cite kotobank |word=蠲免 |encyclopedia=中日辞典 第3版 |access-date=2024-03-27}}</ref>亊、[[赦除]]<ref>{{Cite kotobank |word=赦除 |encyclopedia=普及版 字通 |access-date=2024-03-27}}</ref>亊、[[募賞]]亊、文書幷印亊 *第十八巻 - [[軍毅]]兵士[[鎮兵|鎭兵]]亊、[[統領]][[選士]][[衛卒]][[衛士]][[仕丁]]亊、[[健児|健兒]]亊、[[器仗]]<ref>{{Cite kotobank |word=器仗 |access-date=2024-03-27}}</ref>亊、[[關]]幷[[烽候]]<ref>{{Cite kotobank |word=烽候 |access-date=2024-03-27}}</ref>亊、[[夷俘]]幷[[外蕃人]]亊、[[相撲#歴史|相撲]]亊、國飼幷牧馬牛亊、[[駅伝制#日本|駅傳]]亊、材木亊 *第十九巻 - 禁制亊 *第二十巻 - 断罪[[贖銅]]亊
== 出典 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=国史大系 第12巻 |year=1900 |publisher=経済雑誌社 |chapter=類聚三代格 |volume= |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991102/1/1 |doi=10.11501/991102}} * {{Cite book|和書 |title=国史大系 第25巻 新訂増補 |year=1936 |publisher=国史大系刊行会 |series= |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3431640/1/4 |doi=10.11501/3431640 |chapter=類聚三代格}}

revision.text:
'''Ruijū Sandai Kyaku''' (類聚三代格) is a compilation of legal codes from the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. The compiler is unknown.<ref name="Nipponica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref>
== Overview == The *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* organizes the legal codes of the {{ill|Kōnin Kyaku|ja|弘仁格}}, {{ill|Jōgan Kyaku|ja|貞観格}}, and {{ill|Engi Kyaku|ja|延喜格}} thematically into categories such as "Shinto Shrine Affairs," "Provincial Temple Affairs," and "Tax Matters."<ref name="Nipponica" /><ref name="Sekaidaihyakka">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第2版|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref> It does not re-record items already included in previous compilations.<ref name="Britannica" /> The compilation date is estimated to be between {{ill|Chōhō 4|ja|長保}} (1002) and {{ill|Kanjī 3|ja|寛治}} (1089) according to the *Nipponica* encyclopedia.<ref name="Nipponica" />
The three-generation codes were originally organized by government office, which was inconvenient for legal reference, especially during the mid-Heian period when there was a growing trend towards the study of {{ill|Yūsoku kojitsu|ja|有職故実}} (ancient court practices). Thus, the *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* was compiled as part of legal codification efforts.<ref name="Nipponica" />
No complete manuscript survives; only partial copies are extant.<ref name="Mypedia">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=百科事典マイペディア|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref> The original codes—*Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku*—comprised 10, 12, and 12 volumes respectively.<ref name="Britannica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref> Existing manuscripts of *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* are said to contain 15<ref name="Britannica" /> or 17 volumes,<ref name="Obunsha">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=旺文社日本史事典 三訂版|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref> with versions ranging from 12 to 20 volumes. The *Honchō Shojaku Mokuroku* lists it as having 30 volumes,<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" /> though scholars debate whether the full compilation indeed contained 20<ref name="Obunsha" /> or 30 volumes,<ref name="Mypedia" /> with *Britannica* casting doubt on the 30-volume theory.<ref name="Britannica" /> Even combining the surviving 12- and 20-volume manuscripts, gaps remain.<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" />
Since the original texts of the *Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku* are mostly lost (except for the *Kōnin Kyaku* index), *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku*—which preserves their contents—is an invaluable resource for understanding ancient Japanese legal systems.<ref name="Britannica" />
== Structure of the 12-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Shinto shrine rituals, divine oracles, shrine priests, purification * Volume 2 - Buddha images, Buddhist ceremonies, appointment of clergy * Volume 3 - Provincial temples, clerical ranks, monastic prohibitions, regional lecturers * Volume 4 - Central government office reorganizations and staffing * Volume 5 - Provincial bureaucracy and ranks * Volume 6 - Bureaucratic salary systems * Volume 7 - Opinions of court nobles, oversight of local officials * Volume 8 - Agricultural promotion, taxation, monetary lending * Volume 9 - Land allotments, state-managed fields, land reclamation, mountains, rivers, and wetlands * Volume 10 - Imperial household cuisine, amnesties, documents, seals * Volume 11 - Military affairs, conscription, sumo, postal stations, timber * Volume 12 - Various prohibitions and punishments
== Structure of the 20-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Preface, shrine affairs, divine properties and land taxes, rituals and offerings, divine rankings, oracles, Saiō matters, purification matters * Volume 2 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 1): Creation of Buddha images, sutra copying, Buddhist ceremonies, tantric initiations, state-sponsored monastics * Volume 3 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 2): Provincial temples, established temples, monastic ranks, provincial lecturers, monastic prohibitions * Volume 4 - Government office reforms, staff adjustments, miscellaneous appointments * Volume 5 - Provincial governance, staffing adjustments, official ranks, administrative transitions * Volume 6 - Salaries, stipends, official attire, public resources, labor duties, aid distribution * Volume 7 - Court noble opinions, livestock management, district officials * Volume 8 - Agriculture and sericulture, taxation, household registries, grain storage, economic regulations * Volume 9 - (Content missing) * Volume 10 - Imperial affairs, national mourning, court cuisine * Volume 11 - (Content missing) * Volume 12 - Official missions, legal documents, fugitives, state warehouses * Volume 13 - (Content missing) * Volume 14 - Loans, financial transactions, miscellaneous grains, relief granaries, land management, coin minting * Volume 15 - Land distribution, tax land, state-owned fields, cultivated land, temple lands, official lands * Volume 16 - Abandoned lands, roads, ports, wilderness management, riverbanks, irrigation * Volume 17 - Name changes, amnesties, pardons, reward systems, document management * Volume 18 - Military affairs, conscription, defense units, weapons, border controls, foreign relations, sumo, livestock, postal systems, timber management * Volume 19 - Prohibitions and restrictions * Volume 20 - Criminal judgments and fines
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Sources == * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第12巻 (National Historical Series, Vol. 12) |year=1900 |publisher=経済雑誌社 |chapter=類聚三代格 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991102/1/1 |doi=10.11501/991102}} * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第25巻 新訂増補 (National Historical Series, Vol. 25) |year=1936 |publisher=国史大系刊行会 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3431640/1/4 |doi=10.11501/3431640 |chapter=類聚三代格}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Ruijū Kokushi|ja|類聚国史}}
== External Links == * [http://codh.rois.ac.jp/pmjt/book/200018252/ *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* - Japanese Classical Text Database]

revision.text:
'''Ruijū Sandai Kyaku''' (類聚三代格) is a compilation of legal codes from the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. The compiler is unknown.<ref name="Nipponica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref>
== Overview == The *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* organizes the legal codes of the {{ill|Kōnin Kyaku|ja|弘仁格}}, {{ill|Jōgan Kyaku|ja|貞観格}}, and {{ill|Engi Kyaku|ja|延喜格}} thematically into categories such as "Shinto Shrine Affairs," "Provincial Temple Affairs," and "Tax Matters."<ref name="Nipponica" /><ref name="Sekaidaihyakka">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第2版|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref> It does not re-record items already included in previous compilations.<ref name="Britannica" /> The compilation date is estimated to be between {{ill|Chōhō 4|ja|長保}} (1002) and {{ill|Kanjī 3|ja|寛治}} (1089) according to the *Nipponica* encyclopedia.<ref name="Nipponica" />
The three-generation codes were originally organized by government office, which was inconvenient for legal reference, especially during the mid-Heian period when there was a growing trend towards the study of {{ill|Yūsoku kojitsu|ja|有職故実}} (ancient court practices). Thus, the *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* was compiled as part of legal codification efforts.<ref name="Nipponica" />
No complete manuscript survives; only partial copies are extant.<ref name="Mypedia">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=百科事典マイペディア|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref> The original codes—*Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku*—comprised 10, 12, and 12 volumes respectively.<ref name="Britannica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref> Existing manuscripts of *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* are said to contain 15<ref name="Britannica" /> or 17 volumes,<ref name="Obunsha">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=旺文社日本史事典 三訂版|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref> with versions ranging from 12 to 20 volumes. The *Honchō Shojaku Mokuroku* lists it as having 30 volumes,<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" /> though scholars debate whether the full compilation indeed contained 20<ref name="Obunsha" /> or 30 volumes,<ref name="Mypedia" /> with *Britannica* casting doubt on the 30-volume theory.<ref name="Britannica" /> Even combining the surviving 12- and 20-volume manuscripts, gaps remain.<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" />
Since the original texts of the *Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku* are mostly lost (except for the *Kōnin Kyaku* index), *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku*—which preserves their contents—is an invaluable resource for understanding ancient Japanese legal systems.<ref name="Britannica" />
== Structure of the 12-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Shinto shrine rituals, divine oracles, shrine priests, purification * Volume 2 - Buddha images, Buddhist ceremonies, appointment of clergy * Volume 3 - Provincial temples, clerical ranks, monastic prohibitions, regional lecturers * Volume 4 - Central government office reorganizations and staffing * Volume 5 - Provincial bureaucracy and ranks * Volume 6 - Bureaucratic salary systems * Volume 7 - Opinions of court nobles, oversight of local officials * Volume 8 - Agricultural promotion, taxation, monetary lending * Volume 9 - Land allotments, state-managed fields, land reclamation, mountains, rivers, and wetlands * Volume 10 - Imperial household cuisine, amnesties, documents, seals * Volume 11 - Military affairs, conscription, sumo, postal stations, timber * Volume 12 - Various prohibitions and punishments
== Structure of the 20-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Preface, shrine affairs, divine properties and land taxes, rituals and offerings, divine rankings, oracles, Saiō matters, purification matters * Volume 2 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 1): Creation of Buddha images, sutra copying, Buddhist ceremonies, tantric initiations, state-sponsored monastics * Volume 3 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 2): Provincial temples, established temples, monastic ranks, provincial lecturers, monastic prohibitions * Volume 4 - Government office reforms, staff adjustments, miscellaneous appointments * Volume 5 - Provincial governance, staffing adjustments, official ranks, administrative transitions * Volume 6 - Salaries, stipends, official attire, public resources, labor duties, aid distribution * Volume 7 - Court noble opinions, livestock management, district officials * Volume 8 - Agriculture and sericulture, taxation, household registries, grain storage, economic regulations * Volume 9 - (Content missing) * Volume 10 - Imperial affairs, national mourning, court cuisine * Volume 11 - (Content missing) * Volume 12 - Official missions, legal documents, fugitives, state warehouses * Volume 13 - (Content missing) * Volume 14 - Loans, financial transactions, miscellaneous grains, relief granaries, land management, coin minting * Volume 15 - Land distribution, tax land, state-owned fields, cultivated land, temple lands, official lands * Volume 16 - Abandoned lands, roads, ports, wilderness management, riverbanks, irrigation * Volume 17 - Name changes, amnesties, pardons, reward systems, document management * Volume 18 - Military affairs, conscription, defense units, weapons, border controls, foreign relations, sumo, livestock, postal systems, timber management * Volume 19 - Prohibitions and restrictions * Volume 20 - Criminal judgments and fines
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Sources == * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第12巻 (National Historical Series, Vol. 12) |year=1900 |publisher=経済雑誌社 |chapter=類聚三代格 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991102/1/1 |doi=10.11501/991102}} * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第25巻 新訂増補 (National Historical Series, Vol. 25) |year=1936 |publisher=国史大系刊行会 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3431640/1/4 |doi=10.11501/3431640 |chapter=類聚三代格}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Ruijū Kokushi|ja|類聚国史}}
== External Links == * [http://codh.rois.ac.jp/pmjt/book/200018252/ *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* - Japanese Classical Text Database]

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Ruijū Sandai Kyaku''' (類聚三代格) is a compilation of legal codes from the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. The compiler is unknown.<ref name="Nipponica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref>
== Overview == The *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* organizes the legal codes of the {{ill|Kōnin Kyaku|ja|弘仁格}}, {{ill|Jōgan Kyaku|ja|貞観格}}, and {{ill|Engi Kyaku|ja|延喜格}} thematically into categories such as "Shinto Shrine Affairs," "Provincial Temple Affairs," and "Tax Matters."<ref name="Nipponica" /><ref name="Sekaidaihyakka">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第2版|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref> It does not re-record items already included in previous compilations.<ref name="Britannica" /> The compilation date is estimated to be between {{ill|Chōhō 4|ja|長保}} (1002) and {{ill|Kanjī 3|ja|寛治}} (1089) according to the *Nipponica* encyclopedia.<ref name="Nipponica" />
The three-generation codes were originally organized by government office, which was inconvenient for legal reference, especially during the mid-Heian period when there was a growing trend towards the study of {{ill|Yūsoku kojitsu|ja|有職故実}} (ancient court practices). Thus, the *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* was compiled as part of legal codification efforts.<ref name="Nipponica" />
No complete manuscript survives; only partial copies are extant.<ref name="Mypedia">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=百科事典マイペディア|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref> The original codes—*Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku*—comprised 10, 12, and 12 volumes respectively.<ref name="Britannica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref> Existing manuscripts of *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* are said to contain 15<ref name="Britannica" /> or 17 volumes,<ref name="Obunsha">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=旺文社日本史事典 三訂版|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref> with versions ranging from 12 to 20 volumes. The *Honchō Shojaku Mokuroku* lists it as having 30 volumes,<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" /> though scholars debate whether the full compilation indeed contained 20<ref name="Obunsha" /> or 30 volumes,<ref name="Mypedia" /> with *Britannica* casting doubt on the 30-volume theory.<ref name="Britannica" /> Even combining the surviving 12- and 20-volume manuscripts, gaps remain.<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" />
Since the original texts of the *Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku* are mostly lost (except for the *Kōnin Kyaku* index), *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku*—which preserves their contents—is an invaluable resource for understanding ancient Japanese legal systems.<ref name="Britannica" />
== Structure of the 12-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Shinto shrine rituals, divine oracles, shrine priests, purification * Volume 2 - Buddha images, Buddhist ceremonies, appointment of clergy * Volume 3 - Provincial temples, clerical ranks, monastic prohibitions, regional lecturers * Volume 4 - Central government office reorganizations and staffing * Volume 5 - Provincial bureaucracy and ranks * Volume 6 - Bureaucratic salary systems * Volume 7 - Opinions of court nobles, oversight of local officials * Volume 8 - Agricultural promotion, taxation, monetary lending * Volume 9 - Land allotments, state-managed fields, land reclamation, mountains, rivers, and wetlands * Volume 10 - Imperial household cuisine, amnesties, documents, seals * Volume 11 - Military affairs, conscription, sumo, postal stations, timber * Volume 12 - Various prohibitions and punishments
== Structure of the 20-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Preface, shrine affairs, divine properties and land taxes, rituals and offerings, divine rankings, oracles, Saiō matters, purification matters * Volume 2 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 1): Creation of Buddha images, sutra copying, Buddhist ceremonies, tantric initiations, state-sponsored monastics * Volume 3 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 2): Provincial temples, established temples, monastic ranks, provincial lecturers, monastic prohibitions * Volume 4 - Government office reforms, staff adjustments, miscellaneous appointments * Volume 5 - Provincial governance, staffing adjustments, official ranks, administrative transitions * Volume 6 - Salaries, stipends, official attire, public resources, labor duties, aid distribution * Volume 7 - Court noble opinions, livestock management, district officials * Volume 8 - Agriculture and sericulture, taxation, household registries, grain storage, economic regulations * Volume 9 - (Content missing) * Volume 10 - Imperial affairs, national mourning, court cuisine * Volume 11 - (Content missing) * Volume 12 - Official missions, legal documents, fugitives, state warehouses * Volume 13 - (Content missing) * Volume 14 - Loans, financial transactions, miscellaneous grains, relief granaries, land management, coin minting * Volume 15 - Land distribution, tax land, state-owned fields, cultivated land, temple lands, official lands * Volume 16 - Abandoned lands, roads, ports, wilderness management, riverbanks, irrigation * Volume 17 - Name changes, amnesties, pardons, reward systems, document management * Volume 18 - Military affairs, conscription, defense units, weapons, border controls, foreign relations, sumo, livestock, postal systems, timber management * Volume 19 - Prohibitions and restrictions * Volume 20 - Criminal judgments and fines
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Sources == * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第12巻 (National Historical Series, Vol. 12) |year=1900 |publisher=経済雑誌社 |chapter=類聚三代格 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991102/1/1 |doi=10.11501/991102}} * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第25巻 新訂増補 (National Historical Series, Vol. 25) |year=1936 |publisher=国史大系刊行会 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3431640/1/4 |doi=10.11501/3431640 |chapter=類聚三代格}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Ruijū Kokushi|ja|類聚国史}}
== External Links == * [http://codh.rois.ac.jp/pmjt/book/200018252/ *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* - Japanese Classical Text Database]
{{DEFAULTSORT:Ruijū Sandai Kyaku}} [[Category:Books of the Heian period]] [[Category:Kyaku-shiki in Japan]] [[Category:Legal Codes]] [[ja:類聚三代格]]

revision.text:
'''Ruijū Sandai Kyaku''' (類聚三代格) is a compilation of legal codes from the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. The compiler is unknown.<ref name="Nipponica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref>
== Overview == The *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* organizes the legal codes of the {{ill|Kōnin Kyaku|ja|弘仁格}}, {{ill|Jōgan Kyaku|ja|貞観格}}, and {{ill|Engi Kyaku|ja|延喜格}} thematically into categories such as "Shinto Shrine Affairs," "Provincial Temple Affairs," and "Tax Matters."<ref name="Nipponica" /><ref name="Sekaidaihyakka">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第2版|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref> It does not re-record items already included in previous compilations.<ref name="Britannica" /> The compilation date is estimated to be between {{ill|Chōhō 4|ja|長保}} (1002) and {{ill|Kanjī 3|ja|寛治}} (1089) according to the *Nipponica* encyclopedia.<ref name="Nipponica" />
The three-generation codes were originally organized by government office, which was inconvenient for legal reference, especially during the mid-Heian period when there was a growing trend towards the study of {{ill|Yūsoku kojitsu|ja|有職故実}} (ancient court practices). Thus, the *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* was compiled as part of legal codification efforts.<ref name="Nipponica" />
No complete manuscript survives; only partial copies are extant.<ref name="Mypedia">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=百科事典マイペディア|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref> The original codes—*Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku*—comprised 10, 12, and 12 volumes respectively.<ref name="Britannica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref> Existing manuscripts of *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* are said to contain 15<ref name="Britannica" /> or 17 volumes,<ref name="Obunsha">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=旺文社日本史事典 三訂版|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref> with versions ranging from 12 to 20 volumes. The *Honchō Shojaku Mokuroku* lists it as having 30 volumes,<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" /> though scholars debate whether the full compilation indeed contained 20<ref name="Obunsha" /> or 30 volumes,<ref name="Mypedia" /> with *Britannica* casting doubt on the 30-volume theory.<ref name="Britannica" /> Even combining the surviving 12- and 20-volume manuscripts, gaps remain.<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" />
Since the original texts of the *Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku* are mostly lost (except for the *Kōnin Kyaku* index), *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku*—which preserves their contents—is an invaluable resource for understanding ancient Japanese legal systems.<ref name="Britannica" />
== Structure of the 12-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Shinto shrine rituals, divine oracles, shrine priests, purification * Volume 2 - Buddha images, Buddhist ceremonies, appointment of clergy * Volume 3 - Provincial temples, clerical ranks, monastic prohibitions, regional lecturers * Volume 4 - Central government office reorganizations and staffing * Volume 5 - Provincial bureaucracy and ranks * Volume 6 - Bureaucratic salary systems * Volume 7 - Opinions of court nobles, oversight of local officials * Volume 8 - Agricultural promotion, taxation, monetary lending * Volume 9 - Land allotments, state-managed fields, land reclamation, mountains, rivers, and wetlands * Volume 10 - Imperial household cuisine, amnesties, documents, seals * Volume 11 - Military affairs, conscription, sumo, postal stations, timber * Volume 12 - Various prohibitions and punishments
== Structure of the 20-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Preface, shrine affairs, divine properties and land taxes, rituals and offerings, divine rankings, oracles, Saiō matters, purification matters * Volume 2 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 1): Creation of Buddha images, sutra copying, Buddhist ceremonies, tantric initiations, state-sponsored monastics * Volume 3 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 2): Provincial temples, established temples, monastic ranks, provincial lecturers, monastic prohibitions * Volume 4 - Government office reforms, staff adjustments, miscellaneous appointments * Volume 5 - Provincial governance, staffing adjustments, official ranks, administrative transitions * Volume 6 - Salaries, stipends, official attire, public resources, labor duties, aid distribution * Volume 7 - Court noble opinions, livestock management, district officials * Volume 8 - Agriculture and sericulture, taxation, household registries, grain storage, economic regulations * Volume 9 - (Content missing) * Volume 10 - Imperial affairs, national mourning, court cuisine * Volume 11 - (Content missing) * Volume 12 - Official missions, legal documents, fugitives, state warehouses * Volume 13 - (Content missing) * Volume 14 - Loans, financial transactions, miscellaneous grains, relief granaries, land management, coin minting * Volume 15 - Land distribution, tax land, state-owned fields, cultivated land, temple lands, official lands * Volume 16 - Abandoned lands, roads, ports, wilderness management, riverbanks, irrigation * Volume 17 - Name changes, amnesties, pardons, reward systems, document management * Volume 18 - Military affairs, conscription, defense units, weapons, border controls, foreign relations, sumo, livestock, postal systems, timber management * Volume 19 - Prohibitions and restrictions * Volume 20 - Criminal judgments and fines
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Sources == * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第12巻 (National Historical Series, Vol. 12) |year=1900 |publisher=経済雑誌社 |chapter=類聚三代格 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991102/1/1 |doi=10.11501/991102}} * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第25巻 新訂増補 (National Historical Series, Vol. 25) |year=1936 |publisher=国史大系刊行会 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3431640/1/4 |doi=10.11501/3431640 |chapter=類聚三代格}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Ruijū Kokushi|ja|類聚国史}}
== External Links == * [http://codh.rois.ac.jp/pmjt/book/200018252/ *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* - Japanese Classical Text Database]
{{DEFAULTSORT:Ruijū Sandai Kyaku}} [[Category:Books of the Heian period]] [[Category:Kyaku-shiki in Japan]] [[Category:Legal Codes]] [[ja:類聚三代格]]

'''Ruijū Sandai Kyaku''' (類聚三代格) is a compilation of legal codes from the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. The compiler is unknown.<ref name="Nipponica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref>
== Overview == The *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* organizes the legal codes of the {{ill|Kōnin Kyaku|ja|弘仁格}}, {{ill|Jōgan Kyaku|ja|貞観格}}, and {{ill|Engi Kyaku|ja|延喜格}} thematically into categories such as "Shinto Shrine Affairs," "Provincial Temple Affairs," and "Tax Matters."<ref name="Nipponica" /><ref name="Sekaidaihyakka">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第2版|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref> It does not re-record items already included in previous compilations.<ref name="Britannica" /> The compilation date is estimated to be between {{ill|Chōhō 4|ja|長保}} (1002) and {{ill|Kanjī 3|ja|寛治}} (1089) according to the *Nipponica* encyclopedia.<ref name="Nipponica" />
The three-generation codes were originally organized by government office, which was inconvenient for legal reference, especially during the mid-Heian period when there was a growing trend towards the study of {{ill|Yūsoku kojitsu|ja|有職故実}} (ancient court practices). Thus, the *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* was compiled as part of legal codification efforts.<ref name="Nipponica" />
No complete manuscript survives; only partial copies are extant.<ref name="Mypedia">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=百科事典マイペディア|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref> The original codes—*Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku*—comprised 10, 12, and 12 volumes respectively.<ref name="Britannica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref> Existing manuscripts of *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* are said to contain 15<ref name="Britannica" /> or 17 volumes,<ref name="Obunsha">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=旺文社日本史事典 三訂版|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref> with versions ranging from 12 to 20 volumes. The *Honchō Shojaku Mokuroku* lists it as having 30 volumes,<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" /> though scholars debate whether the full compilation indeed contained 20<ref name="Obunsha" /> or 30 volumes,<ref name="Mypedia" /> with *Britannica* casting doubt on the 30-volume theory.<ref name="Britannica" /> Even combining the surviving 12- and 20-volume manuscripts, gaps remain.<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" />
Since the original texts of the *Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku* are mostly lost (except for the *Kōnin Kyaku* index), *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku*—which preserves their contents—is an invaluable resource for understanding ancient Japanese legal systems.<ref name="Britannica" />
== Structure of the 12-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Shinto shrine rituals, divine oracles, shrine priests, purification * Volume 2 - Buddha images, Buddhist ceremonies, appointment of clergy * Volume 3 - Provincial temples, clerical ranks, monastic prohibitions, regional lecturers * Volume 4 - Central government office reorganizations and staffing * Volume 5 - Provincial bureaucracy and ranks * Volume 6 - Bureaucratic salary systems * Volume 7 - Opinions of court nobles, oversight of local officials * Volume 8 - Agricultural promotion, taxation, monetary lending * Volume 9 - Land allotments, state-managed fields, land reclamation, mountains, rivers, and wetlands * Volume 10 - Imperial household cuisine, amnesties, documents, seals * Volume 11 - Military affairs, conscription, sumo, postal stations, timber * Volume 12 - Various prohibitions and punishments
== Structure of the 20-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Preface, shrine affairs, divine properties and land taxes, rituals and offerings, divine rankings, oracles, Saiō matters, purification matters * Volume 2 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 1): Creation of Buddha images, sutra copying, Buddhist ceremonies, tantric initiations, state-sponsored monastics * Volume 3 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 2): Provincial temples, established temples, monastic ranks, provincial lecturers, monastic prohibitions * Volume 4 - Government office reforms, staff adjustments, miscellaneous appointments * Volume 5 - Provincial governance, staffing adjustments, official ranks, administrative transitions * Volume 6 - Salaries, stipends, official attire, public resources, labor duties, aid distribution * Volume 7 - Court noble opinions, livestock management, district officials * Volume 8 - Agriculture and sericulture, taxation, household registries, grain storage, economic regulations * Volume 9 - (Content missing) * Volume 10 - Imperial affairs, national mourning, court cuisine * Volume 11 - (Content missing) * Volume 12 - Official missions, legal documents, fugitives, state warehouses * Volume 13 - (Content missing) * Volume 14 - Loans, financial transactions, miscellaneous grains, relief granaries, land management, coin minting * Volume 15 - Land distribution, tax land, state-owned fields, cultivated land, temple lands, official lands * Volume 16 - Abandoned lands, roads, ports, wilderness management, riverbanks, irrigation * Volume 17 - Name changes, amnesties, pardons, reward systems, document management * Volume 18 - Military affairs, conscription, defense units, weapons, border controls, foreign relations, sumo, livestock, postal systems, timber management * Volume 19 - Prohibitions and restrictions * Volume 20 - Criminal judgments and fines
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Sources == * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第12巻 (National Historical Series, Vol. 12) |year=1900 |publisher=経済雑誌社 |chapter=類聚三代格 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991102/1/1 |doi=10.11501/991102}} * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第25巻 新訂増補 (National Historical Series, Vol. 25) |year=1936 |publisher=国史大系刊行会 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3431640/1/4 |doi=10.11501/3431640 |chapter=類聚三代格}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Ruijū Kokushi|ja|類聚国史}}
== External Links == * [http://codh.rois.ac.jp/pmjt/book/200018252/ *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* - Japanese Classical Text Database]
{{DEFAULTSORT:Ruijū Sandai Kyaku}} [[Category:Books of the Heian period]] [[Category:Kyaku-shiki in Japan]] [[Category:Legal Codes]] [[ja:類聚三代格]]
== See Also from {{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|ja|格式}} == {{Wiktionary}} * {{ill|Ritsuryō|ja|律令}}－{{ill|Ritsuryō law|ja|律令法}}－{{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}} * {{ill|Three Great Legal Codes|ja|三代格式}} ''' {{ill|Kōnin Kyakushiki|ja|弘仁格式|lt=Kōnin Codes (Kōnin Kyaku, Kōnin Shiki)}} ''' {{ill|Jōgan Kyakushiki|ja|貞観格式|lt=Jōgan Codes (Jōgan Kyaku, Jōgan Shiki)}} ''' {{ill|Engi Kyakushiki|ja|延喜格式|lt=Engi Codes (Engi Kyaku, Engi Shiki)}} * {{ill|Kōtai-shiki|ja|交替式}} * {{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格}} * {{ill|Hachiman Usa-gū Gotakusenshū|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集}} * {{ill|Myōhō-dō|ja|明法道}}－{{ill|Myōhō Hakase|ja|明法博士}} * {{ill|Imperial Examination|ja|科挙}}

'''Ruijū Sandai Kyaku''' (類聚三代格) is a compilation of legal codes from the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. The compiler is unknown.<ref name="Nipponica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref>
== Overview == The *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* organizes the legal codes of the {{ill|Kōnin Kyaku|ja|弘仁格}}, {{ill|Jōgan Kyaku|ja|貞観格}}, and {{ill|Engi Kyaku|ja|延喜格}} thematically into categories such as "Shinto Shrine Affairs," "Provincial Temple Affairs," and "Tax Matters."<ref name="Nipponica" /><ref name="Sekaidaihyakka">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第2版|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref> It does not re-record items already included in previous compilations.<ref name="Britannica" /> The compilation date is estimated to be between {{ill|Chōhō 4|ja|長保}} (1002) and {{ill|Kanjī 3|ja|寛治}} (1089) according to the *Nipponica* encyclopedia.<ref name="Nipponica" />
The three-generation codes were originally organized by government office, which was inconvenient for legal reference, especially during the mid-Heian period when there was a growing trend towards the study of {{ill|Yūsoku kojitsu|ja|有職故実}} (ancient court practices). Thus, the *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* was compiled as part of legal codification efforts.<ref name="Nipponica" />
No complete manuscript survives; only partial copies are extant.<ref name="Mypedia">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=百科事典マイペディア|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref> The original codes—*Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku*—comprised 10, 12, and 12 volumes respectively.<ref name="Britannica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref> Existing manuscripts of *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* are said to contain 15<ref name="Britannica" /> or 17 volumes,<ref name="Obunsha">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=旺文社日本史事典 三訂版|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref> with versions ranging from 12 to 20 volumes. The *Honchō Shojaku Mokuroku* lists it as having 30 volumes,<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" /> though scholars debate whether the full compilation indeed contained 20<ref name="Obunsha" /> or 30 volumes,<ref name="Mypedia" /> with *Britannica* casting doubt on the 30-volume theory.<ref name="Britannica" /> Even combining the surviving 12- and 20-volume manuscripts, gaps remain.<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" />
Since the original texts of the *Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku* are mostly lost (except for the *Kōnin Kyaku* index), *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku*—which preserves their contents—is an invaluable resource for understanding ancient Japanese legal systems.<ref name="Britannica" />
== Structure of the 12-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Shinto shrine rituals, divine oracles, shrine priests, purification * Volume 2 - Buddha images, Buddhist ceremonies, appointment of clergy * Volume 3 - Provincial temples, clerical ranks, monastic prohibitions, regional lecturers * Volume 4 - Central government office reorganizations and staffing * Volume 5 - Provincial bureaucracy and ranks * Volume 6 - Bureaucratic salary systems * Volume 7 - Opinions of court nobles, oversight of local officials * Volume 8 - Agricultural promotion, taxation, monetary lending * Volume 9 - Land allotments, state-managed fields, land reclamation, mountains, rivers, and wetlands * Volume 10 - Imperial household cuisine, amnesties, documents, seals * Volume 11 - Military affairs, conscription, sumo, postal stations, timber * Volume 12 - Various prohibitions and punishments
== Structure of the 20-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Preface, shrine affairs, divine properties and land taxes, rituals and offerings, divine rankings, oracles, Saiō matters, purification matters * Volume 2 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 1): Creation of Buddha images, sutra copying, Buddhist ceremonies, tantric initiations, state-sponsored monastics * Volume 3 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 2): Provincial temples, established temples, monastic ranks, provincial lecturers, monastic prohibitions * Volume 4 - Government office reforms, staff adjustments, miscellaneous appointments * Volume 5 - Provincial governance, staffing adjustments, official ranks, administrative transitions * Volume 6 - Salaries, stipends, official attire, public resources, labor duties, aid distribution * Volume 7 - Court noble opinions, livestock management, district officials * Volume 8 - Agriculture and sericulture, taxation, household registries, grain storage, economic regulations * Volume 9 - (Content missing) * Volume 10 - Imperial affairs, national mourning, court cuisine * Volume 11 - (Content missing) * Volume 12 - Official missions, legal documents, fugitives, state warehouses * Volume 13 - (Content missing) * Volume 14 - Loans, financial transactions, miscellaneous grains, relief granaries, land management, coin minting * Volume 15 - Land distribution, tax land, state-owned fields, cultivated land, temple lands, official lands * Volume 16 - Abandoned lands, roads, ports, wilderness management, riverbanks, irrigation * Volume 17 - Name changes, amnesties, pardons, reward systems, document management * Volume 18 - Military affairs, conscription, defense units, weapons, border controls, foreign relations, sumo, livestock, postal systems, timber management * Volume 19 - Prohibitions and restrictions * Volume 20 - Criminal judgments and fines
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Sources == * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第12巻 (National Historical Series, Vol. 12) |year=1900 |publisher=経済雑誌社 |chapter=類聚三代格 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991102/1/1 |doi=10.11501/991102}} * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第25巻 新訂増補 (National Historical Series, Vol. 25) |year=1936 |publisher=国史大系刊行会 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3431640/1/4 |doi=10.11501/3431640 |chapter=類聚三代格}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Ruijū Kokushi|ja|類聚国史}}
== External Links == * [http://codh.rois.ac.jp/pmjt/book/200018252/ *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* - Japanese Classical Text Database]
{{DEFAULTSORT:Ruijū Sandai Kyaku}} [[Category:Books of the Heian period]] [[Category:Kyaku-shiki in Japan]] [[Category:Legal Codes]] [[ja:類聚三代格]]

'''Ruijū Sandai Kyaku''' (類聚三代格) is a compilation of legal codes from the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. The compiler is unknown.<ref name="Nipponica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref>
== Overview == The *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* organizes the legal codes of the {{ill|Kōnin Kyaku|ja|弘仁格}}, {{ill|Jōgan Kyaku|ja|貞観格}}, and {{ill|Engi Kyaku|ja|延喜格}} thematically into categories such as "Shinto Shrine Affairs," "Provincial Temple Affairs," and "Tax Matters."<ref name="Nipponica" /><ref name="Sekaidaihyakka">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第2版|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref> It does not re-record items already included in previous compilations.<ref name="Britannica" /> The compilation date is estimated to be between {{ill|Chōhō 4|ja|長保}} (1002) and {{ill|Kanjī 3|ja|寛治}} (1089) according to the *Nipponica* encyclopedia.<ref name="Nipponica" />
The three-generation codes were originally organized by government office, which was inconvenient for legal reference, especially during the mid-Heian period when there was a growing trend towards the study of {{ill|Yūsoku kojitsu|ja|有職故実}} (ancient court practices). Thus, the *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* was compiled as part of legal codification efforts.<ref name="Nipponica" />
No complete manuscript survives; only partial copies are extant.<ref name="Mypedia">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=百科事典マイペディア|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref> The original codes—*Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku*—comprised 10, 12, and 12 volumes respectively.<ref name="Britannica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref> Existing manuscripts of *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* are said to contain 15<ref name="Britannica" /> or 17 volumes,<ref name="Obunsha">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=旺文社日本史事典 三訂版|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref> with versions ranging from 12 to 20 volumes. The *Honchō Shojaku Mokuroku* lists it as having 30 volumes,<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" /> though scholars debate whether the full compilation indeed contained 20<ref name="Obunsha" /> or 30 volumes,<ref name="Mypedia" /> with *Britannica* casting doubt on the 30-volume theory.<ref name="Britannica" /> Even combining the surviving 12- and 20-volume manuscripts, gaps remain.<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" />
Since the original texts of the *Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku* are mostly lost (except for the *Kōnin Kyaku* index), *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku*—which preserves their contents—is an invaluable resource for understanding ancient Japanese legal systems.<ref name="Britannica" />
== Structure of the 12-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Shinto shrine rituals, divine oracles, shrine priests, purification * Volume 2 - Buddha images, Buddhist ceremonies, appointment of clergy * Volume 3 - Provincial temples, clerical ranks, monastic prohibitions, regional lecturers * Volume 4 - Central government office reorganizations and staffing * Volume 5 - Provincial bureaucracy and ranks * Volume 6 - Bureaucratic salary systems * Volume 7 - Opinions of court nobles, oversight of local officials * Volume 8 - Agricultural promotion, taxation, monetary lending * Volume 9 - Land allotments, state-managed fields, land reclamation, mountains, rivers, and wetlands * Volume 10 - Imperial household cuisine, amnesties, documents, seals * Volume 11 - Military affairs, conscription, sumo, postal stations, timber * Volume 12 - Various prohibitions and punishments
== Structure of the 20-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Preface, shrine affairs, divine properties and land taxes, rituals and offerings, divine rankings, oracles, Saiō matters, purification matters * Volume 2 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 1): Creation of Buddha images, sutra copying, Buddhist ceremonies, tantric initiations, state-sponsored monastics * Volume 3 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 2): Provincial temples, established temples, monastic ranks, provincial lecturers, monastic prohibitions * Volume 4 - Government office reforms, staff adjustments, miscellaneous appointments * Volume 5 - Provincial governance, staffing adjustments, official ranks, administrative transitions * Volume 6 - Salaries, stipends, official attire, public resources, labor duties, aid distribution * Volume 7 - Court noble opinions, livestock management, district officials * Volume 8 - Agriculture and sericulture, taxation, household registries, grain storage, economic regulations * Volume 9 - (Content missing) * Volume 10 - Imperial affairs, national mourning, court cuisine * Volume 11 - (Content missing) * Volume 12 - Official missions, legal documents, fugitives, state warehouses * Volume 13 - (Content missing) * Volume 14 - Loans, financial transactions, miscellaneous grains, relief granaries, land management, coin minting * Volume 15 - Land distribution, tax land, state-owned fields, cultivated land, temple lands, official lands * Volume 16 - Abandoned lands, roads, ports, wilderness management, riverbanks, irrigation * Volume 17 - Name changes, amnesties, pardons, reward systems, document management * Volume 18 - Military affairs, conscription, defense units, weapons, border controls, foreign relations, sumo, livestock, postal systems, timber management * Volume 19 - Prohibitions and restrictions * Volume 20 - Criminal judgments and fines
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Sources == * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第12巻 (National Historical Series, Vol. 12) |year=1900 |publisher=経済雑誌社 |chapter=類聚三代格 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991102/1/1 |doi=10.11501/991102}} * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第25巻 新訂増補 (National Historical Series, Vol. 25) |year=1936 |publisher=国史大系刊行会 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3431640/1/4 |doi=10.11501/3431640 |chapter=類聚三代格}}

revision.text:
'''Ruijū Sandai Kyaku''' (類聚三代格) is a compilation of legal codes from the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. The compiler is unknown.<ref name="Nipponica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref>
== Overview == The *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* organizes the legal codes of the {{ill|Kōnin Kyaku|ja|弘仁格}}, {{ill|Jōgan Kyaku|ja|貞観格}}, and {{ill|Engi Kyaku|ja|延喜格}} thematically into categories such as "Shinto Shrine Affairs," "Provincial Temple Affairs," and "Tax Matters."<ref name="Nipponica" /><ref name="Sekaidaihyakka">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第2版|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref> It does not re-record items already included in previous compilations.<ref name="Britannica" /> The compilation date is estimated to be between {{ill|Chōhō 4|ja|長保}} (1002) and {{ill|Kanjī 3|ja|寛治}} (1089) according to the *Nipponica* encyclopedia.<ref name="Nipponica" />
The three-generation codes were originally organized by government office, which was inconvenient for legal reference, especially during the mid-Heian period when there was a growing trend towards the study of {{ill|Yūsoku kojitsu|ja|有職故実}} (ancient court practices). Thus, the *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* was compiled as part of legal codification efforts.<ref name="Nipponica" />
No complete manuscript survives; only partial copies are extant.<ref name="Mypedia">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=百科事典マイペディア|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref> The original codes—*Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku*—comprised 10, 12, and 12 volumes respectively.<ref name="Britannica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref> Existing manuscripts of *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* are said to contain 15<ref name="Britannica" /> or 17 volumes,<ref name="Obunsha">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=旺文社日本史事典 三訂版|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref> with versions ranging from 12 to 20 volumes. The *Honchō Shojaku Mokuroku* lists it as having 30 volumes,<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" /> though scholars debate whether the full compilation indeed contained 20<ref name="Obunsha" /> or 30 volumes,<ref name="Mypedia" /> with *Britannica* casting doubt on the 30-volume theory.<ref name="Britannica" /> Even combining the surviving 12- and 20-volume manuscripts, gaps remain.<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" />
Since the original texts of the *Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku* are mostly lost (except for the *Kōnin Kyaku* index), *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku*—which preserves their contents—is an invaluable resource for understanding ancient Japanese legal systems.<ref name="Britannica" />
== Structure of the 12-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Shinto shrine rituals, divine oracles, shrine priests, purification * Volume 2 - Buddha images, Buddhist ceremonies, appointment of clergy * Volume 3 - Provincial temples, clerical ranks, monastic prohibitions, regional lecturers * Volume 4 - Central government office reorganizations and staffing * Volume 5 - Provincial bureaucracy and ranks * Volume 6 - Bureaucratic salary systems * Volume 7 - Opinions of court nobles, oversight of local officials * Volume 8 - Agricultural promotion, taxation, monetary lending * Volume 9 - Land allotments, state-managed fields, land reclamation, mountains, rivers, and wetlands * Volume 10 - Imperial household cuisine, amnesties, documents, seals * Volume 11 - Military affairs, conscription, sumo, postal stations, timber * Volume 12 - Various prohibitions and punishments
== Structure of the 20-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Preface, shrine affairs, divine properties and land taxes, rituals and offerings, divine rankings, oracles, Saiō matters, purification matters * Volume 2 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 1): Creation of Buddha images, sutra copying, Buddhist ceremonies, tantric initiations, state-sponsored monastics * Volume 3 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 2): Provincial temples, established temples, monastic ranks, provincial lecturers, monastic prohibitions * Volume 4 - Government office reforms, staff adjustments, miscellaneous appointments * Volume 5 - Provincial governance, staffing adjustments, official ranks, administrative transitions * Volume 6 - Salaries, stipends, official attire, public resources, labor duties, aid distribution * Volume 7 - Court noble opinions, livestock management, district officials * Volume 8 - Agriculture and sericulture, taxation, household registries, grain storage, economic regulations * Volume 9 - (Content missing) * Volume 10 - Imperial affairs, national mourning, court cuisine * Volume 11 - (Content missing) * Volume 12 - Official missions, legal documents, fugitives, state warehouses * Volume 13 - (Content missing) * Volume 14 - Loans, financial transactions, miscellaneous grains, relief granaries, land management, coin minting * Volume 15 - Land distribution, tax land, state-owned fields, cultivated land, temple lands, official lands * Volume 16 - Abandoned lands, roads, ports, wilderness management, riverbanks, irrigation * Volume 17 - Name changes, amnesties, pardons, reward systems, document management * Volume 18 - Military affairs, conscription, defense units, weapons, border controls, foreign relations, sumo, livestock, postal systems, timber management * Volume 19 - Prohibitions and restrictions * Volume 20 - Criminal judgments and fines
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Sources == * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第12巻 (National Historical Series, Vol. 12) |year=1900 |publisher=経済雑誌社 |chapter=類聚三代格 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991102/1/1 |doi=10.11501/991102}} * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第25巻 新訂増補 (National Historical Series, Vol. 25) |year=1936 |publisher=国史大系刊行会 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3431640/1/4 |doi=10.11501/3431640 |chapter=類聚三代格}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Ruijū Kokushi|ja|類聚国史}}
== External Links == * [http://codh.rois.ac.jp/pmjt/book/200018252/ *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* - Japanese Classical Text Database]

== See Also from {{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|ja|格式}} == {{Wiktionary}} * {{ill|Ritsuryō|ja|律令}}－{{ill|Ritsuryō law|ja|律令法}}－{{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}} * {{ill|Three Great Legal Codes|ja|三代格式}} ''' {{ill|Kōnin Kyakushiki|ja|弘仁格式|lt=Kōnin Codes (Kōnin Kyaku, Kōnin Shiki)}} ''' {{ill|Jōgan Kyakushiki|ja|貞観格式|lt=Jōgan Codes (Jōgan Kyaku, Jōgan Shiki)}} ''' {{ill|Engi Kyakushiki|ja|延喜格式|lt=Engi Codes (Engi Kyaku, Engi Shiki)}} * {{ill|Kōtai-shiki|ja|交替式}} * {{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格}} * {{ill|Hachiman Usa-gū Gotakusenshū|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集}} * {{ill|Myōhō-dō|ja|明法道}}－{{ill|Myōhō Hakase|ja|明法博士}} * {{ill|Imperial Examination|ja|科挙}}

revision.text:
'''Ruijū Sandai Kyaku''' (類聚三代格) is a compilation of legal codes from the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. The compiler is unknown.<ref name="Nipponica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref>
== Overview == The *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* organizes the legal codes of the {{ill|Kōnin Kyaku|ja|弘仁格}}, {{ill|Jōgan Kyaku|ja|貞観格}}, and {{ill|Engi Kyaku|ja|延喜格}} thematically into categories such as "Shinto Shrine Affairs," "Provincial Temple Affairs," and "Tax Matters."<ref name="Nipponica" /><ref name="Sekaidaihyakka">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第2版|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref> It does not re-record items already included in previous compilations.<ref name="Britannica" /> The compilation date is estimated to be between {{ill|Chōhō 4|ja|長保}} (1002) and {{ill|Kanjī 3|ja|寛治}} (1089) according to the *Nipponica* encyclopedia.<ref name="Nipponica" />
The three-generation codes were originally organized by government office, which was inconvenient for legal reference, especially during the mid-Heian period when there was a growing trend towards the study of {{ill|Yūsoku kojitsu|ja|有職故実}} (ancient court practices). Thus, the *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* was compiled as part of legal codification efforts.<ref name="Nipponica" />
No complete manuscript survives; only partial copies are extant.<ref name="Mypedia">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=百科事典マイペディア|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref> The original codes—*Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku*—comprised 10, 12, and 12 volumes respectively.<ref name="Britannica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref> Existing manuscripts of *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* are said to contain 15<ref name="Britannica" /> or 17 volumes,<ref name="Obunsha">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=旺文社日本史事典 三訂版|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref> with versions ranging from 12 to 20 volumes. The *Honchō Shojaku Mokuroku* lists it as having 30 volumes,<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" /> though scholars debate whether the full compilation indeed contained 20<ref name="Obunsha" /> or 30 volumes,<ref name="Mypedia" /> with *Britannica* casting doubt on the 30-volume theory.<ref name="Britannica" /> Even combining the surviving 12- and 20-volume manuscripts, gaps remain.<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" />
Since the original texts of the *Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku* are mostly lost (except for the *Kōnin Kyaku* index), *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku*—which preserves their contents—is an invaluable resource for understanding ancient Japanese legal systems.<ref name="Britannica" />
== Structure of the 12-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Shinto shrine rituals, divine oracles, shrine priests, purification * Volume 2 - Buddha images, Buddhist ceremonies, appointment of clergy * Volume 3 - Provincial temples, clerical ranks, monastic prohibitions, regional lecturers * Volume 4 - Central government office reorganizations and staffing * Volume 5 - Provincial bureaucracy and ranks * Volume 6 - Bureaucratic salary systems * Volume 7 - Opinions of court nobles, oversight of local officials * Volume 8 - Agricultural promotion, taxation, monetary lending * Volume 9 - Land allotments, state-managed fields, land reclamation, mountains, rivers, and wetlands * Volume 10 - Imperial household cuisine, amnesties, documents, seals * Volume 11 - Military affairs, conscription, sumo, postal stations, timber * Volume 12 - Various prohibitions and punishments
== Structure of the 20-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Preface, shrine affairs, divine properties and land taxes, rituals and offerings, divine rankings, oracles, Saiō matters, purification matters * Volume 2 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 1): Creation of Buddha images, sutra copying, Buddhist ceremonies, tantric initiations, state-sponsored monastics * Volume 3 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 2): Provincial temples, established temples, monastic ranks, provincial lecturers, monastic prohibitions * Volume 4 - Government office reforms, staff adjustments, miscellaneous appointments * Volume 5 - Provincial governance, staffing adjustments, official ranks, administrative transitions * Volume 6 - Salaries, stipends, official attire, public resources, labor duties, aid distribution * Volume 7 - Court noble opinions, livestock management, district officials * Volume 8 - Agriculture and sericulture, taxation, household registries, grain storage, economic regulations * Volume 9 - (Content missing) * Volume 10 - Imperial affairs, national mourning, court cuisine * Volume 11 - (Content missing) * Volume 12 - Official missions, legal documents, fugitives, state warehouses * Volume 13 - (Content missing) * Volume 14 - Loans, financial transactions, miscellaneous grains, relief granaries, land management, coin minting * Volume 15 - Land distribution, tax land, state-owned fields, cultivated land, temple lands, official lands * Volume 16 - Abandoned lands, roads, ports, wilderness management, riverbanks, irrigation * Volume 17 - Name changes, amnesties, pardons, reward systems, document management * Volume 18 - Military affairs, conscription, defense units, weapons, border controls, foreign relations, sumo, livestock, postal systems, timber management * Volume 19 - Prohibitions and restrictions * Volume 20 - Criminal judgments and fines
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Sources == * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第12巻 (National Historical Series, Vol. 12) |year=1900 |publisher=経済雑誌社 |chapter=類聚三代格 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991102/1/1 |doi=10.11501/991102}} * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第25巻 新訂増補 (National Historical Series, Vol. 25) |year=1936 |publisher=国史大系刊行会 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3431640/1/4 |doi=10.11501/3431640 |chapter=類聚三代格}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Ruijū Kokushi|ja|類聚国史}}
== External Links == * [http://codh.rois.ac.jp/pmjt/book/200018252/ *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* - Japanese Classical Text Database]

The *Honchō Shojaku Mokuroku* lists it as having 30 volumes,<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" /> though scholars debate whether the full compilation indeed contained 20<ref name="Obunsha" /> or 30 volumes,<ref name="Mypedia" /> with *Britannica* casting doubt on the 30-volume theory.<ref name="Britannica" /> Even combining the surviving 12- and 20-volume manuscripts, gaps remain.<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" />
