'''Ruijū Sandai Kyaku''' (類聚三代格) is a compilation of legal codes from the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}. The compiler is unknown.<ref name="Nipponica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref>
== Overview == The *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* organizes the legal codes of the {{ill|Kōnin Kyaku|ja|弘仁格|12=draft}}, {{ill|Jōgan Kyaku|ja|貞観格|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Engi Kyaku|ja|延喜格|12=draft}} thematically into categories such as "Shinto Shrine Affairs," "Provincial Temple Affairs," and "Tax Matters."<ref name="Nipponica" /><ref name="Sekaidaihyakka">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第2版|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> It does not re-record items already included in previous compilations.<ref name="Britannica" /> The compilation date is estimated to be between {{ill|Chōhō 4|ja|長保|12=draft}} (1002) and {{ill|Kanjī 3|ja|寛治|12=draft}} (1089) according to the *Nipponica* encyclopedia.<ref name="Nipponica" />
The three-generation codes were originally organized by government office, which was inconvenient for legal reference, especially during the mid-Heian period when there was a growing trend towards the study of {{ill|Yūsoku kojitsu|ja|有職故実|12=draft}} (ancient court practices). Thus, the *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* was compiled as part of legal codification efforts.<ref name="Nipponica" />
No complete manuscript survives; only partial copies are extant.<ref name="Mypedia">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=百科事典マイペディア|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> The original codes—*Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku*—comprised 10, 12, and 12 volumes respectively.<ref name="Britannica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> Existing manuscripts of *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* are said to contain 15<ref name="Britannica" /> or 17 volumes,<ref name="Obunsha">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=旺文社日本史事典 三訂版|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> with versions ranging from 12 to 20 volumes. The *Honchō Shojaku Mokuroku* lists it as having 30 volumes,<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" /> though scholars debate whether the full compilation indeed contained 20<ref name="Obunsha" /> or 30 volumes,<ref name="Mypedia" /> with *Britannica* casting doubt on the 30-volume theory.<ref name="Britannica" /> Even combining the surviving 12- and 20-volume manuscripts, gaps remain.<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" />
Since the original texts of the *Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku* are mostly lost (except for the *Kōnin Kyaku* index), *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku*—which preserves their contents—is an invaluable resource for understanding ancient Japanese legal systems.<ref name="Britannica" />
== Structure of the 12-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Shinto shrine rituals, divine oracles, shrine priests, purification * Volume 2 - Buddha images, Buddhist ceremonies, appointment of clergy * Volume 3 - Provincial temples, clerical ranks, monastic prohibitions, regional lecturers * Volume 4 - Central government office reorganizations and staffing * Volume 5 - Provincial bureaucracy and ranks * Volume 6 - Bureaucratic salary systems * Volume 7 - Opinions of court nobles, oversight of local officials * Volume 8 - Agricultural promotion, taxation, monetary lending * Volume 9 - Land allotments, state-managed fields, land reclamation, mountains, rivers, and wetlands * Volume 10 - Imperial household cuisine, amnesties, documents, seals * Volume 11 - Military affairs, conscription, sumo, postal stations, timber * Volume 12 - Various prohibitions and punishments
== Structure of the 20-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Preface, shrine affairs, divine properties and land taxes, rituals and offerings, divine rankings, oracles, Saiō matters, purification matters * Volume 2 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 1): Creation of Buddha images, sutra copying, Buddhist ceremonies, tantric initiations, state-sponsored monastics * Volume 3 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 2): Provincial temples, established temples, monastic ranks, provincial lecturers, monastic prohibitions * Volume 4 - Government office reforms, staff adjustments, miscellaneous appointments * Volume 5 - Provincial governance, staffing adjustments, official ranks, administrative transitions * Volume 6 - Salaries, stipends, official attire, public resources, labor duties, aid distribution * Volume 7 - Court noble opinions, livestock management, district officials * Volume 8 - Agriculture and sericulture, taxation, household registries, grain storage, economic regulations * Volume 9 - (Content missing) * Volume 10 - Imperial affairs, national mourning, court cuisine * Volume 11 - (Content missing) * Volume 12 - Official missions, legal documents, fugitives, state warehouses * Volume 13 - (Content missing) * Volume 14 - Loans, financial transactions, miscellaneous grains, relief granaries, land management, coin minting * Volume 15 - Land distribution, tax land, state-owned fields, cultivated land, temple lands, official lands * Volume 16 - Abandoned lands, roads, ports, wilderness management, riverbanks, irrigation * Volume 17 - Name changes, amnesties, pardons, reward systems, document management * Volume 18 - Military affairs, conscription, defense units, weapons, border controls, foreign relations, sumo, livestock, postal systems, timber management * Volume 19 - Prohibitions and restrictions * Volume 20 - Criminal judgments and fines
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Sources == * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第12巻 (National Historical Series, Vol. 12) |year=1900 |publisher=経済雑誌社 |chapter=類聚三代格 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991102/1/1 |doi=10.11501/991102}} * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第25巻 新訂増補 (National Historical Series, Vol. 25) |year=1936 |publisher=国史大系刊行会 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3431640/1/4 |doi=10.11501/3431640 |chapter=類聚三代格}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ruijū Kokushi|ja|類聚国史|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [http://codh.rois.ac.jp/pmjt/book/200018252/ *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* - Japanese Classical Text Database]
== See Also from {{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|ja|格式|12=draft}} == {{Wiktionary}} * {{ill|Ritsuryō|ja|律令|12=draft}}－{{ill|Ritsuryō law|ja|律令法|12=draft}}－{{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|12=draft}} * {{ill|Three Great Legal Codes|ja|三代格式|12=draft}} ''' {{ill|Kōnin Kyakushiki|ja|弘仁格式|lt=Kōnin Codes (Kōnin Kyaku, Kōnin Shiki)|12=draft}} ''' {{ill|Jōgan Kyakushiki|ja|貞観格式|lt=Jōgan Codes (Jōgan Kyaku, Jōgan Shiki)|12=draft}} ''' {{ill|Engi Kyakushiki|ja|延喜格式|lt=Engi Codes (Engi Kyaku, Engi Shiki)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kōtai-shiki|ja|交替式|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hachiman Usa-gū Gotakusenshū|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|12=draft}} * {{ill|Myōhō-dō|ja|明法道|12=draft}}－{{ill|Myōhō Hakase|ja|明法博士|12=draft}} * {{ill|Imperial Examination|ja|科挙|12=draft}}

revision.text:
'''類聚三代格'''（るいじゅうさんだいきゃく）は、[[平安時代]]に編纂された法令集。編者は不明<ref name="Nipponica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref>。
== 概説 == 三代格（[[弘仁格]]、[[貞観格]]、[[延喜格]]）を内容別に「神社事」・「国分寺事」・「庸調事」などと類聚（ジャンル分け）してまとめたもので<ref name="Nipponica" /><ref name="Sekaidaihyakka">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第2版|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref>、先行の格に収録されているものは再録していない<ref name="Britannica" />。成立時期は『[[日本大百科全書]]』によれば[[長保]]4年（[[1002年]]）と[[寛治]]3年（[[1089年]]）の間とされる<ref name="Nipponica" />。三代格では官司別にまとめており、法典として利用するとき不便である上、[[平安時代]]中期に[[有職故実]]化の傾向があったため、典例整備事業の一環として成立したとされる<ref name="Nipponica" />。
完本が現存せず、不完全な写本が伝わっているのみである<ref name="Mypedia">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=百科事典マイペディア|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref>。元となる弘仁格、貞観格、延喜格はそれぞれ10巻、12巻、12巻構成であり<ref name="Britannica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref>、『類聚三代格』は15巻<ref name="Britannica" />もしくは17巻<ref name="Obunsha">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=旺文社日本史事典 三訂版|access-date=2023年4月17日}}</ref>が現存するとされる。『類聚三代格』の写本は12巻本と20巻本が現存し、『[[本朝書籍目録]]』では全30巻としているものの<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" />、完本の巻数は20巻<ref name="Obunsha" />、30巻<ref name="Mypedia" />など諸説ありで、『ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典』では全30巻の根拠が薄いとしている<ref name="Britannica" />。また、現存する12巻本と20巻本の写本を合わせても欠落がある<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" />。
三代の格は弘仁格の目録を除いては現存しておらず、格の原文が含まれる『類聚三代格』は、当時の古代法制の実態を知るために貴重な史料である<ref name="Britannica" />。
==12巻本系統の構成== *第一巻 - 神社に関する祭幣・託宣・神主・祓い *第二巻 - 造仏・法会・僧職任命 *第三巻 - 国分寺・僧の位階・僧の禁忌・地方の講師 *第四巻 - 中央官司の廃置・定員 *第五巻 - 諸国官僚の廃置・位階 *第六巻 - 官僚の給与体系 *第七巻 - 公卿の意見、地方役人の取り締まり *第八巻 - 農業奨励・租税・金銭貸借 *第九巻 - 班田・公営田・墾田・山川藪沢 *第十巻 - 天皇・宮中の食膳・恩赦・文書・印 *第十一巻 - 軍隊・徴兵・相撲・駅・材木 *第十二巻 - 各種の禁令・刑罰
== 20巻本系統の構成 == *第一巻 - 序亊、神社事、[[神封|神封物]]幷租地子事、祭幷幣事、[[神位|神叙位]]幷[[託宣]]亊、[[斎王|齋王]]亊、[[科祓]]事 *第二巻 佛事上- 造佛々名亊、經論幷法會請僧亊、修法[[灌頂]]事、年分度者事 *第三巻 佛事下 - [[国分寺|國分寺]]亊、[[定額寺]]亊、[[僧綱|僧綱員]]位階幷僧位階亊、諸国[[講讀師]]亊、僧尼禁忌亊 *第四巻 - 癈置[[諸司]]亊、加減諸司官員幷癈置事[[雑任|雜任]] *第五巻 - 分置諸國事、加減諸國官員幷癈置事雜任附出、定官員幷官位亊、定内外五位等級亊、交替幷[[解由]]亊 *第六巻 - [[位祿]][[季禄|季祿]][[時服]][[馬料|馬䉼]]亊、[[要劇料|要劇月䉼]]亊、[[公廨]]亊、[[事力]]幷交替丁亊、[[公粮]]亊、[[賻物]]亊 *第七巻 - 公卿意見亊、[[牧宰]]亊、[[郡司]]亊 *第八巻 - 農桑<ref>{{Cite kotobank |word=農桑 |access-date=2024-03-27}}</ref>亊、調庸亊、[[封戸]]亊、[[不動穀|不動]][[動用倉|動用]]亊 *第九巻 - *第十巻 - 釋尊亊、國忌亊、供御亊 *第十一巻 - *第十二巻 - 諸使幷[[公文]]亊、[[隠首]][[括出]][[浪人]]亊、[[正倉]][[官倉]]亊 *第十三巻 - *第十四巻 - [[出挙|出舉]]亊、借貸亊、[[雑米|雜米]]亊、[[義倉]]亊、[[塡納]]亊、[[鋳銭司|'''鑄'''銭]]亊 *第十五巻 - [[挍班田]]亊、[[損田]]幷租[[地子]]亊、[[易田]]幷[[公営田|公營田]]亊、[[墾田]]幷[[佃]]亊、[[寺田]]亊、[[諸司田]]亊、[[軄田]][[位田]][[公廨田]]亊 *第十六巻 - [[閑癈地]]亊、[[道槁]]亊、[[船瀬]]幷浮槁[[布施屋]]亊、山野薮澤江河池沼亊、堤堰溝渠亊 *第十七巻 - [[國諱]][[追号|追號]]幷[[改姓|改姓名]]亊、[[蠲免]]<ref>{{Cite kotobank |word=蠲免 |encyclopedia=中日辞典 第3版 |access-date=2024-03-27}}</ref>亊、[[赦除]]<ref>{{Cite kotobank |word=赦除 |encyclopedia=普及版 字通 |access-date=2024-03-27}}</ref>亊、[[募賞]]亊、文書幷印亊 *第十八巻 - [[軍毅]]兵士[[鎮兵|鎭兵]]亊、[[統領]][[選士]][[衛卒]][[衛士]][[仕丁]]亊、[[健児|健兒]]亊、[[器仗]]<ref>{{Cite kotobank |word=器仗 |access-date=2024-03-27}}</ref>亊、[[關]]幷[[烽候]]<ref>{{Cite kotobank |word=烽候 |access-date=2024-03-27}}</ref>亊、[[夷俘]]幷[[外蕃人]]亊、[[相撲#歴史|相撲]]亊、國飼幷牧馬牛亊、[[駅伝制#日本|駅傳]]亊、材木亊 *第十九巻 - 禁制亊 *第二十巻 - 断罪[[贖銅]]亊
== 出典 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=国史大系 第12巻 |year=1900 |publisher=経済雑誌社 |chapter=類聚三代格 |volume= |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991102/1/1 |doi=10.11501/991102}} * {{Cite book|和書 |title=国史大系 第25巻 新訂増補 |year=1936 |publisher=国史大系刊行会 |series= |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3431640/1/4 |doi=10.11501/3431640 |chapter=類聚三代格}}
== 関連項目 == *[[類聚国史]]
== 外部リンク == *[https://codh.rois.ac.jp/pmjt/book/200018252/ 類聚三代格 - 日本古典籍データセット]
{{デフォルトソート:るいしゆうさんたいきやく}} [[Category:平安時代の書籍]] [[Category:日本の格式]] [[Category:法令集]]

三代の格は弘仁格の目録を除いては現存しておらず、格の原文が含まれる『類聚三代格』は、当時の古代法制の実態を知るために貴重な史料である<ref name="Britannica" />。
==12巻本系統の構成== *第一巻 - 神社に関する祭幣・託宣・神主・祓い *第二巻 - 造仏・法会・僧職任命 *第三巻 - 国分寺・僧の位階・僧の禁忌・地方の講師 *第四巻 - 中央官司の廃置・定員 *第五巻 - 諸国官僚の廃置・位階 *第六巻 - 官僚の給与体系 *第七巻 - 公卿の意見、地方役人の取り締まり *第八巻 - 農業奨励・租税・金銭貸借 *第九巻 - 班田・公営田・墾田・山川藪沢 *第十巻 - 天皇・宮中の食膳・恩赦・文書・印 *第十一巻 - 軍隊・徴兵・相撲・駅・材木 *第十二巻 - 各種の禁令・刑罰
== 20巻本系統の構成 == *第一巻 - 序亊、神社事、[[神封|神封物]]幷租地子事、祭幷幣事、[[神位|神叙位]]幷[[託宣]]亊、[[斎王|齋王]]亊、[[科祓]]事 *第二巻 佛事上- 造佛々名亊、經論幷法會請僧亊、修法[[灌頂]]事、年分度者事 *第三巻 佛事下 - [[国分寺|國分寺]]亊、[[定額寺]]亊、[[僧綱|僧綱員]]位階幷僧位階亊、諸国[[講讀師]]亊、僧尼禁忌亊 *第四巻 - 癈置[[諸司]]亊、加減諸司官員幷癈置事[[雑任|雜任]] *第五巻 - 分置諸國事、加減諸國官員幷癈置事雜任附出、定官員幷官位亊、定内外五位等級亊、交替幷[[解由]]亊 *第六巻 - [[位祿]][[季禄|季祿]][[時服]][[馬料|馬䉼]]亊、[[要劇料|要劇月䉼]]亊、[[公廨]]亊、[[事力]]幷交替丁亊、[[公粮]]亊、[[賻物]]亊 *第七巻 - 公卿意見亊、[[牧宰]]亊、[[郡司]]亊 *第八巻 - 農桑<ref>{{Cite kotobank |word=農桑 |access-date=2024-03-27}}</ref>亊、調庸亊、[[封戸]]亊、[[不動穀|不動]][[動用倉|動用]]亊 *第九巻 - *第十巻 - 釋尊亊、國忌亊、供御亊 *第十一巻 - *第十二巻 - 諸使幷[[公文]]亊、[[隠首]][[括出]][[浪人]]亊、[[正倉]][[官倉]]亊 *第十三巻 - *第十四巻 - [[出挙|出舉]]亊、借貸亊、[[雑米|雜米]]亊、[[義倉]]亊、[[塡納]]亊、[[鋳銭司|'''鑄'''銭]]亊 *第十五巻 - [[挍班田]]亊、[[損田]]幷租[[地子]]亊、[[易田]]幷[[公営田|公營田]]亊、[[墾田]]幷[[佃]]亊、[[寺田]]亊、[[諸司田]]亊、[[軄田]][[位田]][[公廨田]]亊 *第十六巻 - [[閑癈地]]亊、[[道槁]]亊、[[船瀬]]幷浮槁[[布施屋]]亊、山野薮澤江河池沼亊、堤堰溝渠亊 *第十七巻 - [[國諱]][[追号|追號]]幷[[改姓|改姓名]]亊、[[蠲免]]<ref>{{Cite kotobank |word=蠲免 |encyclopedia=中日辞典 第3版 |access-date=2024-03-27}}</ref>亊、[[赦除]]<ref>{{Cite kotobank |word=赦除 |encyclopedia=普及版 字通 |access-date=2024-03-27}}</ref>亊、[[募賞]]亊、文書幷印亊 *第十八巻 - [[軍毅]]兵士[[鎮兵|鎭兵]]亊、[[統領]][[選士]][[衛卒]][[衛士]][[仕丁]]亊、[[健児|健兒]]亊、[[器仗]]<ref>{{Cite kotobank |word=器仗 |access-date=2024-03-27}}</ref>亊、[[關]]幷[[烽候]]<ref>{{Cite kotobank |word=烽候 |access-date=2024-03-27}}</ref>亊、[[夷俘]]幷[[外蕃人]]亊、[[相撲#歴史|相撲]]亊、國飼幷牧馬牛亊、[[駅伝制#日本|駅傳]]亊、材木亊 *第十九巻 - 禁制亊 *第二十巻 - 断罪[[贖銅]]亊
== 出典 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=国史大系 第12巻 |year=1900 |publisher=経済雑誌社 |chapter=類聚三代格 |volume= |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991102/1/1 |doi=10.11501/991102}} * {{Cite book|和書 |title=国史大系 第25巻 新訂増補 |year=1936 |publisher=国史大系刊行会 |series= |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3431640/1/4 |doi=10.11501/3431640 |chapter=類聚三代格}}
== 関連項目 == *[[類聚国史]]
== 外部リンク == *[https://codh.rois.ac.jp/pmjt/book/200018252/ 類聚三代格 - 日本古典籍データセット]

revision.text:
'''Ruijū Sandai Kyaku''' (類聚三代格) is a compilation of legal codes from the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. The compiler is unknown.<ref name="Nipponica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref>
== Overview == The *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* organizes the legal codes of the {{ill|Kōnin Kyaku|ja|弘仁格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnin Kyaku|qq=}}, {{ill|Jōgan Kyaku|ja|貞観格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan Kyaku|qq=}}, and {{ill|Engi Kyaku|ja|延喜格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Kyaku|qq=}} thematically into categories such as "Shinto Shrine Affairs," "Provincial Temple Affairs," and "Tax Matters."<ref name="Nipponica" /><ref name="Sekaidaihyakka">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第2版|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> It does not re-record items already included in previous compilations.<ref name="Britannica" /> The compilation date is estimated to be between [[:en:Chōhō (era)|Chōhō 4]] (1002) and [[:en:Kanji (era)|Kanjī 3]] (1089) according to the *Nipponica* encyclopedia.<ref name="Nipponica" />
The three-generation codes were originally organized by government office, which was inconvenient for legal reference, especially during the mid-Heian period when there was a growing trend towards the study of {{ill|Yūsoku kojitsu|ja|有職故実|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūsoku kojitsu|qq=}} (ancient court practices). Thus, the *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* was compiled as part of legal codification efforts.<ref name="Nipponica" />
No complete manuscript survives; only partial copies are extant.<ref name="Mypedia">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=百科事典マイペディア|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> The original codes—*Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku*—comprised 10, 12, and 12 volumes respectively.<ref name="Britannica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> Existing manuscripts of *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* are said to contain 15<ref name="Britannica" /> or 17 volumes,<ref name="Obunsha">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=旺文社日本史事典 三訂版|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> with versions ranging from 12 to 20 volumes. The *Honchō Shojaku Mokuroku* lists it as having 30 volumes,<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" /> though scholars debate whether the full compilation indeed contained 20<ref name="Obunsha" /> or 30 volumes,<ref name="Mypedia" /> with *Britannica* casting doubt on the 30-volume theory.<ref name="Britannica" /> Even combining the surviving 12- and 20-volume manuscripts, gaps remain.<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" />
Since the original texts of the *Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku* are mostly lost (except for the *Kōnin Kyaku* index), *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku*—which preserves their contents—is an invaluable resource for understanding ancient Japanese legal systems.<ref name="Britannica" />
== Structure of the 12-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Shinto shrine rituals, divine oracles, shrine priests, purification * Volume 2 - Buddha images, Buddhist ceremonies, appointment of clergy * Volume 3 - Provincial temples, clerical Ranks, monastic prohibitions, regional lecturers * Volume 4 - Central government office reorganizations and staffing * Volume 5 - Provincial bureaucracy and Ranks * Volume 6 - Bureaucratic salary systems * Volume 7 - Opinions of court nobles, oversight of local officials * Volume 8 - Agricultural promotion, taxation, monetary lending * Volume 9 - Land allotments, state-managed fields, land reclamation, mountains, rivers, and wetlands * Volume 10 - Imperial household cuisine, amnesties, documents, seals * Volume 11 - Military affairs, conscription, sumo, postal stations, timber * Volume 12 - Various prohibitions and punishments
== Structure of the 20-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Preface, shrine affairs, divine properties and land taxes, rituals and offerings, divine Rankings, oracles, Saiō matters, purification matters * Volume 2 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 1): Creation of Buddha images, sutra copying, Buddhist ceremonies, tantric initiations, state-sponsored monastics * Volume 3 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 2): Provincial temples, established temples, monastic Ranks, provincial lecturers, monastic prohibitions * Volume 4 - Government office reforms, staff adjustments, miscellaneous appointments * Volume 5 - Provincial governance, staffing adjustments, official Ranks, administrative transitions * Volume 6 - Salaries, stipends, official attire, public resources, labor duties, aid distribution * Volume 7 - Court noble opinions, livestock management, district officials * Volume 8 - Agriculture and sericulture, taxation, household registries, grain storage, economic regulations * Volume 9 - (Content missing) * Volume 10 - Imperial affairs, national mourning, court cuisine * Volume 11 - (Content missing) * Volume 12 - Official missions, legal documents, fugitives, state warehouses * Volume 13 - (Content missing) * Volume 14 - Loans, financial transactions, miscellaneous grains, relief granaries, land management, coin minting * Volume 15 - Land distribution, tax land, state-owned fields, cultivated land, temple lands, official lands * Volume 16 - Abandoned lands, roads, ports, wilderness management, riverbanks, irrigation * Volume 17 - Name changes, amnesties, pardons, reward systems, document management * Volume 18 - Military affairs, conscription, defense units, weapons, border controls, foreign relations, sumo, livestock, postal systems, timber management * Volume 19 - Prohibitions and restrictions * Volume 20 - Criminal judgments and fines
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Sources == * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第12巻 (National Historical Series, Vol. 12) |year=1900 |publisher=経済雑誌社 |chapter=類聚三代格 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991102/1/1 |doi=10.11501/991102}} * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第25巻 新訂増補 (National Historical Series, Vol. 25) |year=1936 |publisher=国史大系刊行会 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3431640/1/4 |doi=10.11501/3431640 |chapter=類聚三代格}} == See Also == * [[:en:Ruijū Kokushi|Ruijū Kokushi]]
== External Links == * [http://codh.rois.ac.jp/pmjt/book/200018252/ *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* - Japanese Classical Text Database]
== See Also from {{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|ja|格式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyaku and Shiki|qq=}} == {{Wiktionary}} * [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|Ritsuryō]]－{{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Ritsuryō law|ja|律令法|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō law|qq=}}－[[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] * {{ill|Three Great Legal Codes|ja|三代格式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Legal Codes|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Kōnin Kyakushiki|ja|弘仁格式|lt=Kōnin Codes (Kōnin Kyaku, Kōnin Shiki)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnin Kyakushiki|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Jōgan Kyakushiki|ja|貞観格式|lt=Jōgan Codes (Jōgan Kyaku, Jōgan Shiki)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan Kyakushiki|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Engi Kyakushiki|ja|延喜格式|lt=Engi Codes (Engi Kyaku, Engi Shiki)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Kyakushiki|qq=}} * {{ill|Kōtai-shiki|ja|交替式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai-shiki|qq=}} * {{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}} * {{ill|Hachiman Usa-gū Gotakusenshū|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Usa-gū Gotakusenshū|qq=}} * {{ill|Myōhō-dō|ja|明法道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōhō-dō|qq=}}－{{ill|Myōhō Hakase|ja|明法博士|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōhō Hakase|qq=}} * [[:en:Imperial examination|Imperial Examination]]
* [[Abe no Seimei]] * [[Da Liu Ren]] * [[Goryō]] * [[Itako]] * [[Kuji-in]] * [[Onmyōdō]] * [[Sanpaku]] * [[Seimei Shrine]] * [[Senji Ryakketsu]] * [[Shikigami]] * [[Shinigami]] * [[Tengenjutsu (fortune telling)]] * [[Ushi no toki mairi]]
{{translated page|ja|類聚三代格|version=104526082|comment=Imported full ja history}}

}}

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:日本の格式
revision.text:
'''Ruijū Sandai Kyaku''' (類聚三代格) is a compilation of legal codes from the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. The compiler is unknown.<ref name="Nipponica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref>
== Overview == The *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* organizes the legal codes of the {{ill|Kōnin Kyaku|ja|弘仁格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnin Kyaku|qq=}}, {{ill|Jōgan Kyaku|ja|貞観格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan Kyaku|qq=}}, and {{ill|Engi Kyaku|ja|延喜格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Kyaku|qq=}} thematically into categories such as "Shinto Shrine Affairs," "Provincial Temple Affairs," and "Tax Matters."<ref name="Nipponica" /><ref name="Sekaidaihyakka">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第2版|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> It does not re-record items already included in previous compilations.<ref name="Britannica" /> The compilation date is estimated to be between [[:en:Chōhō (era)|Chōhō 4]] (1002) and [[:en:Kanji (era)|Kanjī 3]] (1089) according to the *Nipponica* encyclopedia.<ref name="Nipponica" />
The three-generation codes were originally organized by government office, which was inconvenient for legal reference, especially during the mid-Heian period when there was a growing trend towards the study of {{ill|Yūsoku kojitsu|ja|有職故実|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūsoku kojitsu|qq=}} (ancient court practices). Thus, the *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* was compiled as part of legal codification efforts.<ref name="Nipponica" />
No complete manuscript survives; only partial copies are extant.<ref name="Mypedia">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=百科事典マイペディア|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> The original codes—*Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku*—comprised 10, 12, and 12 volumes respectively.<ref name="Britannica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> Existing manuscripts of *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* are said to contain 15<ref name="Britannica" /> or 17 volumes,<ref name="Obunsha">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=旺文社日本史事典 三訂版|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> with versions ranging from 12 to 20 volumes. The *Honchō Shojaku Mokuroku* lists it as having 30 volumes,<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" /> though scholars debate whether the full compilation indeed contained 20<ref name="Obunsha" /> or 30 volumes,<ref name="Mypedia" /> with *Britannica* casting doubt on the 30-volume theory.<ref name="Britannica" /> Even combining the surviving 12- and 20-volume manuscripts, gaps remain.<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" />
Since the original texts of the *Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku* are mostly lost (except for the *Kōnin Kyaku* index), *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku*—which preserves their contents—is an invaluable resource for understanding ancient Japanese legal systems.<ref name="Britannica" />
== Structure of the 12-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Shinto shrine rituals, divine oracles, shrine priests, purification * Volume 2 - Buddha images, Buddhist ceremonies, appointment of clergy * Volume 3 - Provincial temples, clerical Ranks, monastic prohibitions, regional lecturers * Volume 4 - Central government office reorganizations and staffing * Volume 5 - Provincial bureaucracy and Ranks * Volume 6 - Bureaucratic salary systems * Volume 7 - Opinions of court nobles, oversight of local officials * Volume 8 - Agricultural promotion, taxation, monetary lending * Volume 9 - Land allotments, state-managed fields, land reclamation, mountains, rivers, and wetlands * Volume 10 - Imperial household cuisine, amnesties, documents, seals * Volume 11 - Military affairs, conscription, sumo, postal stations, timber * Volume 12 - Various prohibitions and punishments
== Structure of the 20-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Preface, shrine affairs, divine properties and land taxes, rituals and offerings, divine Rankings, oracles, Saiō matters, purification matters * Volume 2 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 1): Creation of Buddha images, sutra copying, Buddhist ceremonies, tantric initiations, state-sponsored monastics * Volume 3 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 2): Provincial temples, established temples, monastic Ranks, provincial lecturers, monastic prohibitions * Volume 4 - Government office reforms, staff adjustments, miscellaneous appointments * Volume 5 - Provincial governance, staffing adjustments, official Ranks, administrative transitions * Volume 6 - Salaries, stipends, official attire, public resources, labor duties, aid distribution * Volume 7 - Court noble opinions, livestock management, district officials * Volume 8 - Agriculture and sericulture, taxation, household registries, grain storage, economic regulations * Volume 9 - (Content missing) * Volume 10 - Imperial affairs, national mourning, court cuisine * Volume 11 - (Content missing) * Volume 12 - Official missions, legal documents, fugitives, state warehouses * Volume 13 - (Content missing) * Volume 14 - Loans, financial transactions, miscellaneous grains, relief granaries, land management, coin minting * Volume 15 - Land distribution, tax land, state-owned fields, cultivated land, temple lands, official lands * Volume 16 - Abandoned lands, roads, ports, wilderness management, riverbanks, irrigation * Volume 17 - Name changes, amnesties, pardons, reward systems, document management * Volume 18 - Military affairs, conscription, defense units, weapons, border controls, foreign relations, sumo, livestock, postal systems, timber management * Volume 19 - Prohibitions and restrictions * Volume 20 - Criminal judgments and fines
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Sources == * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第12巻 (National Historical Series, Vol. 12) |year=1900 |publisher=経済雑誌社 |chapter=類聚三代格 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991102/1/1 |doi=10.11501/991102}} * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第25巻 新訂増補 (National Historical Series, Vol. 25) |year=1936 |publisher=国史大系刊行会 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3431640/1/4 |doi=10.11501/3431640 |chapter=類聚三代格}} == See Also == * [[:en:Ruijū Kokushi|Ruijū Kokushi]]
== External Links == * [http://codh.rois.ac.jp/pmjt/book/200018252/ *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* - Japanese Classical Text Database]
== See Also from {{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|ja|格式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyaku and Shiki|qq=}} == {{Wiktionary}} * [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|Ritsuryō]]－{{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Ritsuryō law|ja|律令法|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō law|qq=}}－[[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] * {{ill|Three Great Legal Codes|ja|三代格式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Legal Codes|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Kōnin Kyakushiki|ja|弘仁格式|lt=Kōnin Codes (Kōnin Kyaku, Kōnin Shiki)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnin Kyakushiki|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Jōgan Kyakushiki|ja|貞観格式|lt=Jōgan Codes (Jōgan Kyaku, Jōgan Shiki)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan Kyakushiki|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Engi Kyakushiki|ja|延喜格式|lt=Engi Codes (Engi Kyaku, Engi Shiki)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Kyakushiki|qq=}} * {{ill|Kōtai-shiki|ja|交替式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai-shiki|qq=}} * {{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}} * {{ill|Hachiman Usa-gū Gotakusenshū|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Usa-gū Gotakusenshū|qq=}} * {{ill|Myōhō-dō|ja|明法道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōhō-dō|qq=}}－{{ill|Myōhō Hakase|ja|明法博士|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōhō Hakase|qq=}} * [[:en:Imperial examination|Imperial Examination]]
* [[Abe no Seimei]] * [[Da Liu Ren]] * [[Goryō]] * [[Itako]] * [[Kuji-in]] * [[Onmyōdō]] * [[Sanpaku]] * [[Seimei Shrine]] * [[Senji Ryakketsu]] * [[Shikigami]] * [[Shinigami]] * [[Tengenjutsu (fortune telling)]] * [[Ushi no toki mairi]]
{{translated page|ja|類聚三代格|version=104526082|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.comment: Bot: ill fix final-12
revision.text:
'''Ruijū Sandai Kyaku''' (類聚三代格) is a compilation of legal codes from the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. The compiler is unknown.<ref name="Nipponica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref>
== Overview == The *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* organizes the legal codes of the {{ill|Kōnin Kyaku|ja|弘仁格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnin Kyaku|qq=}}, {{ill|Jōgan Kyaku|ja|貞観格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan Kyaku|qq=}}, and {{ill|Engi Kyaku|ja|延喜格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Kyaku|qq=}} thematically into categories such as "Shinto Shrine Affairs," "Provincial Temple Affairs," and "Tax Matters."<ref name="Nipponica" /><ref name="Sekaidaihyakka">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第2版|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> It does not re-record items already included in previous compilations.<ref name="Britannica" /> The compilation date is estimated to be between [[:en:Chōhō (era)|Chōhō 4]] (1002) and [[:en:Kanji (era)|Kanjī 3]] (1089) according to the *Nipponica* encyclopedia.<ref name="Nipponica" />
The three-generation codes were originally organized by government office, which was inconvenient for legal reference, especially during the mid-Heian period when there was a growing trend towards the study of {{ill|Yūsoku kojitsu|ja|有職故実|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūsoku kojitsu|qq=}} (ancient court practices). Thus, the *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* was compiled as part of legal codification efforts.<ref name="Nipponica" />
No complete manuscript survives; only partial copies are extant.<ref name="Mypedia">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=百科事典マイペディア|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> The original codes—*Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku*—comprised 10, 12, and 12 volumes respectively.<ref name="Britannica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> Existing manuscripts of *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* are said to contain 15<ref name="Britannica" /> or 17 volumes,<ref name="Obunsha">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=旺文社日本史事典 三訂版|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> with versions ranging from 12 to 20 volumes. The *Honchō Shojaku Mokuroku* lists it as having 30 volumes,<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" /> though scholars debate whether the full compilation indeed contained 20<ref name="Obunsha" /> or 30 volumes,<ref name="Mypedia" /> with *Britannica* casting doubt on the 30-volume theory.<ref name="Britannica" /> Even combining the surviving 12- and 20-volume manuscripts, gaps remain.<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" />
Since the original texts of the *Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku* are mostly lost (except for the *Kōnin Kyaku* index), *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku*—which preserves their contents—is an invaluable resource for understanding ancient Japanese legal systems.<ref name="Britannica" />
== Structure of the 12-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Shinto shrine rituals, divine oracles, shrine priests, purification * Volume 2 - Buddha images, Buddhist ceremonies, appointment of clergy * Volume 3 - Provincial temples, clerical Ranks, monastic prohibitions, regional lecturers * Volume 4 - Central government office reorganizations and staffing * Volume 5 - Provincial bureaucracy and Ranks * Volume 6 - Bureaucratic salary systems * Volume 7 - Opinions of court nobles, oversight of local officials * Volume 8 - Agricultural promotion, taxation, monetary lending * Volume 9 - Land allotments, state-managed fields, land reclamation, mountains, rivers, and wetlands * Volume 10 - Imperial household cuisine, amnesties, documents, seals * Volume 11 - Military affairs, conscription, sumo, postal stations, timber * Volume 12 - Various prohibitions and punishments
== Structure of the 20-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Preface, shrine affairs, divine properties and land taxes, rituals and offerings, divine Rankings, oracles, Saiō matters, purification matters * Volume 2 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 1): Creation of Buddha images, sutra copying, Buddhist ceremonies, tantric initiations, state-sponsored monastics * Volume 3 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 2): Provincial temples, established temples, monastic Ranks, provincial lecturers, monastic prohibitions * Volume 4 - Government office reforms, staff adjustments, miscellaneous appointments * Volume 5 - Provincial governance, staffing adjustments, official Ranks, administrative transitions * Volume 6 - Salaries, stipends, official attire, public resources, labor duties, aid distribution * Volume 7 - Court noble opinions, livestock management, district officials * Volume 8 - Agriculture and sericulture, taxation, household registries, grain storage, economic regulations * Volume 9 - (Content missing) * Volume 10 - Imperial affairs, national mourning, court cuisine * Volume 11 - (Content missing) * Volume 12 - Official missions, legal documents, fugitives, state warehouses * Volume 13 - (Content missing) * Volume 14 - Loans, financial transactions, miscellaneous grains, relief granaries, land management, coin minting * Volume 15 - Land distribution, tax land, state-owned fields, cultivated land, temple lands, official lands * Volume 16 - Abandoned lands, roads, ports, wilderness management, riverbanks, irrigation * Volume 17 - Name changes, amnesties, pardons, reward systems, document management * Volume 18 - Military affairs, conscription, defense units, weapons, border controls, foreign relations, sumo, livestock, postal systems, timber management * Volume 19 - Prohibitions and restrictions * Volume 20 - Criminal judgments and fines
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Sources == * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第12巻 (National Historical Series, Vol. 12) |year=1900 |publisher=経済雑誌社 |chapter=類聚三代格 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991102/1/1 |doi=10.11501/991102}} * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第25巻 新訂増補 (National Historical Series, Vol. 25) |year=1936 |publisher=国史大系刊行会 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3431640/1/4 |doi=10.11501/3431640 |chapter=類聚三代格}} == See Also == * [[:en:Ruijū Kokushi|Ruijū Kokushi]]
== External Links == * [http://codh.rois.ac.jp/pmjt/book/200018252/ *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* - Japanese Classical Text Database]
== See Also from {{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|ja|格式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyaku and Shiki|qq=}} == {{Wiktionary}} * [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|Ritsuryō]]－{{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Ritsuryō law|ja|律令法|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō law|qq=}}－[[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] * {{ill|Three Great Legal Codes|ja|三代格式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Legal Codes|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Kōnin Kyakushiki|ja|弘仁格式|lt=Kōnin Codes (Kōnin Kyaku, Kōnin Shiki)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnin Kyakushiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三代格式}} ''' {{ill|Jōgan Kyakushiki|ja|貞観格式|lt=Jōgan Codes (Jōgan Kyaku, Jōgan Shiki)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan Kyakushiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三代格式}} ''' {{ill|Engi Kyakushiki|ja|延喜格式|lt=Engi Codes (Engi Kyaku, Engi Shiki)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Kyakushiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三代格式}} * {{ill|Kōtai-shiki|ja|交替式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai-shiki|qq=}} * {{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}} * {{ill|Hachiman Usa-gū Gotakusenshū|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Usa-gū Gotakusenshū|qq=}} * {{ill|Myōhō-dō|ja|明法道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōhō-dō|qq=}}－{{ill|Myōhō Hakase|ja|明法博士|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōhō Hakase|qq=}} * [[:en:Imperial examination|Imperial Examination]]
* [[Abe no Seimei]] * [[Da Liu Ren]] * [[Goryō]] * [[Itako]] * [[Kuji-in]] * [[Onmyōdō]] * [[Sanpaku]] * [[Seimei Shrine]] * [[Senji Ryakketsu]] * [[Shikigami]] * [[Shinigami]] * [[Tengenjutsu (fortune telling)]] * [[Ushi no toki mairi]]

The *Honchō Shojaku Mokuroku* lists it as having 30 volumes,<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" /> though scholars debate whether the full compilation indeed contained 20<ref name="Obunsha" /> or 30 volumes,<ref name="Mypedia" /> with *Britannica* casting doubt on the 30-volume theory.<ref name="Britannica" /> Even combining the surviving 12- and 20-volume manuscripts, gaps remain.<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" />
Since the original texts of the *Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku* are mostly lost (except for the *Kōnin Kyaku* index), *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku*—which preserves their contents—is an invaluable resource for understanding ancient Japanese legal systems.<ref name="Britannica" />
== Structure of the 12-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Shinto shrine rituals, divine oracles, shrine priests, purification * Volume 2 - Buddha images, Buddhist ceremonies, appointment of clergy * Volume 3 - Provincial temples, clerical Ranks, monastic prohibitions, regional lecturers * Volume 4 - Central government office reorganizations and staffing * Volume 5 - Provincial bureaucracy and Ranks * Volume 6 - Bureaucratic salary systems * Volume 7 - Opinions of court nobles, oversight of local officials * Volume 8 - Agricultural promotion, taxation, monetary lending * Volume 9 - Land allotments, state-managed fields, land reclamation, mountains, rivers, and wetlands * Volume 10 - Imperial household cuisine, amnesties, documents, seals * Volume 11 - Military affairs, conscription, sumo, postal stations, timber * Volume 12 - Various prohibitions and punishments
== Structure of the 20-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Preface, shrine affairs, divine properties and land taxes, rituals and offerings, divine Rankings, oracles, Saiō matters, purification matters * Volume 2 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 1): Creation of Buddha images, sutra copying, Buddhist ceremonies, tantric initiations, state-sponsored monastics * Volume 3 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 2): Provincial temples, established temples, monastic Ranks, provincial lecturers, monastic prohibitions * Volume 4 - Government office reforms, staff adjustments, miscellaneous appointments * Volume 5 - Provincial governance, staffing adjustments, official Ranks, administrative transitions * Volume 6 - Salaries, stipends, official attire, public resources, labor duties, aid distribution * Volume 7 - Court noble opinions, livestock management, district officials * Volume 8 - Agriculture and sericulture, taxation, household registries, grain storage, economic regulations * Volume 9 - (Content missing) * Volume 10 - Imperial affairs, national mourning, court cuisine * Volume 11 - (Content missing) * Volume 12 - Official missions, legal documents, fugitives, state warehouses * Volume 13 - (Content missing) * Volume 14 - Loans, financial transactions, miscellaneous grains, relief granaries, land management, coin minting * Volume 15 - Land distribution, tax land, state-owned fields, cultivated land, temple lands, official lands * Volume 16 - Abandoned lands, roads, ports, wilderness management, riverbanks, irrigation * Volume 17 - Name changes, amnesties, pardons, reward systems, document management * Volume 18 - Military affairs, conscription, defense units, weapons, border controls, foreign relations, sumo, livestock, postal systems, timber management * Volume 19 - Prohibitions and restrictions * Volume 20 - Criminal judgments and fines

revision.text:
'''Ruijū Sandai Kyaku''' (類聚三代格) is a compilation of legal codes from the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. The compiler is unknown.<ref name="Nipponica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref>
== Overview == The *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* organizes the legal codes of the {{ill|Kōnin Kyaku|ja|弘仁格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnin Kyaku|qq=}}, {{ill|Jōgan Kyaku|ja|貞観格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan Kyaku|qq=}}, and {{ill|Engi Kyaku|ja|延喜格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Kyaku|qq=}} thematically into categories such as "Shinto Shrine Affairs," "Provincial Temple Affairs," and "Tax Matters."<ref name="Nipponica" /><ref name="Sekaidaihyakka">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第2版|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> It does not re-record items already included in previous compilations.<ref name="Britannica" /> The compilation date is estimated to be between [[:en:Chōhō (era)|Chōhō 4]] (1002) and [[:en:Kanji (era)|Kanjī 3]] (1089) according to the *Nipponica* encyclopedia.<ref name="Nipponica" />
The three-generation codes were originally organized by government office, which was inconvenient for legal reference, especially during the mid-Heian period when there was a growing trend towards the study of {{ill|Yūsoku kojitsu|ja|有職故実|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūsoku kojitsu|qq=}} (ancient court practices). Thus, the *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* was compiled as part of legal codification efforts.<ref name="Nipponica" />
No complete manuscript survives; only partial copies are extant.<ref name="Mypedia">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=百科事典マイペディア|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> The original codes—*Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku*—comprised 10, 12, and 12 volumes respectively.<ref name="Britannica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> Existing manuscripts of *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* are said to contain 15<ref name="Britannica" /> or 17 volumes,<ref name="Obunsha">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=旺文社日本史事典 三訂版|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> with versions ranging from 12 to 20 volumes. The *Honchō Shojaku Mokuroku* lists it as having 30 volumes,<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" /> though scholars debate whether the full compilation indeed contained 20<ref name="Obunsha" /> or 30 volumes,<ref name="Mypedia" /> with *Britannica* casting doubt on the 30-volume theory.<ref name="Britannica" /> Even combining the surviving 12- and 20-volume manuscripts, gaps remain.<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" />
Since the original texts of the *Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku* are mostly lost (except for the *Kōnin Kyaku* index), *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku*—which preserves their contents—is an invaluable resource for understanding ancient Japanese legal systems.<ref name="Britannica" />
== Structure of the 12-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Shinto shrine rituals, divine oracles, shrine priests, purification * Volume 2 - Buddha images, Buddhist ceremonies, appointment of clergy * Volume 3 - Provincial temples, clerical Ranks, monastic prohibitions, regional lecturers * Volume 4 - Central government office reorganizations and staffing * Volume 5 - Provincial bureaucracy and Ranks * Volume 6 - Bureaucratic salary systems * Volume 7 - Opinions of court nobles, oversight of local officials * Volume 8 - Agricultural promotion, taxation, monetary lending * Volume 9 - Land allotments, state-managed fields, land reclamation, mountains, rivers, and wetlands * Volume 10 - Imperial household cuisine, amnesties, documents, seals * Volume 11 - Military affairs, conscription, sumo, postal stations, timber * Volume 12 - Various prohibitions and punishments
== Structure of the 20-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Preface, shrine affairs, divine properties and land taxes, rituals and offerings, divine Rankings, oracles, Saiō matters, purification matters * Volume 2 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 1): Creation of Buddha images, sutra copying, Buddhist ceremonies, tantric initiations, state-sponsored monastics * Volume 3 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 2): Provincial temples, established temples, monastic Ranks, provincial lecturers, monastic prohibitions * Volume 4 - Government office reforms, staff adjustments, miscellaneous appointments * Volume 5 - Provincial governance, staffing adjustments, official Ranks, administrative transitions * Volume 6 - Salaries, stipends, official attire, public resources, labor duties, aid distribution * Volume 7 - Court noble opinions, livestock management, district officials * Volume 8 - Agriculture and sericulture, taxation, household registries, grain storage, economic regulations * Volume 9 - (Content missing) * Volume 10 - Imperial affairs, national mourning, court cuisine * Volume 11 - (Content missing) * Volume 12 - Official missions, legal documents, fugitives, state warehouses * Volume 13 - (Content missing) * Volume 14 - Loans, financial transactions, miscellaneous grains, relief granaries, land management, coin minting * Volume 15 - Land distribution, tax land, state-owned fields, cultivated land, temple lands, official lands * Volume 16 - Abandoned lands, roads, ports, wilderness management, riverbanks, irrigation * Volume 17 - Name changes, amnesties, pardons, reward systems, document management * Volume 18 - Military affairs, conscription, defense units, weapons, border controls, foreign relations, sumo, livestock, postal systems, timber management * Volume 19 - Prohibitions and restrictions * Volume 20 - Criminal judgments and fines

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Japanese Legal Codes
revision.text:
'''Ruijū Sandai Kyaku''' (類聚三代格) is a compilation of legal codes from the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. The compiler is unknown.<ref name="Nipponica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref>
== Overview == The *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* organizes the legal codes of the {{ill|Kōnin Kyaku|ja|弘仁格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnin Kyaku|qq=}}, {{ill|Jōgan Kyaku|ja|貞観格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan Kyaku|qq=}}, and {{ill|Engi Kyaku|ja|延喜格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Kyaku|qq=}} thematically into categories such as "Shinto Shrine Affairs," "Provincial Temple Affairs," and "Tax Matters."<ref name="Nipponica" /><ref name="Sekaidaihyakka">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第2版|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> It does not re-record items already included in previous compilations.<ref name="Britannica" /> The compilation date is estimated to be between [[:en:Chōhō (era)|Chōhō 4]] (1002) and [[:en:Kanji (era)|Kanjī 3]] (1089) according to the *Nipponica* encyclopedia.<ref name="Nipponica" />
The three-generation codes were originally organized by government office, which was inconvenient for legal reference, especially during the mid-Heian period when there was a growing trend towards the study of {{ill|Yūsoku kojitsu|ja|有職故実|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūsoku kojitsu|qq=}} (ancient court practices). Thus, the *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* was compiled as part of legal codification efforts.<ref name="Nipponica" />
No complete manuscript survives; only partial copies are extant.<ref name="Mypedia">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=百科事典マイペディア|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> The original codes—*Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku*—comprised 10, 12, and 12 volumes respectively.<ref name="Britannica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> Existing manuscripts of *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* are said to contain 15<ref name="Britannica" /> or 17 volumes,<ref name="Obunsha">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=旺文社日本史事典 三訂版|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> with versions ranging from 12 to 20 volumes. The *Honchō Shojaku Mokuroku* lists it as having 30 volumes,<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" /> though scholars debate whether the full compilation indeed contained 20<ref name="Obunsha" /> or 30 volumes,<ref name="Mypedia" /> with *Britannica* casting doubt on the 30-volume theory.<ref name="Britannica" /> Even combining the surviving 12- and 20-volume manuscripts, gaps remain.<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" />
Since the original texts of the *Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku* are mostly lost (except for the *Kōnin Kyaku* index), *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku*—which preserves their contents—is an invaluable resource for understanding ancient Japanese legal systems.<ref name="Britannica" />
== Structure of the 12-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Shinto shrine rituals, divine oracles, shrine priests, purification * Volume 2 - Buddha images, Buddhist ceremonies, appointment of clergy * Volume 3 - Provincial temples, clerical Ranks, monastic prohibitions, regional lecturers * Volume 4 - Central government office reorganizations and staffing * Volume 5 - Provincial bureaucracy and Ranks * Volume 6 - Bureaucratic salary systems * Volume 7 - Opinions of court nobles, oversight of local officials * Volume 8 - Agricultural promotion, taxation, monetary lending * Volume 9 - Land allotments, state-managed fields, land reclamation, mountains, rivers, and wetlands * Volume 10 - Imperial household cuisine, amnesties, documents, seals * Volume 11 - Military affairs, conscription, sumo, postal stations, timber * Volume 12 - Various prohibitions and punishments
== Structure of the 20-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Preface, shrine affairs, divine properties and land taxes, rituals and offerings, divine Rankings, oracles, Saiō matters, purification matters * Volume 2 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 1): Creation of Buddha images, sutra copying, Buddhist ceremonies, tantric initiations, state-sponsored monastics * Volume 3 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 2): Provincial temples, established temples, monastic Ranks, provincial lecturers, monastic prohibitions * Volume 4 - Government office reforms, staff adjustments, miscellaneous appointments * Volume 5 - Provincial governance, staffing adjustments, official Ranks, administrative transitions * Volume 6 - Salaries, stipends, official attire, public resources, labor duties, aid distribution * Volume 7 - Court noble opinions, livestock management, district officials * Volume 8 - Agriculture and sericulture, taxation, household registries, grain storage, economic regulations * Volume 9 - (Content missing) * Volume 10 - Imperial affairs, national mourning, court cuisine * Volume 11 - (Content missing) * Volume 12 - Official missions, legal documents, fugitives, state warehouses * Volume 13 - (Content missing) * Volume 14 - Loans, financial transactions, miscellaneous grains, relief granaries, land management, coin minting * Volume 15 - Land distribution, tax land, state-owned fields, cultivated land, temple lands, official lands * Volume 16 - Abandoned lands, roads, ports, wilderness management, riverbanks, irrigation * Volume 17 - Name changes, amnesties, pardons, reward systems, document management * Volume 18 - Military affairs, conscription, defense units, weapons, border controls, foreign relations, sumo, livestock, postal systems, timber management * Volume 19 - Prohibitions and restrictions * Volume 20 - Criminal judgments and fines
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Sources == * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第12巻 (National Historical Series, Vol. 12) |year=1900 |publisher=経済雑誌社 |chapter=類聚三代格 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991102/1/1 |doi=10.11501/991102}} * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第25巻 新訂増補 (National Historical Series, Vol. 25) |year=1936 |publisher=国史大系刊行会 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3431640/1/4 |doi=10.11501/3431640 |chapter=類聚三代格}} == See Also == * [[:en:Ruijū Kokushi|Ruijū Kokushi]]
== External Links == * [http://codh.rois.ac.jp/pmjt/book/200018252/ *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* - Japanese Classical Text Database]
== See Also from {{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|ja|格式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyaku and Shiki|qq=}} == {{Wiktionary}} * [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|Ritsuryō]]－{{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Ritsuryō law|ja|律令法|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō law|qq=}}－[[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] * {{ill|Three Great Legal Codes|ja|三代格式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Legal Codes|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Kōnin Kyakushiki|ja|弘仁格式|lt=Kōnin Codes (Kōnin Kyaku, Kōnin Shiki)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnin Kyakushiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三代格式}} ''' {{ill|Jōgan Kyakushiki|ja|貞観格式|lt=Jōgan Codes (Jōgan Kyaku, Jōgan Shiki)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan Kyakushiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三代格式}} ''' {{ill|Engi Kyakushiki|ja|延喜格式|lt=Engi Codes (Engi Kyaku, Engi Shiki)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Kyakushiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三代格式}} * {{ill|Kōtai-shiki|ja|交替式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai-shiki|qq=}} * {{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}} * {{ill|Hachiman Usa-gū Gotakusenshū|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Usa-gū Gotakusenshū|qq=}} * {{ill|Myōhō-dō|ja|明法道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōhō-dō|qq=}}－{{ill|Myōhō Hakase|ja|明法博士|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōhō Hakase|qq=}} * [[:en:Imperial examination|Imperial Examination]]
* [[Abe no Seimei]] * [[Da Liu Ren]] * [[Goryō]] * [[Itako]] * [[Kuji-in]] * [[Onmyōdō]] * [[Sanpaku]] * [[Seimei Shrine]] * [[Senji Ryakketsu]] * [[Shikigami]] * [[Shinigami]] * [[Tengenjutsu (fortune telling)]] * [[Ushi no toki mairi]]

== Structure of the 12-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Shinto shrine rituals, divine oracles, shrine priests, purification * Volume 2 - Buddha images, Buddhist ceremonies, appointment of clergy * Volume 3 - Provincial temples, clerical Ranks, monastic prohibitions, regional lecturers * Volume 4 - Central government office reorganizations and staffing * Volume 5 - Provincial bureaucracy and Ranks * Volume 6 - Bureaucratic salary systems * Volume 7 - Opinions of court nobles, oversight of local officials * Volume 8 - Agricultural promotion, taxation, monetary lending * Volume 9 - Land allotments, state-managed fields, land reclamation, mountains, rivers, and wetlands * Volume 10 - Imperial household cuisine, amnesties, documents, seals * Volume 11 - Military affairs, conscription, sumo, postal stations, timber * Volume 12 - Various prohibitions and punishments
== Structure of the 20-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Preface, shrine affairs, divine properties and land taxes, rituals and offerings, divine Rankings, oracles, Saiō matters, purification matters * Volume 2 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 1): Creation of Buddha images, sutra copying, Buddhist ceremonies, tantric initiations, state-sponsored monastics * Volume 3 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 2): Provincial temples, established temples, monastic Ranks, provincial lecturers, monastic prohibitions * Volume 4 - Government office reforms, staff adjustments, miscellaneous appointments * Volume 5 - Provincial governance, staffing adjustments, official Ranks, administrative transitions * Volume 6 - Salaries, stipends, official attire, public resources, labor duties, aid distribution * Volume 7 - Court noble opinions, livestock management, district officials * Volume 8 - Agriculture and sericulture, taxation, household registries, grain storage, economic regulations * Volume 9 - (Content missing) * Volume 10 - Imperial affairs, national mourning, court cuisine * Volume 11 - (Content missing) * Volume 12 - Official missions, legal documents, fugitives, state warehouses * Volume 13 - (Content missing) * Volume 14 - Loans, financial transactions, miscellaneous grains, relief granaries, land management, coin minting * Volume 15 - Land distribution, tax land, state-owned fields, cultivated land, temple lands, official lands * Volume 16 - Abandoned lands, roads, ports, wilderness management, riverbanks, irrigation * Volume 17 - Name changes, amnesties, pardons, reward systems, document management * Volume 18 - Military affairs, conscription, defense units, weapons, border controls, foreign relations, sumo, livestock, postal systems, timber management * Volume 19 - Prohibitions and restrictions * Volume 20 - Criminal judgments and fines
== References == {{Reflist}}

== External Links == * [http://codh.rois.ac.jp/pmjt/book/200018252/ *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* - Japanese Classical Text Database]
== See Also from {{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|ja|格式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyaku and Shiki|qq=}} == {{Wiktionary}} * [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|Ritsuryō]]－{{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Ritsuryō law|ja|律令法|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō law|qq=}}－[[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] * {{ill|Three Great Legal Codes|ja|三代格式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Legal Codes|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Kōnin Kyakushiki|ja|弘仁格式|lt=Kōnin Codes (Kōnin Kyaku, Kōnin Shiki)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnin Kyakushiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三代格式}} ''' {{ill|Jōgan Kyakushiki|ja|貞観格式|lt=Jōgan Codes (Jōgan Kyaku, Jōgan Shiki)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan Kyakushiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三代格式}} ''' {{ill|Engi Kyakushiki|ja|延喜格式|lt=Engi Codes (Engi Kyaku, Engi Shiki)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Kyakushiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三代格式}} * {{ill|Kōtai-shiki|ja|交替式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai-shiki|qq=}} * {{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}} * {{ill|Hachiman Usa-gū Gotakusenshū|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Usa-gū Gotakusenshū|qq=}} * {{ill|Myōhō-dō|ja|明法道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōhō-dō|qq=}}－{{ill|Myōhō Hakase|ja|明法博士|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōhō Hakase|qq=}} * [[:en:Imperial examination|Imperial Examination]]

revision.text:
'''Ruijū Sandai Kyaku''' (類聚三代格) is a compilation of legal codes from the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. The compiler is unknown.<ref name="Nipponica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref>
== Overview == The *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* organizes the legal codes of the {{ill|Kōnin Kyaku|ja|弘仁格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnin Kyaku|qq=}}, {{ill|Jōgan Kyaku|ja|貞観格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan Kyaku|qq=}}, and {{ill|Engi Kyaku|ja|延喜格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Kyaku|qq=}} thematically into categories such as "Shinto Shrine Affairs," "Provincial Temple Affairs," and "Tax Matters."<ref name="Nipponica" /><ref name="Sekaidaihyakka">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第2版|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> It does not re-record items already included in previous compilations.<ref name="Britannica" /> The compilation date is estimated to be between [[:en:Chōhō (era)|Chōhō 4]] (1002) and [[:en:Kanji (era)|Kanjī 3]] (1089) according to the *Nipponica* encyclopedia.<ref name="Nipponica" />
The three-generation codes were originally organized by government office, which was inconvenient for legal reference, especially during the mid-Heian period when there was a growing trend towards the study of {{ill|Yūsoku kojitsu|ja|有職故実|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūsoku kojitsu|qq=}} (ancient court practices). Thus, the *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* was compiled as part of legal codification efforts.<ref name="Nipponica" />
No complete manuscript survives; only partial copies are extant.<ref name="Mypedia">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=百科事典マイペディア|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> The original codes—*Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku*—comprised 10, 12, and 12 volumes respectively.<ref name="Britannica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> Existing manuscripts of *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* are said to contain 15<ref name="Britannica" /> or 17 volumes,<ref name="Obunsha">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=旺文社日本史事典 三訂版|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> with versions ranging from 12 to 20 volumes. The *Honchō Shojaku Mokuroku* lists it as having 30 volumes,<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" /> though scholars debate whether the full compilation indeed contained 20<ref name="Obunsha" /> or 30 volumes,<ref name="Mypedia" /> with *Britannica* casting doubt on the 30-volume theory.<ref name="Britannica" /> Even combining the surviving 12- and 20-volume manuscripts, gaps remain.<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" />
Since the original texts of the *Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku* are mostly lost (except for the *Kōnin Kyaku* index), *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku*—which preserves their contents—is an invaluable resource for understanding ancient Japanese legal systems.<ref name="Britannica" />
== Structure of the 12-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Shinto shrine rituals, divine oracles, shrine priests, purification * Volume 2 - Buddha images, Buddhist ceremonies, appointment of clergy * Volume 3 - Provincial temples, clerical Ranks, monastic prohibitions, regional lecturers * Volume 4 - Central government office reorganizations and staffing * Volume 5 - Provincial bureaucracy and Ranks * Volume 6 - Bureaucratic salary systems * Volume 7 - Opinions of court nobles, oversight of local officials * Volume 8 - Agricultural promotion, taxation, monetary lending * Volume 9 - Land allotments, state-managed fields, land reclamation, mountains, rivers, and wetlands * Volume 10 - Imperial household cuisine, amnesties, documents, seals * Volume 11 - Military affairs, conscription, sumo, postal stations, timber * Volume 12 - Various prohibitions and punishments
== Structure of the 20-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Preface, shrine affairs, divine properties and land taxes, rituals and offerings, divine Rankings, oracles, Saiō matters, purification matters * Volume 2 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 1): Creation of Buddha images, sutra copying, Buddhist ceremonies, tantric initiations, state-sponsored monastics * Volume 3 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 2): Provincial temples, established temples, monastic Ranks, provincial lecturers, monastic prohibitions * Volume 4 - Government office reforms, staff adjustments, miscellaneous appointments * Volume 5 - Provincial governance, staffing adjustments, official Ranks, administrative transitions * Volume 6 - Salaries, stipends, official attire, public resources, labor duties, aid distribution * Volume 7 - Court noble opinions, livestock management, district officials * Volume 8 - Agriculture and sericulture, taxation, household registries, grain storage, economic regulations * Volume 9 - (Content missing) * Volume 10 - Imperial affairs, national mourning, court cuisine * Volume 11 - (Content missing) * Volume 12 - Official missions, legal documents, fugitives, state warehouses * Volume 13 - (Content missing) * Volume 14 - Loans, financial transactions, miscellaneous grains, relief granaries, land management, coin minting * Volume 15 - Land distribution, tax land, state-owned fields, cultivated land, temple lands, official lands * Volume 16 - Abandoned lands, roads, ports, wilderness management, riverbanks, irrigation * Volume 17 - Name changes, amnesties, pardons, reward systems, document management * Volume 18 - Military affairs, conscription, defense units, weapons, border controls, foreign relations, sumo, livestock, postal systems, timber management * Volume 19 - Prohibitions and restrictions * Volume 20 - Criminal judgments and fines
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Sources == * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第12巻 (National Historical Series, Vol. 12) |year=1900 |publisher=経済雑誌社 |chapter=類聚三代格 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991102/1/1 |doi=10.11501/991102}} * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第25巻 新訂増補 (National Historical Series, Vol. 25) |year=1936 |publisher=国史大系刊行会 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3431640/1/4 |doi=10.11501/3431640 |chapter=類聚三代格}} == See Also == * [[:en:Ruijū Kokushi|Ruijū Kokushi]]
== External Links == * [http://codh.rois.ac.jp/pmjt/book/200018252/ *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* - Japanese Classical Text Database]
== See Also from {{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|ja|格式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyaku and Shiki|qq=}} == {{Wiktionary}} * [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|Ritsuryō]]－{{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Ritsuryō law|ja|律令法|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō law|qq=}}－[[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] * {{ill|Three Great Legal Codes|ja|三代格式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Legal Codes|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Kōnin Kyakushiki|ja|弘仁格式|lt=Kōnin Codes (Kōnin Kyaku, Kōnin Shiki)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnin Kyakushiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三代格式}} ''' {{ill|Jōgan Kyakushiki|ja|貞観格式|lt=Jōgan Codes (Jōgan Kyaku, Jōgan Shiki)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan Kyakushiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三代格式}} ''' {{ill|Engi Kyakushiki|ja|延喜格式|lt=Engi Codes (Engi Kyaku, Engi Shiki)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Kyakushiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三代格式}} * {{ill|Kōtai-shiki|ja|交替式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai-shiki|qq=}} * {{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}} * {{ill|Hachiman Usa-gū Gotakusenshū|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Usa-gū Gotakusenshū|qq=}} * {{ill|Myōhō-dō|ja|明法道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōhō-dō|qq=}}－{{ill|Myōhō Hakase|ja|明法博士|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōhō Hakase|qq=}} * [[:en:Imperial examination|Imperial Examination]]
* [[Abe no Seimei]] * [[:en:Da Liu Ren|Da Liu Ren]] * [[Goryō]] * [[Itako]] * [[Kuji-in]] * [[Onmyōdō]] * [[Sanpaku]] * [[Seimei Shrine]] * [[Senji Ryakketsu]] * [[Shikigami]] * [[Shinigami]] * [[Tengenjutsu (fortune telling)]] * [[Ushi no toki mairi]]

revision.text:
'''Ruijū Sandai Kyaku''' (類聚三代格) is a compilation of legal codes from the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. The compiler is unknown.<ref name="Nipponica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref>
== Overview == The *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* organizes the legal codes of the {{ill|Kōnin Kyaku|ja|弘仁格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnin Kyaku|qq=}}, {{ill|Jōgan Kyaku|ja|貞観格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan Kyaku|qq=}}, and {{ill|Engi Kyaku|ja|延喜格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Kyaku|qq=}} thematically into categories such as "Shinto Shrine Affairs," "Provincial Temple Affairs," and "Tax Matters."<ref name="Nipponica" /><ref name="Sekaidaihyakka">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第2版|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> It does not re-record items already included in previous compilations.<ref name="Britannica" /> The compilation date is estimated to be between [[:en:Chōhō (era)|Chōhō 4]] (1002) and [[:en:Kanji (era)|Kanjī 3]] (1089) according to the *Nipponica* encyclopedia.<ref name="Nipponica" />
The three-generation codes were originally organized by government office, which was inconvenient for legal reference, especially during the mid-Heian period when there was a growing trend towards the study of {{ill|Yūsoku kojitsu|ja|有職故実|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūsoku kojitsu|qq=}} (ancient court practices). Thus, the *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* was compiled as part of legal codification efforts.<ref name="Nipponica" />
No complete manuscript survives; only partial copies are extant.<ref name="Mypedia">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=百科事典マイペディア|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> The original codes—*Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku*—comprised 10, 12, and 12 volumes respectively.<ref name="Britannica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> Existing manuscripts of *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* are said to contain 15<ref name="Britannica" /> or 17 volumes,<ref name="Obunsha">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=旺文社日本史事典 三訂版|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> with versions ranging from 12 to 20 volumes. The *Honchō Shojaku Mokuroku* lists it as having 30 volumes,<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" /> though scholars debate whether the full compilation indeed contained 20<ref name="Obunsha" /> or 30 volumes,<ref name="Mypedia" /> with *Britannica* casting doubt on the 30-volume theory.<ref name="Britannica" /> Even combining the surviving 12- and 20-volume manuscripts, gaps remain.<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" />
Since the original texts of the *Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku* are mostly lost (except for the *Kōnin Kyaku* index), *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku*—which preserves their contents—is an invaluable resource for understanding ancient Japanese legal systems.<ref name="Britannica" />
== Structure of the 12-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Shinto shrine rituals, divine oracles, shrine priests, purification * Volume 2 - Buddha images, Buddhist ceremonies, appointment of clergy * Volume 3 - Provincial temples, clerical Ranks, monastic prohibitions, regional lecturers * Volume 4 - Central government office reorganizations and staffing * Volume 5 - Provincial bureaucracy and Ranks * Volume 6 - Bureaucratic salary systems * Volume 7 - Opinions of court nobles, oversight of local officials * Volume 8 - Agricultural promotion, taxation, monetary lending * Volume 9 - Land allotments, state-managed fields, land reclamation, mountains, rivers, and wetlands * Volume 10 - Imperial household cuisine, amnesties, documents, seals * Volume 11 - Military affairs, conscription, sumo, postal stations, timber * Volume 12 - Various prohibitions and punishments
== Structure of the 20-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Preface, shrine affairs, divine properties and land taxes, rituals and offerings, divine Rankings, oracles, Saiō matters, purification matters * Volume 2 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 1): Creation of Buddha images, sutra copying, Buddhist ceremonies, tantric initiations, state-sponsored monastics * Volume 3 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 2): Provincial temples, established temples, monastic Ranks, provincial lecturers, monastic prohibitions * Volume 4 - Government office reforms, staff adjustments, miscellaneous appointments * Volume 5 - Provincial governance, staffing adjustments, official Ranks, administrative transitions * Volume 6 - Salaries, stipends, official attire, public resources, labor duties, aid distribution * Volume 7 - Court noble opinions, livestock management, district officials * Volume 8 - Agriculture and sericulture, taxation, household registries, grain storage, economic regulations * Volume 9 - (Content missing) * Volume 10 - Imperial affairs, national mourning, court cuisine * Volume 11 - (Content missing) * Volume 12 - Official missions, legal documents, fugitives, state warehouses * Volume 13 - (Content missing) * Volume 14 - Loans, financial transactions, miscellaneous grains, relief granaries, land management, coin minting * Volume 15 - Land distribution, tax land, state-owned fields, cultivated land, temple lands, official lands * Volume 16 - Abandoned lands, roads, ports, wilderness management, riverbanks, irrigation * Volume 17 - Name changes, amnesties, pardons, reward systems, document management * Volume 18 - Military affairs, conscription, defense units, weapons, border controls, foreign relations, sumo, livestock, postal systems, timber management * Volume 19 - Prohibitions and restrictions * Volume 20 - Criminal judgments and fines
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Sources == * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第12巻 (National Historical Series, Vol. 12) |year=1900 |publisher=経済雑誌社 |chapter=類聚三代格 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991102/1/1 |doi=10.11501/991102}} * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第25巻 新訂増補 (National Historical Series, Vol. 25) |year=1936 |publisher=国史大系刊行会 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3431640/1/4 |doi=10.11501/3431640 |chapter=類聚三代格}} == See Also == * [[:en:Ruijū Kokushi|Ruijū Kokushi]]
== External Links == * [http://codh.rois.ac.jp/pmjt/book/200018252/ *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* - Japanese Classical Text Database]
== See Also from {{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|ja|格式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyaku and Shiki|qq=}} == {{Wiktionary}} * [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|Ritsuryō]]－{{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Ritsuryō law|ja|律令法|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō law|qq=}}－[[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] * {{ill|Three Great Legal Codes|ja|三代格式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Legal Codes|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Kōnin Kyakushiki|ja|弘仁格式|lt=Kōnin Codes (Kōnin Kyaku, Kōnin Shiki)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnin Kyakushiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三代格式}} ''' {{ill|Jōgan Kyakushiki|ja|貞観格式|lt=Jōgan Codes (Jōgan Kyaku, Jōgan Shiki)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan Kyakushiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三代格式}} ''' {{ill|Engi Kyakushiki|ja|延喜格式|lt=Engi Codes (Engi Kyaku, Engi Shiki)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Kyakushiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三代格式}} * {{ill|Kōtai-shiki|ja|交替式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai-shiki|qq=}} * {{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}} * {{ill|Hachiman Usa-gū Gotakusenshū|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Usa-gū Gotakusenshū|qq=}} * {{ill|Myōhō-dō|ja|明法道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōhō-dō|qq=}}－{{ill|Myōhō Hakase|ja|明法博士|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōhō Hakase|qq=}} * [[:en:Imperial examination|Imperial Examination]]
* [[Abe no Seimei]] * [[:en:Da Liu Ren|Da Liu Ren]] * [[Goryō]] * [[Itako]] * [[Kuji-in]] * [[Onmyōdō]] * [[:en:Sanpaku|Sanpaku]] * [[Seimei Shrine]] * [[Senji Ryakketsu]] * [[Shikigami]] * [[Shinigami]] * [[Tengenjutsu (fortune telling)]] * [[Ushi no toki mairi]]
{{translated page|ja|類聚三代格|version=104526082|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories|
[[Category:Completion tier 2]]
}}
