== Sources == * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第12巻 (National Historical Series, Vol. 12) |year=1900 |publisher=経済雑誌社 |chapter=類聚三代格 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991102/1/1 |doi=10.11501/991102}} * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第25巻 新訂増補 (National Historical Series, Vol. 25) |year=1936 |publisher=国史大系刊行会 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3431640/1/4 |doi=10.11501/3431640 |chapter=類聚三代格}} == See Also == * [[:en:Ruijū Kokushi|Ruijū Kokushi]]
== External Links == * [http://codh.rois.ac.jp/pmjt/book/200018252/ *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* - Japanese Classical Text Database]
== See Also from {{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|ja|格式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyaku and Shiki|qq=}} == {{Wiktionary}} * [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|Ritsuryō]]－{{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Ritsuryō law|ja|律令法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō law|qq=}}－[[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] * {{ill|Three Great Legal Codes|ja|三代格式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Legal Codes|1=Sandai Kyakushiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354355}} ''' {{ill|Kōnin Kyakushiki|ja|弘仁格式|lt=Kōnin Codes (Kōnin Kyaku, Kōnin Shiki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnin Kyakushiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三代格式|1=Sandai Kyakushiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354355}} ''' {{ill|Jōgan Kyakushiki|ja|貞観格式|lt=Jōgan Codes (Jōgan Kyaku, Jōgan Shiki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan Kyakushiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三代格式|1=Sandai Kyakushiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354355}} ''' {{ill|Engi Kyakushiki|ja|延喜格式|lt=Engi Codes (Engi Kyaku, Engi Shiki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Kyakushiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三代格式|1=Sandai Kyakushiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354355}} * {{ill|Kōtai-shiki|ja|交替式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai-shiki|1=kōtaishiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11373940}} * {{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}} * {{ill|Hachiman Usa-gū Gotakusenshū|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Usa-gū Gotakusenshū|qq=}} * {{ill|Myōhō-dō|ja|明法道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōhō-dō|1=Myōbōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512427}}－{{ill|Myōhō Hakase|ja|明法博士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōhō Hakase|qq=}} * [[:en:Imperial examination|Imperial Examination]]
* [[Abe no Seimei]] * [[:en:Da Liu Ren|Da Liu Ren]] * [[Goryō]] * [[Itako]] * [[Kuji-in]] * [[Onmyōdō]] * [[:en:Sanpaku|Sanpaku]] * [[Seimei Shrine]] * [[Senji Ryakketsu]] * [[Shikigami]] * [[:en:Shinigami|Shinigami]] * [[Tengenjutsu (fortune telling)]] * [[Ushi no toki mairi]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵↵↵" to " "
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Ruiju Sandai Kyaku]][[ja:類聚三代格]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Ruiju Sandai Kyaku]][[ja:類聚三代格]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ruiju Sandai Kyaku]][[ja:類聚三代格]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11665185]] Wikidata name: Ruiju Sandaikyaku [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ruijū Sandai Kyaku''' (類聚三代格) is a compilation of legal codes from the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. The compiler is unknown.<ref name="Nipponica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref>
== Overview == The *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* organizes the legal codes of the {{ill|Kōnin Kyaku|ja|弘仁格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnin Kyaku|1=Kōnin Kyaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487196}}, {{ill|Jōgan Kyaku|ja|貞観格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan Kyaku|1=Jōgan Kyaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634549}}, and {{ill|Engi Kyaku|ja|延喜格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Kyaku|1=Engi Kyaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486506}} thematically into categories such as "Shinto Shrine Affairs," "Provincial Temple Affairs," and "Tax Matters."<ref name="Nipponica" /><ref name="Sekaidaihyakka">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第2版|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> It does not re-record items already included in previous compilations.<ref name="Britannica" /> The compilation date is estimated to be between [[:en:Chōhō (era)|Chōhō 4]] (1002) and [[:en:Kanji (era)|Kanjī 3]] (1089) according to the *Nipponica* encyclopedia.<ref name="Nipponica" />
The three-generation codes were originally organized by government office, which was inconvenient for legal reference, especially during the mid-Heian period when there was a growing trend towards the study of {{ill|Yūsoku kojitsu|ja|有職故実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūsoku kojitsu|1=Yūsoku Kojitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1623327}} (ancient court practices). Thus, the *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* was compiled as part of legal codification efforts.<ref name="Nipponica" />
No complete manuscript survives; only partial copies are extant.<ref name="Mypedia">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=百科事典マイペディア|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> The original codes—*Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku*—comprised 10, 12, and 12 volumes respectively.<ref name="Britannica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> Existing manuscripts of *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* are said to contain 15<ref name="Britannica" /> or 17 volumes,<ref name="Obunsha">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=旺文社日本史事典 三訂版|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> with versions ranging from 12 to 20 volumes. The *Honchō Shojaku Mokuroku* lists it as having 30 volumes,<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" /> though scholars debate whether the full compilation indeed contained 20<ref name="Obunsha" /> or 30 volumes,<ref name="Mypedia" /> with *Britannica* casting doubt on the 30-volume theory.<ref name="Britannica" /> Even combining the surviving 12- and 20-volume manuscripts, gaps remain.<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" />
Since the original texts of the *Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku* are mostly lost (except for the *Kōnin Kyaku* index), *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku*—which preserves their contents—is an invaluable resource for understanding ancient Japanese legal systems.<ref name="Britannica" />
== Structure of the 12-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Shinto shrine rituals, divine oracles, shrine priests, purification * Volume 2 - Buddha images, Buddhist ceremonies, appointment of clergy * Volume 3 - Provincial temples, clerical Ranks, monastic prohibitions, regional lecturers * Volume 4 - Central government office reorganizations and staffing * Volume 5 - Provincial bureaucracy and Ranks * Volume 6 - Bureaucratic salary systems * Volume 7 - Opinions of court nobles, oversight of local officials * Volume 8 - Agricultural promotion, taxation, monetary lending * Volume 9 - Land allotments, state-managed fields, land reclamation, mountains, rivers, and wetlands * Volume 10 - Imperial household cuisine, amnesties, documents, seals * Volume 11 - Military affairs, conscription, sumo, postal stations, timber * Volume 12 - Various prohibitions and punishments
== Structure of the 20-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Preface, shrine affairs, divine properties and land taxes, rituals and offerings, divine Rankings, oracles, Saiō matters, purification matters * Volume 2 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 1): Creation of Buddha images, sutra copying, Buddhist ceremonies, tantric initiations, state-sponsored monastics * Volume 3 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 2): Provincial temples, established temples, monastic Ranks, provincial lecturers, monastic prohibitions * Volume 4 - Government office reforms, staff adjustments, miscellaneous appointments * Volume 5 - Provincial governance, staffing adjustments, official Ranks, administrative transitions * Volume 6 - Salaries, stipends, official attire, public resources, labor duties, aid distribution * Volume 7 - Court noble opinions, livestock management, district officials * Volume 8 - Agriculture and sericulture, taxation, household registries, grain storage, economic regulations * Volume 9 - (Content missing) * Volume 10 - Imperial affairs, national mourning, court cuisine * Volume 11 - (Content missing) * Volume 12 - Official missions, legal documents, fugitives, state warehouses * Volume 13 - (Content missing) * Volume 14 - Loans, financial transactions, miscellaneous grains, relief granaries, land management, coin minting * Volume 15 - Land distribution, tax land, state-owned fields, cultivated land, temple lands, official lands * Volume 16 - Abandoned lands, roads, ports, wilderness management, riverbanks, irrigation * Volume 17 - Name changes, amnesties, pardons, reward systems, document management * Volume 18 - Military affairs, conscription, defense units, weapons, border controls, foreign relations, sumo, livestock, postal systems, timber management * Volume 19 - Prohibitions and restrictions * Volume 20 - Criminal judgments and fines
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Sources == * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第12巻 (National Historical Series, Vol. 12) |year=1900 |publisher=経済雑誌社 |chapter=類聚三代格 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991102/1/1 |doi=10.11501/991102}} * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第25巻 新訂増補 (National Historical Series, Vol. 25) |year=1936 |publisher=国史大系刊行会 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3431640/1/4 |doi=10.11501/3431640 |chapter=類聚三代格}} == See Also == * [[:en:Ruijū Kokushi|Ruijū Kokushi]]
== External Links == * [http://codh.rois.ac.jp/pmjt/book/200018252/ *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* - Japanese Classical Text Database]
== See Also from {{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|ja|格式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyaku and Shiki|qq=}} == {{Wiktionary}} * [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|Ritsuryō]]－{{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Ritsuryō law|ja|律令法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō law|qq=}}－[[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] * {{ill|Three Great Legal Codes|ja|三代格式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Legal Codes|1=Sandai Kyakushiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354355}} ''' {{ill|Kōnin Kyakushiki|ja|弘仁格式|lt=Kōnin Codes (Kōnin Kyaku, Kōnin Shiki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnin Kyakushiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三代格式|1=Sandai Kyakushiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354355}} ''' {{ill|Jōgan Kyakushiki|ja|貞観格式|lt=Jōgan Codes (Jōgan Kyaku, Jōgan Shiki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan Kyakushiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三代格式|1=Sandai Kyakushiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354355}} ''' {{ill|Engi Kyakushiki|ja|延喜格式|lt=Engi Codes (Engi Kyaku, Engi Shiki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Kyakushiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三代格式|1=Sandai Kyakushiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354355}} * {{ill|Kōtai-shiki|ja|交替式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai-shiki|1=kōtaishiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11373940}} * {{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}} * {{ill|Hachiman Usa-gū Gotakusenshū|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Usa-gū Gotakusenshū|qq=}} * {{ill|Myōhō-dō|ja|明法道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōhō-dō|1=Myōbōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512427}}－{{ill|Myōhō Hakase|ja|明法博士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōhō Hakase|qq=}} * [[:en:Imperial examination|Imperial Examination]]
* [[Abe no Seimei]] * [[:en:Da Liu Ren|Da Liu Ren]] * [[Goryō]] * [[Itako]] * [[Kuji-in]] * [[Onmyōdō]] * [[:en:Sanpaku|Sanpaku]] * [[Seimei Shrine]] * [[Senji Ryakketsu]] * [[Shikigami]] * [[:en:Shinigami|Shinigami]] * [[Tengenjutsu (fortune telling)]] * [[Ushi no toki mairi]]

No complete manuscript survives; only partial copies are extant.<ref name="Mypedia">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=百科事典マイペディア|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> The original codes—*Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku*—comprised 10, 12, and 12 volumes respectively.<ref name="Britannica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> Existing manuscripts of *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* are said to contain 15<ref name="Britannica" /> or 17 volumes,<ref name="Obunsha">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=旺文社日本史事典 三訂版|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> with versions ranging from 12 to 20 volumes. The *Honchō Shojaku Mokuroku* lists it as having 30 volumes,<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" /> though scholars debate whether the full compilation indeed contained 20<ref name="Obunsha" /> or 30 volumes,<ref name="Mypedia" /> with *Britannica* casting doubt on the 30-volume theory.<ref name="Britannica" /> Even combining the surviving 12- and 20-volume manuscripts, gaps remain.<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" />

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Ruiju Sandai Kyaku]][[ja:類聚三代格]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Ruiju Sandai Kyaku]][[ja:類聚三代格]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ruiju Sandai Kyaku]][[ja:類聚三代格]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11665185]] Wikidata name: Ruiju Sandaikyaku [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ruijū Sandai Kyaku''' (類聚三代格) is a compilation of legal codes from the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. The compiler is unknown.<ref name="Nipponica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref>
== Overview == The *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* organizes the legal codes of the {{ill|Kōnin Kyaku|ja|弘仁格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnin Kyaku|1=Kōnin Kyaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487196}}, {{ill|Jōgan Kyaku|ja|貞観格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan Kyaku|1=Jōgan Kyaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634549}}, and {{ill|Engi Kyaku|ja|延喜格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Kyaku|1=Engi Kyaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486506}} thematically into categories such as "Shinto Shrine Affairs," "Provincial Temple Affairs," and "Tax Matters."<ref name="Nipponica" /><ref name="Sekaidaihyakka">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第2版|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> It does not re-record items already included in previous compilations.<ref name="Britannica" /> The compilation date is estimated to be between [[:en:Chōhō (era)|Chōhō 4]] (1002) and [[:en:Kanji (era)|Kanjī 3]] (1089) according to the *Nipponica* encyclopedia.<ref name="Nipponica" />
The three-generation codes were originally organized by government office, which was inconvenient for legal reference, especially during the mid-Heian period when there was a growing trend towards the study of {{ill|Yūsoku kojitsu|ja|有職故実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūsoku kojitsu|1=Yūsoku Kojitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1623327}} (ancient court practices). Thus, the *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* was compiled as part of legal codification efforts.<ref name="Nipponica" />
No complete manuscript survives; only partial copies are extant.<ref name="Mypedia">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=百科事典マイペディア|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> The original codes—*Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku*—comprised 10, 12, and 12 volumes respectively.<ref name="Britannica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> Existing manuscripts of *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* are said to contain 15<ref name="Britannica" /> or 17 volumes,<ref name="Obunsha">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=旺文社日本史事典 三訂版|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> with versions ranging from 12 to 20 volumes. The *Honchō Shojaku Mokuroku* lists it as having 30 volumes,<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" /> though scholars debate whether the full compilation indeed contained 20<ref name="Obunsha" /> or 30 volumes,<ref name="Mypedia" /> with *Britannica* casting doubt on the 30-volume theory.<ref name="Britannica" /> Even combining the surviving 12- and 20-volume manuscripts, gaps remain.<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" />
Since the original texts of the *Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku* are mostly lost (except for the *Kōnin Kyaku* index), *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku*—which preserves their contents—is an invaluable resource for understanding ancient Japanese legal systems.<ref name="Britannica" />
== Structure of the 12-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Shinto shrine rituals, divine oracles, shrine priests, purification * Volume 2 - Buddha images, Buddhist ceremonies, appointment of clergy * Volume 3 - Provincial temples, clerical Ranks, monastic prohibitions, regional lecturers * Volume 4 - Central government office reorganizations and staffing * Volume 5 - Provincial bureaucracy and Ranks * Volume 6 - Bureaucratic salary systems * Volume 7 - Opinions of court nobles, oversight of local officials * Volume 8 - Agricultural promotion, taxation, monetary lending * Volume 9 - Land allotments, state-managed fields, land reclamation, mountains, rivers, and wetlands * Volume 10 - Imperial household cuisine, amnesties, documents, seals * Volume 11 - Military affairs, conscription, sumo, postal stations, timber * Volume 12 - Various prohibitions and punishments
== Structure of the 20-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Preface, shrine affairs, divine properties and land taxes, rituals and offerings, divine Rankings, oracles, Saiō matters, purification matters * Volume 2 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 1): Creation of Buddha images, sutra copying, Buddhist ceremonies, tantric initiations, state-sponsored monastics * Volume 3 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 2): Provincial temples, established temples, monastic Ranks, provincial lecturers, monastic prohibitions * Volume 4 - Government office reforms, staff adjustments, miscellaneous appointments * Volume 5 - Provincial governance, staffing adjustments, official Ranks, administrative transitions * Volume 6 - Salaries, stipends, official attire, public resources, labor duties, aid distribution * Volume 7 - Court noble opinions, livestock management, district officials * Volume 8 - Agriculture and sericulture, taxation, household registries, grain storage, economic regulations * Volume 9 - (Content missing) * Volume 10 - Imperial affairs, national mourning, court cuisine * Volume 11 - (Content missing) * Volume 12 - Official missions, legal documents, fugitives, state warehouses * Volume 13 - (Content missing) * Volume 14 - Loans, financial transactions, miscellaneous grains, relief granaries, land management, coin minting * Volume 15 - Land distribution, tax land, state-owned fields, cultivated land, temple lands, official lands * Volume 16 - Abandoned lands, roads, ports, wilderness management, riverbanks, irrigation * Volume 17 - Name changes, amnesties, pardons, reward systems, document management * Volume 18 - Military affairs, conscription, defense units, weapons, border controls, foreign relations, sumo, livestock, postal systems, timber management * Volume 19 - Prohibitions and restrictions * Volume 20 - Criminal judgments and fines
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Sources == * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第12巻 (National Historical Series, Vol. 12) |year=1900 |publisher=経済雑誌社 |chapter=類聚三代格 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991102/1/1 |doi=10.11501/991102}} * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第25巻 新訂増補 (National Historical Series, Vol. 25) |year=1936 |publisher=国史大系刊行会 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3431640/1/4 |doi=10.11501/3431640 |chapter=類聚三代格}} == See Also == * [[:en:Ruijū Kokushi|Ruijū Kokushi]]
== External Links == * [http://codh.rois.ac.jp/pmjt/book/200018252/ *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* - Japanese Classical Text Database]
== See Also from {{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|ja|格式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyaku and Shiki|qq=}} == {{Wiktionary}} * [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|Ritsuryō]]－{{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Ritsuryō law|ja|律令法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō law|qq=}}－[[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] * {{ill|Three Great Legal Codes|ja|三代格式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Legal Codes|1=Sandai Kyakushiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354355}} ''' {{ill|Kōnin Kyakushiki|ja|弘仁格式|lt=Kōnin Codes (Kōnin Kyaku, Kōnin Shiki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnin Kyakushiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三代格式|1=Sandai Kyakushiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354355}} ''' {{ill|Jōgan Kyakushiki|ja|貞観格式|lt=Jōgan Codes (Jōgan Kyaku, Jōgan Shiki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan Kyakushiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三代格式|1=Sandai Kyakushiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354355}} ''' {{ill|Engi Kyakushiki|ja|延喜格式|lt=Engi Codes (Engi Kyaku, Engi Shiki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Kyakushiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三代格式|1=Sandai Kyakushiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354355}} * {{ill|Kōtai-shiki|ja|交替式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai-shiki|1=kōtaishiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11373940}} * {{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}} * {{ill|Hachiman Usa-gū Gotakusenshū|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Usa-gū Gotakusenshū|qq=}} * {{ill|Myōhō-dō|ja|明法道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōhō-dō|1=Myōbōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512427}}－{{ill|Myōhō Hakase|ja|明法博士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōhō Hakase|qq=}} * [[:en:Imperial examination|Imperial Examination]]
* [[Abe no Seimei]] * [[:en:Da Liu Ren|Da Liu Ren]] * [[Goryō]] * [[Itako]] * [[Kuji-in]] * [[Onmyōdō]] * [[:en:Sanpaku|Sanpaku]] * [[Seimei Shrine]] * [[Senji Ryakketsu]] * [[Shikigami]] * [[:en:Shinigami|Shinigami]] * [[Tengenjutsu (fortune telling)]] * [[Ushi no toki mairi]]

No complete manuscript survives; only partial copies are extant.<ref name="Mypedia">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=百科事典マイペディア|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> The original codes—*Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku*—comprised 10, 12, and 12 volumes respectively.<ref name="Britannica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> Existing manuscripts of *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* are said to contain 15<ref name="Britannica" /> or 17 volumes,<ref name="Obunsha">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=旺文社日本史事典 三訂版|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> with versions ranging from 12 to 20 volumes. == Sources == * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第12巻 (National Historical Series, Vol. 12) |year=1900 |publisher=経済雑誌社 |chapter=類聚三代格 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991102/1/1 |doi=10.11501/991102}} * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第25巻 新訂増補 (National Historical Series, Vol. 25) |year=1936 |publisher=国史大系刊行会 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3431640/1/4 |doi=10.11501/3431640 |chapter=類聚三代格}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ruijū Kokushi|en|Ruijū Kokushi|WD=Q1038639|lt=Ruijū Kokushi}}

== See Also from {{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|ja|格式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyaku and Shiki|qq=}} == {{Wiktionary}} * {{ill|Traditional Chinese law|en|Traditional Chinese law|WD=Q4380197|lt=Ritsuryō}}－{{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Ritsuryō law|ja|律令法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō law|qq=}}－{{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system}} * {{ill|Three Great Legal Codes|ja|三代格式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Legal Codes|1=Sandai Kyakushiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354355}} ''' {{ill|Kōnin Kyakushiki|ja|弘仁格式|lt=Kōnin Codes (Kōnin Kyaku, Kōnin Shiki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnin Kyakushiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三代格式|1=Sandai Kyakushiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354355}} ''' {{ill|Jōgan Kyakushiki|ja|貞観格式|lt=Jōgan Codes (Jōgan Kyaku, Jōgan Shiki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan Kyakushiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三代格式|1=Sandai Kyakushiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354355}} ''' {{ill|Engi Kyakushiki|ja|延喜格式|lt=Engi Codes (Engi Kyaku, Engi Shiki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Kyakushiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三代格式|1=Sandai Kyakushiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354355}} * {{ill|Kōtai-shiki|ja|交替式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai-shiki|1=kōtaishiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11373940}} * {{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}} * {{ill|Hachiman Usa-gū Gotakusenshū|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Usa-gū Gotakusenshū|qq=}} * {{ill|Myōhō-dō|ja|明法道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōhō-dō|1=Myōbōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512427}}－{{ill|Myōhō Hakase|ja|明法博士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōhō Hakase|qq=}} * {{ill|Imperial examination|en|Imperial examination|WD=Q7408|lt=Imperial Examination}}

== Structure of the 12-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Shinto shrine rituals, divine oracles, shrine priests, purification * Volume 2 - Buddha images, Buddhist ceremonies, appointment of clergy * Volume 3 - Provincial temples, clerical Ranks, monastic prohibitions, regional lecturers * Volume 4 - Central government office reorganizations and staffing * Volume 5 - Provincial bureaucracy and Ranks * Volume 6 - Bureaucratic salary systems * Volume 7 - Opinions of court nobles, oversight of local officials * Volume 8 - Agricultural promotion, taxation, monetary lending * Volume 9 - Land allotments, state-managed fields, land reclamation, mountains, rivers, and wetlands * Volume 10 - Imperial household cuisine, amnesties, documents, seals * Volume 11 - Military affairs, conscription, sumo, postal stations, timber * Volume 12 - Various prohibitions and punishments
== Structure of the 20-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Preface, shrine affairs, divine properties and land taxes, rituals and offerings, divine Rankings, oracles, Saiō matters, purification matters * Volume 2 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 1): Creation of Buddha images, sutra copying, Buddhist ceremonies, tantric initiations, state-sponsored monastics * Volume 3 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 2): Provincial temples, established temples, monastic Ranks, provincial lecturers, monastic prohibitions * Volume 4 - Government office reforms, staff adjustments, miscellaneous appointments * Volume 5 - Provincial governance, staffing adjustments, official Ranks, administrative transitions * Volume 6 - Salaries, stipends, official attire, public resources, labor duties, aid distribution * Volume 7 - Court noble opinions, livestock management, district officials * Volume 8 - Agriculture and sericulture, taxation, household registries, grain storage, economic regulations * Volume 9 - (Content missing) * Volume 10 - Imperial affairs, national mourning, court cuisine * Volume 11 - (Content missing) * Volume 12 - Official missions, legal documents, fugitives, state warehouses * Volume 13 - (Content missing) * Volume 14 - Loans, financial transactions, miscellaneous grains, relief granaries, land management, coin minting * Volume 15 - Land distribution, tax land, state-owned fields, cultivated land, temple lands, official lands * Volume 16 - Abandoned lands, roads, ports, wilderness management, riverbanks, irrigation * Volume 17 - Name changes, amnesties, pardons, reward systems, document management * Volume 18 - Military affairs, conscription, defense units, weapons, border controls, foreign relations, sumo, livestock, postal systems, timber management * Volume 19 - Prohibitions and restrictions * Volume 20 - Criminal judgments and fines

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Ruiju Sandai Kyaku]][[ja:類聚三代格]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Ruiju Sandai Kyaku]][[ja:類聚三代格]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ruiju Sandai Kyaku]][[ja:類聚三代格]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11665185]] Wikidata name: Ruiju Sandaikyaku [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ruijū Sandai Kyaku''' (類聚三代格) is a compilation of legal codes from the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}. The compiler is unknown.<ref name="Nipponica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref>
== Overview == The *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* organizes the legal codes of the {{ill|Kōnin Kyaku|ja|弘仁格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnin Kyaku|1=Kōnin Kyaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487196}}, {{ill|Jōgan Kyaku|ja|貞観格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan Kyaku|1=Jōgan Kyaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634549}}, and {{ill|Engi Kyaku|ja|延喜格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Kyaku|1=Engi Kyaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486506}} thematically into categories such as "Shinto Shrine Affairs," "Provincial Temple Affairs," and "Tax Matters."<ref name="Nipponica" /><ref name="Sekaidaihyakka">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第2版|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> It does not re-record items already included in previous compilations.<ref name="Britannica" /> The compilation date is estimated to be between {{ill|Chōhō (era)|en|Chōhō (era)|WD=Q1324844|lt=Chōhō 4}} (1002) and {{ill|Kanji (era)|en|Kanji (era)|WD=Q1193874|lt=Kanjī 3}} (1089) according to the *Nipponica* encyclopedia.<ref name="Nipponica" />
The three-generation codes were originally organized by government office, which was inconvenient for legal reference, especially during the mid-Heian period when there was a growing trend towards the study of {{ill|Yūsoku kojitsu|ja|有職故実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūsoku kojitsu|1=Yūsoku Kojitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1623327}} (ancient court practices). Thus, the *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* was compiled as part of legal codification efforts.<ref name="Nipponica" />
No complete manuscript survives; only partial copies are extant.<ref name="Mypedia">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=百科事典マイペディア|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> The original codes—*Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku*—comprised 10, 12, and 12 volumes respectively.<ref name="Britannica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> Existing manuscripts of *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* are said to contain 15<ref name="Britannica" /> or 17 volumes,<ref name="Obunsha">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=旺文社日本史事典 三訂版|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> with versions ranging from 12 to 20 volumes. The *Honchō Shojaku Mokuroku* lists it as having 30 volumes,<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" /> though scholars debate whether the full compilation indeed contained 20<ref name="Obunsha" /> or 30 volumes,<ref name="Mypedia" /> with *Britannica* casting doubt on the 30-volume theory.<ref name="Britannica" /> Even combining the surviving 12- and 20-volume manuscripts, gaps remain.<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" />
Since the original texts of the *Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku* are mostly lost (except for the *Kōnin Kyaku* index), *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku*—which preserves their contents—is an invaluable resource for understanding ancient Japanese legal systems.<ref name="Britannica" />
== Structure of the 12-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Shinto shrine rituals, divine oracles, shrine priests, purification * Volume 2 - Buddha images, Buddhist ceremonies, appointment of clergy * Volume 3 - Provincial temples, clerical Ranks, monastic prohibitions, regional lecturers * Volume 4 - Central government office reorganizations and staffing * Volume 5 - Provincial bureaucracy and Ranks * Volume 6 - Bureaucratic salary systems * Volume 7 - Opinions of court nobles, oversight of local officials * Volume 8 - Agricultural promotion, taxation, monetary lending * Volume 9 - Land allotments, state-managed fields, land reclamation, mountains, rivers, and wetlands * Volume 10 - Imperial household cuisine, amnesties, documents, seals * Volume 11 - Military affairs, conscription, sumo, postal stations, timber * Volume 12 - Various prohibitions and punishments
== Structure of the 20-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Preface, shrine affairs, divine properties and land taxes, rituals and offerings, divine Rankings, oracles, Saiō matters, purification matters * Volume 2 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 1): Creation of Buddha images, sutra copying, Buddhist ceremonies, tantric initiations, state-sponsored monastics * Volume 3 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 2): Provincial temples, established temples, monastic Ranks, provincial lecturers, monastic prohibitions * Volume 4 - Government office reforms, staff adjustments, miscellaneous appointments * Volume 5 - Provincial governance, staffing adjustments, official Ranks, administrative transitions * Volume 6 - Salaries, stipends, official attire, public resources, labor duties, aid distribution * Volume 7 - Court noble opinions, livestock management, district officials * Volume 8 - Agriculture and sericulture, taxation, household registries, grain storage, economic regulations * Volume 9 - (Content missing) * Volume 10 - Imperial affairs, national mourning, court cuisine * Volume 11 - (Content missing) * Volume 12 - Official missions, legal documents, fugitives, state warehouses * Volume 13 - (Content missing) * Volume 14 - Loans, financial transactions, miscellaneous grains, relief granaries, land management, coin minting * Volume 15 - Land distribution, tax land, state-owned fields, cultivated land, temple lands, official lands * Volume 16 - Abandoned lands, roads, ports, wilderness management, riverbanks, irrigation * Volume 17 - Name changes, amnesties, pardons, reward systems, document management * Volume 18 - Military affairs, conscription, defense units, weapons, border controls, foreign relations, sumo, livestock, postal systems, timber management * Volume 19 - Prohibitions and restrictions * Volume 20 - Criminal judgments and fines

== See Also from {{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|ja|格式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyaku and Shiki|qq=}} == {{Wiktionary}} * {{ill|Traditional Chinese law|en|Traditional Chinese law|WD=Q4380197|lt=Ritsuryō}}－{{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Ritsuryō law|ja|律令法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō law|qq=}}－{{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system}} * {{ill|Three Great Legal Codes|ja|三代格式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Legal Codes|1=Sandai Kyakushiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354355}} ''' {{ill|Kōnin Kyakushiki|ja|弘仁格式|lt=Kōnin Codes (Kōnin Kyaku, Kōnin Shiki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnin Kyakushiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三代格式|1=Sandai Kyakushiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354355}} ''' {{ill|Jōgan Kyakushiki|ja|貞観格式|lt=Jōgan Codes (Jōgan Kyaku, Jōgan Shiki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan Kyakushiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三代格式|1=Sandai Kyakushiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354355}} ''' {{ill|Engi Kyakushiki|ja|延喜格式|lt=Engi Codes (Engi Kyaku, Engi Shiki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Kyakushiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三代格式|1=Sandai Kyakushiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354355}} * {{ill|Kōtai-shiki|ja|交替式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai-shiki|1=kōtaishiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11373940}} * {{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}} * {{ill|Hachiman Usa-gū Gotakusenshū|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Usa-gū Gotakusenshū|qq=}} * {{ill|Myōhō-dō|ja|明法道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōhō-dō|1=Myōbōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512427}}－{{ill|Myōhō Hakase|ja|明法博士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōhō Hakase|qq=}} * {{ill|Imperial examination|en|Imperial examination|WD=Q7408|lt=Imperial Examination}}
* [[Abe no Seimei]] * [[:en:Da Liu Ren|Da Liu Ren]] * [[Goryō]] * [[Itako]] * [[Kuji-in]] * [[Onmyōdō]] * {{ill|Sanpaku|en|Sanpaku|WD=Q1815087|lt=Sanpaku}} * [[Seimei Shrine]] * [[Senji Ryakketsu]] * [[Shikigami]] * {{ill|Shinigami|en|Shinigami|WD=Q479033|lt=Shinigami}} * [[Tengenjutsu (fortune telling)]] * [[Ushi no toki mairi]]

The *Honchō Shojaku Mokuroku* lists it as having 30 volumes,<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" /> though scholars debate whether the full compilation indeed contained 20<ref name="Obunsha" /> or 30 volumes,<ref name="Mypedia" /> with *Britannica* casting doubt on the 30-volume theory.<ref name="Britannica" /> Even combining the surviving 12- and 20-volume manuscripts, gaps remain.<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" />

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Ruiju Sandai Kyaku]][[ja:類聚三代格]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Ruiju Sandai Kyaku]][[ja:類聚三代格]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ruiju Sandai Kyaku]][[ja:類聚三代格]]
'''Ruijū Sandai Kyaku''' (類聚三代格) is a compilation of legal codes from the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}. The compiler is unknown.<ref name="Nipponica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref>
== Overview == The *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* organizes the legal codes of the {{ill|Kōnin Kyaku|ja|弘仁格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnin Kyaku|1=Kōnin Kyaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487196}}, {{ill|Jōgan Kyaku|ja|貞観格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan Kyaku|1=Jōgan Kyaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634549}}, and {{ill|Engi Kyaku|ja|延喜格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Kyaku|1=Engi Kyaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486506}} thematically into categories such as "Shinto Shrine Affairs," "Provincial Temple Affairs," and "Tax Matters."<ref name="Nipponica" /><ref name="Sekaidaihyakka">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第2版|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> It does not re-record items already included in previous compilations.<ref name="Britannica" /> The compilation date is estimated to be between {{ill|Chōhō (era)|en|Chōhō (era)|WD=Q1324844|lt=Chōhō 4}} (1002) and {{ill|Kanji (era)|en|Kanji (era)|WD=Q1193874|lt=Kanjī 3}} (1089) according to the *Nipponica* encyclopedia.<ref name="Nipponica" />
The three-generation codes were originally organized by government office, which was inconvenient for legal reference, especially during the mid-Heian period when there was a growing trend towards the study of {{ill|Yūsoku kojitsu|ja|有職故実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūsoku kojitsu|1=Yūsoku Kojitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1623327}} (ancient court practices). Thus, the *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* was compiled as part of legal codification efforts.<ref name="Nipponica" />
No complete manuscript survives; only partial copies are extant.<ref name="Mypedia">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=百科事典マイペディア|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> The original codes—*Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku*—comprised 10, 12, and 12 volumes respectively.<ref name="Britannica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> Existing manuscripts of *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* are said to contain 15<ref name="Britannica" /> or 17 volumes,<ref name="Obunsha">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=旺文社日本史事典 三訂版|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> with versions ranging from 12 to 20 volumes. The *Honchō Shojaku Mokuroku* lists it as having 30 volumes,<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" /> though scholars debate whether the full compilation indeed contained 20<ref name="Obunsha" /> or 30 volumes,<ref name="Mypedia" /> with *Britannica* casting doubt on the 30-volume theory.<ref name="Britannica" /> Even combining the surviving 12- and 20-volume manuscripts, gaps remain.<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" />
Since the original texts of the *Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku* are mostly lost (except for the *Kōnin Kyaku* index), *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku*—which preserves their contents—is an invaluable resource for understanding ancient Japanese legal systems.<ref name="Britannica" />
== Structure of the 12-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Shinto shrine rituals, divine oracles, shrine priests, purification * Volume 2 - Buddha images, Buddhist ceremonies, appointment of clergy * Volume 3 - Provincial temples, clerical Ranks, monastic prohibitions, regional lecturers * Volume 4 - Central government office reorganizations and staffing * Volume 5 - Provincial bureaucracy and Ranks * Volume 6 - Bureaucratic salary systems * Volume 7 - Opinions of court nobles, oversight of local officials * Volume 8 - Agricultural promotion, taxation, monetary lending * Volume 9 - Land allotments, state-managed fields, land reclamation, mountains, rivers, and wetlands * Volume 10 - Imperial household cuisine, amnesties, documents, seals * Volume 11 - Military affairs, conscription, sumo, postal stations, timber * Volume 12 - Various prohibitions and punishments
== Structure of the 20-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Preface, shrine affairs, divine properties and land taxes, rituals and offerings, divine Rankings, oracles, Saiō matters, purification matters * Volume 2 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 1): Creation of Buddha images, sutra copying, Buddhist ceremonies, tantric initiations, state-sponsored monastics * Volume 3 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 2): Provincial temples, established temples, monastic Ranks, provincial lecturers, monastic prohibitions * Volume 4 - Government office reforms, staff adjustments, miscellaneous appointments * Volume 5 - Provincial governance, staffing adjustments, official Ranks, administrative transitions * Volume 6 - Salaries, stipends, official attire, public resources, labor duties, aid distribution * Volume 7 - Court noble opinions, livestock management, district officials * Volume 8 - Agriculture and sericulture, taxation, household registries, grain storage, economic regulations * Volume 9 - (Content missing) * Volume 10 - Imperial affairs, national mourning, court cuisine * Volume 11 - (Content missing) * Volume 12 - Official missions, legal documents, fugitives, state warehouses * Volume 13 - (Content missing) * Volume 14 - Loans, financial transactions, miscellaneous grains, relief granaries, land management, coin minting * Volume 15 - Land distribution, tax land, state-owned fields, cultivated land, temple lands, official lands * Volume 16 - Abandoned lands, roads, ports, wilderness management, riverbanks, irrigation * Volume 17 - Name changes, amnesties, pardons, reward systems, document management * Volume 18 - Military affairs, conscription, defense units, weapons, border controls, foreign relations, sumo, livestock, postal systems, timber management * Volume 19 - Prohibitions and restrictions * Volume 20 - Criminal judgments and fines
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Sources == * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第12巻 (National Historical Series, Vol. 12) |year=1900 |publisher=経済雑誌社 |chapter=類聚三代格 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991102/1/1 |doi=10.11501/991102}} * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第25巻 新訂増補 (National Historical Series, Vol. 25) |year=1936 |publisher=国史大系刊行会 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3431640/1/4 |doi=10.11501/3431640 |chapter=類聚三代格}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ruijū Kokushi|en|Ruijū Kokushi|WD=Q1038639|lt=Ruijū Kokushi}}
== External Links == * [http://codh.rois.ac.jp/pmjt/book/200018252/ *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* - Japanese Classical Text Database]
== See Also from {{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|ja|格式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyaku and Shiki|qq=}} == {{Wiktionary}} * {{ill|Traditional Chinese law|en|Traditional Chinese law|WD=Q4380197|lt=Ritsuryō}}－{{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Ritsuryō law|ja|律令法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō law|qq=}}－{{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system}} * {{ill|Three Great Legal Codes|ja|三代格式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Legal Codes|1=Sandai Kyakushiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354355}} ''' {{ill|Kōnin Kyakushiki|ja|弘仁格式|lt=Kōnin Codes (Kōnin Kyaku, Kōnin Shiki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnin Kyakushiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三代格式|1=Sandai Kyakushiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354355}} ''' {{ill|Jōgan Kyakushiki|ja|貞観格式|lt=Jōgan Codes (Jōgan Kyaku, Jōgan Shiki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan Kyakushiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三代格式|1=Sandai Kyakushiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354355}} ''' {{ill|Engi Kyakushiki|ja|延喜格式|lt=Engi Codes (Engi Kyaku, Engi Shiki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Kyakushiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三代格式|1=Sandai Kyakushiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354355}} * {{ill|Kōtai-shiki|ja|交替式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai-shiki|1=kōtaishiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11373940}} * {{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}} * {{ill|Hachiman Usa-gū Gotakusenshū|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Usa-gū Gotakusenshū|qq=}} * {{ill|Myōhō-dō|ja|明法道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōhō-dō|1=Myōbōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512427}}－{{ill|Myōhō Hakase|ja|明法博士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōhō Hakase|qq=}} * {{ill|Imperial examination|en|Imperial examination|WD=Q7408|lt=Imperial Examination}}
* [[Abe no Seimei]] * [[:en:Da Liu Ren|Da Liu Ren]] * [[Goryō]] * [[Itako]] * [[Kuji-in]] * [[Onmyōdō]] * {{ill|Sanpaku|en|Sanpaku|WD=Q1815087|lt=Sanpaku}} * [[Seimei Shrine]] * [[Senji Ryakketsu]] * [[Shikigami]] * {{ill|Shinigami|en|Shinigami|WD=Q479033|lt=Shinigami}} * [[Tengenjutsu (fortune telling)]] * [[Ushi no toki mairi]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Ruiju Sandai Kyaku]][[ja:類聚三代格]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Ruiju Sandai Kyaku]][[ja:類聚三代格]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Ruiju Sandai Kyaku]][[ja:類聚三代格]]
'''Ruijū Sandai Kyaku''' (類聚三代格) is a compilation of legal codes from the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. The compiler is unknown.<ref name="Nipponica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref>
== Overview == The *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* organizes the legal codes of the {{ill|Kōnin Kyaku|ja|弘仁格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnin Kyaku|1=Q11487196|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487196}}, {{ill|Jōgan Kyaku|ja|貞観格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan Kyaku|1=Q11634549|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634549}}, and {{ill|Engi Kyaku|ja|延喜格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Kyaku|1=Q11486506|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486506}} thematically into categories such as "Shinto Shrine Affairs," "Provincial Temple Affairs," and "Tax Matters."<ref name="Nipponica" /><ref name="Sekaidaihyakka">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第2版|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> It does not re-record items already included in previous compilations.<ref name="Britannica" /> The compilation date is estimated to be between {{ill|Chōhō (era)|en|Chōhō (era)|WD=Q1324844|lt=Chōhō 4|1=Chōhō (era)}} (1002) and {{ill|Kanji (era)|en|Kanji (era)|WD=Q1193874|lt=Kanjī 3|1=Kanji (era)}} (1089) according to the *Nipponica* encyclopedia.<ref name="Nipponica" />
The three-generation codes were originally organized by government office, which was inconvenient for legal reference, especially during the mid-Heian period when there was a growing trend towards the study of {{ill|Yūsoku kojitsu|ja|有職故実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūsoku kojitsu|1=Yūsoku Kojitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1623327}} (ancient court practices). Thus, the *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* was compiled as part of legal codification efforts.<ref name="Nipponica" />
No complete manuscript survives; only partial copies are extant.<ref name="Mypedia">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=百科事典マイペディア|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> The original codes—*Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku*—comprised 10, 12, and 12 volumes respectively.<ref name="Britannica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> Existing manuscripts of *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* are said to contain 15<ref name="Britannica" /> or 17 volumes,<ref name="Obunsha">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=旺文社日本史事典 三訂版|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> with versions ranging from 12 to 20 volumes. The *Honchō Shojaku Mokuroku* lists it as having 30 volumes,<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" /> though scholars debate whether the full compilation indeed contained 20<ref name="Obunsha" /> or 30 volumes,<ref name="Mypedia" /> with *Britannica* casting doubt on the 30-volume theory.<ref name="Britannica" /> Even combining the surviving 12- and 20-volume manuscripts, gaps remain.<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" />
Since the original texts of the *Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku* are mostly lost (except for the *Kōnin Kyaku* index), *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku*—which preserves their contents—is an invaluable resource for understanding ancient Japanese legal systems.<ref name="Britannica" />
== Structure of the 12-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Shinto shrine rituals, divine oracles, shrine priests, purification * Volume 2 - Buddha images, Buddhist ceremonies, appointment of clergy * Volume 3 - Provincial temples, clerical Ranks, monastic prohibitions, regional lecturers * Volume 4 - Central government office reorganizations and staffing * Volume 5 - Provincial bureaucracy and Ranks * Volume 6 - Bureaucratic salary systems * Volume 7 - Opinions of court nobles, oversight of local officials * Volume 8 - Agricultural promotion, taxation, monetary lending * Volume 9 - Land allotments, state-managed fields, land reclamation, mountains, rivers, and wetlands * Volume 10 - Imperial household cuisine, amnesties, documents, seals * Volume 11 - Military affairs, conscription, sumo, postal stations, timber * Volume 12 - Various prohibitions and punishments
== Structure of the 20-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Preface, shrine affairs, divine properties and land taxes, rituals and offerings, divine Rankings, oracles, Saiō matters, purification matters * Volume 2 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 1): Creation of Buddha images, sutra copying, Buddhist ceremonies, tantric initiations, state-sponsored monastics * Volume 3 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 2): Provincial temples, established temples, monastic Ranks, provincial lecturers, monastic prohibitions * Volume 4 - Government office reforms, staff adjustments, miscellaneous appointments * Volume 5 - Provincial governance, staffing adjustments, official Ranks, administrative transitions * Volume 6 - Salaries, stipends, official attire, public resources, labor duties, aid distribution * Volume 7 - Court noble opinions, livestock management, district officials * Volume 8 - Agriculture and sericulture, taxation, household registries, grain storage, economic regulations * Volume 9 - (Content missing) * Volume 10 - Imperial affairs, national mourning, court cuisine * Volume 11 - (Content missing) * Volume 12 - Official missions, legal documents, fugitives, state warehouses * Volume 13 - (Content missing) * Volume 14 - Loans, financial transactions, miscellaneous grains, relief granaries, land management, coin minting * Volume 15 - Land distribution, tax land, state-owned fields, cultivated land, temple lands, official lands * Volume 16 - Abandoned lands, roads, ports, wilderness management, riverbanks, irrigation * Volume 17 - Name changes, amnesties, pardons, reward systems, document management * Volume 18 - Military affairs, conscription, defense units, weapons, border controls, foreign relations, sumo, livestock, postal systems, timber management * Volume 19 - Prohibitions and restrictions * Volume 20 - Criminal judgments and fines
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Sources == * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第12巻 (National Historical Series, Vol. 12) |year=1900 |publisher=経済雑誌社 |chapter=類聚三代格 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991102/1/1 |doi=10.11501/991102}} * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第25巻 新訂増補 (National Historical Series, Vol. 25) |year=1936 |publisher=国史大系刊行会 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3431640/1/4 |doi=10.11501/3431640 |chapter=類聚三代格}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ruijū Kokushi|en|Ruijū Kokushi|WD=Q1038639|lt=Ruijū Kokushi|1=Ruijū Kokushi}}
== External Links == * [http://codh.rois.ac.jp/pmjt/book/200018252/ *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* - Japanese Classical Text Database]
== See Also from {{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|ja|格式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyaku and Shiki|qq=}} == {{Wiktionary}} * {{ill|Traditional Chinese law|en|Traditional Chinese law|WD=Q4380197|lt=Ritsuryō|1=Traditional Chinese law}}－{{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Ritsuryō law|ja|律令法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō law|qq=}}－{{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}} * {{ill|Three Great Legal Codes|ja|三代格式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Legal Codes|1=Sandai Kyakushiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354355}} ''' {{ill|Kōnin Kyakushiki|ja|弘仁格式|lt=Kōnin Codes (Kōnin Kyaku, Kōnin Shiki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnin Kyakushiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三代格式|1=Sandai Kyakushiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354355}} ''' {{ill|Jōgan Kyakushiki|ja|貞観格式|lt=Jōgan Codes (Jōgan Kyaku, Jōgan Shiki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan Kyakushiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三代格式|1=Sandai Kyakushiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354355}} ''' {{ill|Engi Kyakushiki|ja|延喜格式|lt=Engi Codes (Engi Kyaku, Engi Shiki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Kyakushiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三代格式|1=Sandai Kyakushiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354355}} * {{ill|Kōtai-shiki|ja|交替式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai-shiki|1=kōtaishiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11373940}} * {{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}} * {{ill|Hachiman Usa-gū Gotakusenshū|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Usa-gū Gotakusenshū|qq=}} * {{ill|Myōhō-dō|ja|明法道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōhō-dō|1=Myōbōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512427}}－{{ill|Myōhō Hakase|ja|明法博士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōhō Hakase|qq=}} * {{ill|Imperial examination|en|Imperial examination|WD=Q7408|lt=Imperial Examination|1=Imperial examination}}
* [[Abe no Seimei]] * [[:en:Da Liu Ren|Da Liu Ren]] * [[Goryō]] * [[Itako]] * [[Kuji-in]] * [[Onmyōdō]] * {{ill|Sanpaku|en|Sanpaku|WD=Q1815087|lt=Sanpaku|1=Sanpaku}} * [[Seimei Shrine]] * [[Senji Ryakketsu]] * [[Shikigami]] * {{ill|Shinigami|en|Shinigami|WD=Q479033|lt=Shinigami|1=Shinigami}} * [[Tengenjutsu (fortune telling)]] * [[Ushi no toki mairi]]

'''Ruijū Sandai Kyaku''' (類聚三代格) is a compilation of legal codes from the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. The compiler is unknown.<ref name="Nipponica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref>

Thus, the *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* was compiled as part of legal codification efforts.<ref name="Nipponica" />
No complete manuscript survives; only partial copies are extant.<ref name="Mypedia">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=百科事典マイペディア|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> The original codes—*Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku*—comprised 10, 12, and 12 volumes respectively.<ref name="Britannica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> Existing manuscripts of *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* are said to contain 15<ref name="Britannica" /> or 17 volumes,<ref name="Obunsha">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=旺文社日本史事典 三訂版|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> with versions ranging from 12 to 20 volumes. The *Honchō Shojaku Mokuroku* lists it as having 30 volumes,<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" /> though scholars debate whether the full compilation indeed contained 20<ref name="Obunsha" /> or 30 volumes,<ref name="Mypedia" /> with *Britannica* casting doubt on the 30-volume theory.<ref name="Britannica" /> Even combining the surviving 12- and 20-volume manuscripts, gaps remain.<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" />

== References == {{Reflist}}
== Sources == * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第12巻 (National Historical Series, Vol. 12) |year=1900 |publisher=経済雑誌社 |chapter=類聚三代格 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991102/1/1 |doi=10.11501/991102}} * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第25巻 新訂増補 (National Historical Series, Vol. 25) |year=1936 |publisher=国史大系刊行会 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3431640/1/4 |doi=10.11501/3431640 |chapter=類聚三代格}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ruijū Kokushi|en|Ruijū Kokushi|WD=Q1038639|lt=Ruijū Kokushi|1=Ruijū Kokushi}}

== See Also from {{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|ja|格式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyaku and Shiki|qq=}} == {{Wiktionary}} * {{ill|Traditional Chinese law|en|Traditional Chinese law|WD=Q4380197|lt=Ritsuryō|1=Traditional Chinese law}}－{{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Ritsuryō law|ja|律令法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō law|qq=}}－{{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}} * {{ill|Three Great Legal Codes|ja|三代格式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Legal Codes|1=Sandai Kyakushiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354355}} ''' {{ill|Kōnin Kyakushiki|ja|弘仁格式|lt=Kōnin Codes (Kōnin Kyaku, Kōnin Shiki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnin Kyakushiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三代格式|1=Sandai Kyakushiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354355}} ''' {{ill|Jōgan Kyakushiki|ja|貞観格式|lt=Jōgan Codes (Jōgan Kyaku, Jōgan Shiki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan Kyakushiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三代格式|1=Sandai Kyakushiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354355}} ''' {{ill|Engi Kyakushiki|ja|延喜格式|lt=Engi Codes (Engi Kyaku, Engi Shiki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Kyakushiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三代格式|1=Sandai Kyakushiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354355}} * {{ill|Kōtai-shiki|ja|交替式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai-shiki|1=kōtaishiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11373940}} * {{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}} * {{ill|Hachiman Usa-gū Gotakusenshū|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Usa-gū Gotakusenshū|qq=}} * {{ill|Myōhō-dō|ja|明法道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōhō-dō|1=Myōbōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512427}}－{{ill|Myōhō Hakase|ja|明法博士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōhō Hakase|qq=}} * {{ill|Imperial examination|en|Imperial examination|WD=Q7408|lt=Imperial Examination|1=Imperial examination}}

The *Honchō Shojaku Mokuroku* lists it as having 30 volumes,<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" /> though scholars debate whether the full compilation indeed contained 20<ref name="Obunsha" /> or 30 volumes,<ref name="Mypedia" /> with *Britannica* casting doubt on the 30-volume theory.<ref name="Britannica" /> Even combining the surviving 12- and 20-volume manuscripts, gaps remain.<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" />
Since the original texts of the *Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku* are mostly lost (except for the *Kōnin Kyaku* index), *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku*—which preserves their contents—is an invaluable resource for understanding ancient Japanese legal systems.<ref name="Britannica" />
== Structure of the 12-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Shinto shrine rituals, divine oracles, shrine priests, purification * Volume 2 - Buddha images, Buddhist ceremonies, appointment of clergy * Volume 3 - Provincial temples, clerical Ranks, monastic prohibitions, regional lecturers * Volume 4 - Central government office reorganizations and staffing * Volume 5 - Provincial bureaucracy and Ranks * Volume 6 - Bureaucratic salary systems * Volume 7 - Opinions of court nobles, oversight of local officials * Volume 8 - Agricultural promotion, taxation, monetary lending * Volume 9 - Land allotments, state-managed fields, land reclamation, mountains, rivers, and wetlands * Volume 10 - Imperial household cuisine, amnesties, documents, seals * Volume 11 - Military affairs, conscription, sumo, postal stations, timber * Volume 12 - Various prohibitions and punishments
== Structure of the 20-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Preface, shrine affairs, divine properties and land taxes, rituals and offerings, divine Rankings, oracles, Saiō matters, purification matters * Volume 2 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 1): Creation of Buddha images, sutra copying, Buddhist ceremonies, tantric initiations, state-sponsored monastics * Volume 3 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 2): Provincial temples, established temples, monastic Ranks, provincial lecturers, monastic prohibitions * Volume 4 - Government office reforms, staff adjustments, miscellaneous appointments * Volume 5 - Provincial governance, staffing adjustments, official Ranks, administrative transitions * Volume 6 - Salaries, stipends, official attire, public resources, labor duties, aid distribution * Volume 7 - Court noble opinions, livestock management, district officials * Volume 8 - Agriculture and sericulture, taxation, household registries, grain storage, economic regulations * Volume 9 - (Content missing) * Volume 10 - Imperial affairs, national mourning, court cuisine * Volume 11 - (Content missing) * Volume 12 - Official missions, legal documents, fugitives, state warehouses * Volume 13 - (Content missing) * Volume 14 - Loans, financial transactions, miscellaneous grains, relief granaries, land management, coin minting * Volume 15 - Land distribution, tax land, state-owned fields, cultivated land, temple lands, official lands * Volume 16 - Abandoned lands, roads, ports, wilderness management, riverbanks, irrigation * Volume 17 - Name changes, amnesties, pardons, reward systems, document management * Volume 18 - Military affairs, conscription, defense units, weapons, border controls, foreign relations, sumo, livestock, postal systems, timber management * Volume 19 - Prohibitions and restrictions * Volume 20 - Criminal judgments and fines
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Sources == * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第12巻 (National Historical Series, Vol. 12) |year=1900 |publisher=経済雑誌社 |chapter=類聚三代格 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991102/1/1 |doi=10.11501/991102}} * {{Cite book|ja |title=国史大系 第25巻 新訂増補 (National Historical Series, Vol. 25) |year=1936 |publisher=国史大系刊行会 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3431640/1/4 |doi=10.11501/3431640 |chapter=類聚三代格}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ruijū Kokushi|en|Ruijū Kokushi|WD=Q1038639|lt=Ruijū Kokushi|1=Ruijū Kokushi}}
== External Links == * [http://codh.rois.ac.jp/pmjt/book/200018252/ *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* - Japanese Classical Text Database]
== See Also from {{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|ja|格式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyaku and Shiki|qq=}} == {{Wiktionary}} * {{ill|Traditional Chinese law|en|Traditional Chinese law|WD=Q4380197|lt=Ritsuryō|1=Traditional Chinese law}}－{{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Ritsuryō law|ja|律令法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō law|qq=}}－{{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}} * {{ill|Three Great Legal Codes|ja|三代格式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Legal Codes|1=Sandai Kyakushiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354355}} ''' {{ill|Kōnin Kyakushiki|ja|弘仁格式|lt=Kōnin Codes (Kōnin Kyaku, Kōnin Shiki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnin Kyakushiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三代格式|1=Sandai Kyakushiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354355}} ''' {{ill|Jōgan Kyakushiki|ja|貞観格式|lt=Jōgan Codes (Jōgan Kyaku, Jōgan Shiki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan Kyakushiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三代格式|1=Sandai Kyakushiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354355}} ''' {{ill|Engi Kyakushiki|ja|延喜格式|lt=Engi Codes (Engi Kyaku, Engi Shiki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Kyakushiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三代格式|1=Sandai Kyakushiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354355}} * {{ill|Kōtai-shiki|ja|交替式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai-shiki|1=kōtaishiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11373940}} * {{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}} * {{ill|Hachiman Usa-gū Gotakusenshū|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Usa-gū Gotakusenshū|qq=}} * {{ill|Myōhō-dō|ja|明法道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōhō-dō|1=Myōbōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512427}}－{{ill|Myōhō Hakase|ja|明法博士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōhō Hakase|qq=}} * {{ill|Imperial examination|en|Imperial examination|WD=Q7408|lt=Imperial Examination|1=Imperial examination}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Ruiju Sandai Kyaku]][[ja:類聚三代格]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Ruiju Sandai Kyaku]][[ja:類聚三代格]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:類聚三代格]]
'''Ruijū Sandai Kyaku''' (類聚三代格) is a compilation of legal codes from the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. The compiler is unknown.<ref name="Nipponica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref>
== Overview == The *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* organizes the legal codes of the {{ill|Kōnin Kyaku|ja|弘仁格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnin Kyaku|1=Q11487196|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487196}}, {{ill|Jōgan Kyaku|ja|貞観格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan Kyaku|1=Q11634549|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634549}}, and {{ill|Engi Kyaku|ja|延喜格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Kyaku|1=Q11486506|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486506}} thematically into categories such as "Shinto Shrine Affairs," "Provincial Temple Affairs," and "Tax Matters."<ref name="Nipponica" /><ref name="Sekaidaihyakka">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第2版|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> It does not re-record items already included in previous compilations.<ref name="Britannica" /> The compilation date is estimated to be between {{ill|Chōhō (era)|en|Chōhō (era)|WD=Q1324844|lt=Chōhō 4|1=Chōhō (era)}} (1002) and {{ill|Kanji (era)|en|Kanji (era)|WD=Q1193874|lt=Kanjī 3|1=Kanji (era)}} (1089) according to the *Nipponica* encyclopedia.<ref name="Nipponica" />
The three-generation codes were originally organized by government office, which was inconvenient for legal reference, especially during the mid-Heian period when there was a growing trend towards the study of {{ill|Yūsoku kojitsu|ja|有職故実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūsoku kojitsu|1=Yūsoku Kojitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1623327}} (ancient court practices). Thus, the *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* was compiled as part of legal codification efforts.<ref name="Nipponica" />
No complete manuscript survives; only partial copies are extant.<ref name="Mypedia">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=百科事典マイペディア|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> The original codes—*Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku*—comprised 10, 12, and 12 volumes respectively.<ref name="Britannica">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> Existing manuscripts of *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku* are said to contain 15<ref name="Britannica" /> or 17 volumes,<ref name="Obunsha">{{Cite Kotobank|word=類聚三代格|encyclopedia=旺文社日本史事典 三訂版|access-date=2023rd year 4月17日}}</ref> with versions ranging from 12 to 20 volumes. The *Honchō Shojaku Mokuroku* lists it as having 30 volumes,<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" /> though scholars debate whether the full compilation indeed contained 20<ref name="Obunsha" /> or 30 volumes,<ref name="Mypedia" /> with *Britannica* casting doubt on the 30-volume theory.<ref name="Britannica" /> Even combining the surviving 12- and 20-volume manuscripts, gaps remain.<ref name="Sekaidaihyakka" />
Since the original texts of the *Kōnin Kyaku*, *Jōgan Kyaku*, and *Engi Kyaku* are mostly lost (except for the *Kōnin Kyaku* index), *Ruijū Sandai Kyaku*—which preserves their contents—is an invaluable resource for understanding ancient Japanese legal systems.<ref name="Britannica" />
== Structure of the 12-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Shinto shrine rituals, divine oracles, shrine priests, purification * Volume 2 - Buddha images, Buddhist ceremonies, appointment of clergy * Volume 3 - Provincial temples, clerical Ranks, monastic prohibitions, regional lecturers * Volume 4 - Central government office reorganizations and staffing * Volume 5 - Provincial bureaucracy and Ranks * Volume 6 - Bureaucratic salary systems * Volume 7 - Opinions of court nobles, oversight of local officials * Volume 8 - Agricultural promotion, taxation, monetary lending * Volume 9 - Land allotments, state-managed fields, land reclamation, mountains, rivers, and wetlands * Volume 10 - Imperial household cuisine, amnesties, documents, seals * Volume 11 - Military affairs, conscription, sumo, postal stations, timber * Volume 12 - Various prohibitions and punishments
== Structure of the 20-Volume Version == * Volume 1 - Preface, shrine affairs, divine properties and land taxes, rituals and offerings, divine Rankings, oracles, Saiō matters, purification matters * Volume 2 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 1): Creation of Buddha images, sutra copying, Buddhist ceremonies, tantric initiations, state-sponsored monastics * Volume 3 - Buddhist Affairs (Part 2): Provincial temples, established temples, monastic Ranks, provincial lecturers, monastic prohibitions * Volume 4 - Government office reforms, staff adjustments, miscellaneous appointments * Volume 5 - Provincial governance, staffing adjustments, official Ranks, administrative transitions * Volume 6 - Salaries, stipends, official attire, public resources, labor duties, aid distribution * Volume 7 - Court noble opinions, livestock management, district officials * Volume 8 - Agriculture and sericulture, taxation, household registries, grain storage, economic regulations * Volume 9 - (Content missing) * Volume 10 - Imperial affairs, national mourning, court cuisine * Volume 11 - (Content missing) * Volume 12 - Official missions, legal documents, fugitives, state warehouses * Volume 13 - (Content missing) * Volume 14 - Loans, financial transactions, miscellaneous grains, relief granaries, land management, coin minting * Volume 15 - Land distribution, tax land, state-owned fields, cultivated land, temple lands, official lands * Volume 16 - Abandoned lands, roads, ports, wilderness management, riverbanks, irrigation * Volume 17 - Name changes, amnesties, pardons, reward systems, document management * Volume 18 - Military affairs, conscription, defense units, weapons, border controls, foreign relations, sumo, livestock, postal systems, timber management * Volume 19 - Prohibitions and restrictions * Volume 20 - Criminal judgments and fines

== See Also from {{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|ja|格式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyaku and Shiki|qq=}} == {{Wiktionary}} * {{ill|Traditional Chinese law|en|Traditional Chinese law|WD=Q4380197|lt=Ritsuryō|1=Traditional Chinese law}}－{{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Ritsuryō law|ja|律令法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō law|qq=}}－{{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}} * {{ill|Three Great Legal Codes|ja|三代格式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Legal Codes|1=Sandai Kyakushiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354355}} ''' {{ill|Kōnin Kyakushiki|ja|弘仁格式|lt=Kōnin Codes (Kōnin Kyaku, Kōnin Shiki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnin Kyakushiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三代格式|1=Sandai Kyakushiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354355}} ''' {{ill|Jōgan Kyakushiki|ja|貞観格式|lt=Jōgan Codes (Jōgan Kyaku, Jōgan Shiki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan Kyakushiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三代格式|1=Sandai Kyakushiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354355}} ''' {{ill|Engi Kyakushiki|ja|延喜格式|lt=Engi Codes (Engi Kyaku, Engi Shiki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Kyakushiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三代格式|1=Sandai Kyakushiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354355}} * {{ill|Kōtai-shiki|ja|交替式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai-shiki|1=kōtaishiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11373940}} * {{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}} * {{ill|Hachiman Usa-gū Gotakusenshū|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Usa-gū Gotakusenshū|qq=}} * {{ill|Myōhō-dō|ja|明法道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōhō-dō|1=Myōbōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512427}}－{{ill|Myōhō Hakase|ja|明法博士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōhō Hakase|qq=}} * {{ill|Imperial examination|en|Imperial examination|WD=Q7408|lt=Imperial Examination|1=Imperial examination}}
